definition.en: Thoughts have their own kind of structure, as cells do, and they seek their own fulfillment.
definition.zh: 思想拥有它们自己的结构，就像细胞一样，并且它们寻求自身的实现。

definition.en: Your thoughts are also as natural as your cells. Your thoughts propel you toward survival and growth also, and in the same way that your cells do.
definition.zh: 你的思想也像你的细胞一样自然。你的思想也推动你走向生存和成长，并且以与你的细胞相同的方式。

definition.en: Your thoughts are as real as snowflakes or raindrops or clouds. They mix and merge with the thoughts of others, to form man's livingscape, providing the vast mental elements from which physical events will be formed.
definition.zh: 你的思想像雪花、雨滴或云朵一样真实。它们与他人的思想混合交融，形成人类的生活景观，提供构成物理事件的庞大精神元素。

definition.en: In one respect, and in quite a legitimate and objective manner, all thoughts are also projections. They leave you in an objective actual manner, and exert an objective effect.
definition.zh: 在一个方面，并且以一种相当合法且客观的方式，所有思想也是投射。它们以一种客观实际的方式离开你，并施加客观的影响。

definition.en: Thoughts have what we will for now term electromagnetic properties. In those terms your thoughts mix and match with others in Framework 2, creating mass patterns that form the overall psychological basis behind world events.
definition.zh: 思想具有我们暂且称之为电磁特性的性质。就这些而言，你的思想在架构二中与他人的思想混合搭配，创造出形成世界事件背后整体心理基础的大众模式。

explanation:
思想具有类似细胞的结构，它们寻求自身的实现，并会吸引同类思想。每个人的思想都是内在理想化的表达，同时也在表达中不断修改和创造这些内在模式。你身体的每个细胞都会因每个思想而改变，这些细胞反应又会改变你的环境，大脑随之响应——这是一个持续的互动过程。

思想与细胞一样自然，同样推动你生存和成长。思想匮乏会带来物质匮乏，思想丰盛则带来丰盛。当思想离开意识时并不会消失，它们作为电性实相，不受物理定律束缚，可以同时存在于你的过去和现在。每个思想都是实际存在的，它影响每个行动，塑造你的物理环境。

你通过物理感官感知的，正是你已创造出来的现实。思想与情绪本身就是实际存在，直接作用于身体机制。

quotes.text: Thoughts have their own kind of structure, as cells do, and they seek their own fulfillment. They move toward like thoughts, and you have as a species an inner mass body of thought. Privately your thoughts are expressions of your idealizations; and while expressing those inner patterns they also modify and creatively change them. Each cell in your body is to some extent altered with each thought that you think. Each reaction of the cells alters your environment. The brain then responds to the alteration. There is a constant give-and-take. As the cells respond at certain levels to ever-changing streams of probabilities, so do your thoughts. Your body responds as you think it should, however, and so your conscious beliefs about reality have much to do with those probable experiences that you accept as a part of your intimate living.
quotes.translation: 思想拥有自己特有的结构，如同细胞一般，它们寻求自身的圆满。它们会趋向同类思想，而作为物种，你们拥有一个内在的思想集体。就个人而言，你的思想是你理想化的表达；在表达这些内在模式的同时，它们也修改并创造性地改变这些模式。你体内的每个细胞都会因你思考的每个念头而在某种程度上发生改变。细胞的每个反应都会改变你的环境。大脑随后会对这种改变做出回应。这里存在着一种持续的互动。正如细胞在特定层面上对不断变化的可能性之流做出反应，你的思想也是如此。然而，你的身体会按照你所认为的方式做出反应，因此你对实相的有意识信念，与那些你接纳为亲密生活一部分的可能经验密切相关。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 697
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Because of your attitudes, ideas do not seem as real to you as objects, or as practical. Thoughts are not given the same validity as rocks or trees or beer cans (two of which sat on the coffee table between us at the moment) or automobiles. In your terms an automobile gets you somewhere. You do not understand the great mobility of thought, nor grasp its practical nature. You make your world, and in an important manner your thoughts are indeed the immediate personal blueprints for it. When you manipulate objects you feel efficient. The manipulation of thoughts is far more practical. Here is a brief example.
quotes.translation: 由于你的态度，观念对你而言似乎不如物体那样真实或实用。思想没有被赋予与岩石、树木、啤酒罐（此刻我们之间的咖啡桌上就放着两个）或汽车同等的真实性。用你的话说，汽车能把你带到某个地方。你不理解思想的巨大流动性，也不领会其实用本质。你创造了自己的世界，而在一个重要意义上，你的思想确实是这个世界直接的个人蓝图。当你操控物体时，你感到有效率。而操控思想则要实用得多。这里有一个简短的例子。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 703
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Your thoughts are also as natural as your cells. Your thoughts propel you toward survival and growth also, and in the same way that your cells do. If you find yourself in physical difficulties healthwise, you cannot say: "Why doesn't my body stop me and assert its own wisdom?" because in the truest sense there is no division between your thoughts and your body. You are given the ability to think, as you are given the ability to move. Your thoughts multiply even as your cells do. Your thinking is meant to ensure your survival in those terms, as much as your body mechanism.
quotes.translation: 你的思想也像你的细胞一样自然。你的思想同样促使你走向生存与成长，方式与你的细胞相同。如果你在健康方面遇到身体上的困难，你不能说："为什么我的身体不阻止我，并坚持它自身的智慧？"因为从最真实的意义上说，你的思想与你的身体之间没有分裂。你被赋予了思考的能力，就像你被赋予了行动的能力一样。你的思想甚至像你的细胞一样繁衍。你的思考旨在确保你在那些方面的生存，正如你的身体机制所做的那样。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Thoughts of plenty will bring you plenty in material and financial terms, and in other terms. Thoughts of want will produce want. I have not made these rules. When you think in terms of want, and then see physical materiaizationls of want, you are tempted to think that your fears were justified, and therefore those thoughts are reinforced. But the thoughts caused the want, and I am simply trying to help you stop that cycle. Now, if you and Ruburt could agree on the sort of home you would like, and if you dreamed of it and thought about it specifically, you would have it.
quotes.translation: 丰盛的思想会给你带来物质和财务上的丰盛，以及其他方面的丰盛。匮乏的思想会产生匮乏。这些规则并非我所制定。当你以匮乏的方式思考，然后看到匮乏的物质显化时，你很容易认为你的恐惧是有道理的，因此那些思想得到了强化。但正是思想导致了匮乏，而我只是想帮助你停止这个循环。现在，如果你和鲁柏能就你们想要的房子达成一致，并且你们具体地梦想它、思考它，你们就会拥有它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: What happens to a thought when it leaves your conscious mind? It does not simply disappear. You can learn to follow it, but you are usually frightened of turning your attention away from its intense focus in three-dimensional existence. Therefore, it seems that the thought disappears. It seems also that your subjectivity has a mysterious unknown quality about it, and that even your mental life has a sort of insidious dropping-off point, a subjective cliff over which thoughts and memories fall, to disappear into nothingness. Therefore to protect yourself, to protect your subjectivity from drifting, you erect various psychological barriers at what you suppose to be the danger points. Instead, you see, you can follow these thoughts and emotions simply by realizing that your own reality continues in another direction, beside the one with which you mainly identify. For these thoughts and emotions that have left your conscious mind will lead you into other environments.
quotes.translation: 当一个思想离开你的意识心智时，它会发生什么？它不会简单地消失。你可以学会跟随它，但你通常害怕将注意力从它在三维存在中的强烈焦点上移开。因此，思想似乎消失了。你的主观性似乎也具有一种神秘未知的特质，甚至你的精神生活也有某种阴险的断点，一个主观的悬崖，思想和记忆从那里坠落，消失于虚无之中。因此，为了保护自己，保护你的主观性免于漂移，你在你认为是危险点的地方竖立起各种心理屏障。然而，你看，你可以通过认识到你的实相在另一个方向继续存在，而不仅仅是你主要认同的那个方向，来跟随这些思想和情感。因为这些已经离开你意识心智的思想和情感，会引领你进入其他环境。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Forget now that in this case such a division occurred, and imagine instead the successive thoughts and feelings that you possess. When you feel weak you are weak. When you feel joyful your body benefits and becomes stronger. Augustus's case simply shows in exaggerated form the effects of your beliefs upon your physical image. If you think, "Aha, then from now on I will only think good thoughts -- and therefore be healthy, and inhibit my 'bad' thoughts, or do anything at all with them but think them," then in your own way you are doing what Augustus did. He began by believing that some of his thoughts were so evil that they must somehow be made nonexistent. So inhibiting what you consider as negative thoughts, or assuming that they are so terrible, is no answer.
quotes.translation: 现在忘记在这个案例中发生了这样的分裂，而是想象你所拥有的连续不断的思想和感受。当你感到虚弱时，你就是虚弱的。当你感到喜悦时，你的身体受益并变得更强壮。奥古斯都的案例只是以夸张的形式展示了你的信念对你身体形象的影响。如果你认为，"啊哈，那么从现在起我只想好的念头——因此就会健康，并抑制我的'坏'念头，或者对它们做任何事，就是不去想它们，"那么你就在以自己的方式做着奥古斯都所做的事。他一开始相信他的一些念头是如此邪恶，以至于必须以某种方式让它们不存在。因此，抑制你认为的负面念头，或假设它们如此可怕，都不是解决之道。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Your own value system then is built up of your beliefs about reality, and those beliefs form your experience. Suppose you believe that to be "good" you must try to be perfect. You may have been told, or read, that the spirit is perfect, and hence thought that your duty was to reproduce that perfect spirit in flesh as best you could. To this end you attempt to deny all imperfect thoughts and emotions. Your own "negative" thoughts appall you. You may believe also what I have told you -- that your thoughts create your reality -- so you become all the more frightened at mental or actual expressions of an aggressive nature. You may be so concerned about hurting someone else that you hardly dare move. Trying to be perfect all the time can be far more than a nuisance: It can be disastrous because of your misunderstanding.
quotes.translation: 你的价值体系是由你对实相的看法所构成的，而这些看法又形成了你的经验。假设你相信要“好”就必须力求完美。你可能被告知或读到过，灵性是完美的，因此认为你的职责就是尽己所能，在肉体中复制那完美的灵性。为此，你试图否认所有不完美的思想和情绪。你自己的“负面”思想让你惊恐。你可能也相信我所告诉你的——你的思想创造了你的实相——因此你对任何攻击性的心智或实际表达更加恐惧。你可能如此担心伤害别人，以至于几乎不敢动弹。一直试图完美，远不止是麻烦：由于你的误解，它可能是灾难性的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The thought, as an electrical reality, is not subject to the physical laws that bind the physical body. The durability of the thought as an electrical act is determined by its initial electric charge. This electric charge can propel the thought, as an electrical action, through the apparent dimensions of your physical time in such a manner that it can exist simultaneously within your past and your present. Because a thought, as electric action, may exist both within your past and your present, this is not to say that it becomes two electric actions, one existing in the past and one existing in the future. Rather the one electric action or thought is simultaneously projected, through a peculiarity of its axis, so that it appears within your field, not in two places at once, but in two times at once.
quotes.translation: 思想作为一种电性实相，不受束缚肉体的物理法则所制约。思想作为电性行为的持久性，由其初始电荷决定。这种电荷能将思想作为电性行为，以某种方式推进你物理时间的明显维度，使其能同时存在于你的过去和现在。因为思想作为电性行为，可能同时存在于你的过去和现在，但这并不意味着它变成了两个电性行为，一个存在于过去，一个存在于未来。相反，这单一的电性行为或思想，通过其轴线的特性被同时投射，因此它出现在你的场域中，不是同时出现在两个地方，而是同时出现在两个时间点。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Every thought in one way or another is constructed by you in physical terms. You cannot escape the result of one thought. Every thought is an actuality. It affects every action and it forms your physical environment. As you sit listening to my voice, you are all subconsciously forming the physical environment of your classroom. You are forming the physical chairs. You are forming the blackboard. You are all forming reality, as you know it. Then with your physical senses, you are perceiving that which you have already so created. If you do not like what you see, then who is to blame? For these are your own constructions , formed in faithful replica to your own thoughts. We do not have the time to explain now how this is done, but in the material we have explained it. Certain telepathic connections exist which are what we term root assumptions, of which you are all telepathically aware. Using these you form a physical environment that is cohesive enough so that you can all agree with what you see and feel and smell and touch.
quotes.translation: 每一个思想，都以某种方式由你在物理层面构建。你无法逃避任何一个思想的结果。每一个思想都是一个实相。它影响每一个行动，并形成你的物理环境。当你坐着听我的声音时，你们都在潜意识地形成你们教室的物理环境。你们在形成物理的椅子。你们在形成黑板。你们都在形成你们所知的实相。然后，用你们的物理感官，你们在感知你们已经如此创造的东西。如果你不喜欢你所看到的，那么该怪谁呢？因为这些都是你自己的构建，忠实地复制了你自己的思想。我们现在没有时间解释这是如何做到的，但在资料中我们已经解释过了。存在某些心灵感应的连接，我们称之为根本假设，你们所有人都能心灵感应地意识到这些。利用这些，你们形成一个足够凝聚的物理环境，以便你们都能对所看、所感、所闻、所触达成一致。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: We must go much further into this particular subject. Nevertheless it must be clearly understood that thoughts and emotions are actualities in themselves. that directly work upon the physical mechanism. Any thought or emotion is bound to directly affect the physical body. Because thoughts and emotions, as electrical actualities, are independent from their subjective point of origin, a given thought or emotion, initiated by an individual, may be rejected by him and cast out. If the thought or emotion is similar to those usually accepted, then this will take time, for new electrical patterns must be set up. But an idea, thought or emotion so rejected still has independent actuality, and may be attracted to the emotional climate of another.
quotes.translation: 我们必须更深入地探讨这个特定主题。然而，必须清楚理解，思想和情绪本身就是实相，它们直接作用于物理机制。任何思想或情绪都必然直接影响物理身体。因为思想和情绪作为电性实相，独立于它们的主观起源点，一个由个体发起的特定思想或情绪，可能被他拒绝并抛弃。如果该思想或情绪与通常被接受的相似，那么这将需要时间，因为必须建立新的电性模式。但一个被如此拒绝的想法、思想或情绪仍然具有独立的实相，并可能被另一个人的情绪氛围所吸引。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: I have myself heard it said that other creatures behave with a natural grace, save man. I have myself heard it said that all of nature is (pause) content unto itself save man, who is filled with discontent. Such thoughts follow "naturally" the dictums of so-called rational thought. When you think such thoughts, you think of them at the most strained level of intellectual speculation -- that is, the thoughts seem self-evident to the intellect that is forced to operate by itself, relatively speaking, divorced from the self's other faculties. It then does indeed seem that man is somehow apart from nature -- or worse, an ungrateful blight, almost a parasite, upon the face of the planet.
quotes.translation: 我亲耳听到有人说，除了人类，其他生物都带着自然的优雅行事。我亲耳听到有人说，除了充满不满的人类，整个自然都（停顿）自得其乐。这类思想“自然”地遵循所谓理性思维的教条。当你思考这些思想时，你是在最紧张的知识思辨层面上思考它们——也就是说，这些思想对于被迫独自运作的智力来说似乎是显而易见的，相对而言，与自我的其他能力分离。那么，人类确实似乎与自然分离——或者更糟，是地球表面一个忘恩负义的祸害，几乎是一个寄生虫。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth speaks in Session 826, held for Mass Events on March 8, 1978:

quotes.text: You think of viruses as evil, spreading perhaps from country to country, to "invade" scores of physical mechanisms. Now thoughts are "contagious." You have a natural immunity against all thoughts that do not fit in with your own purposes and beliefs, and naturally (pause, groping), you are "inoculated" with a wholesome trust and belief in your own thoughts above others. The old ideas of voodooism recognized some of these concepts, but complicated and distorted them with fears of evil, psychic invasion, psychic killing, and so forth. You cannot divide, say, mental and physical health, nor can you divide a person's philosophy from his bodily condition.
quotes.translation: 你认为病毒是邪恶的，可能从一个国家传播到另一个国家，以“入侵”大量的物理机制。现在，思想是“有传染性的”。你对所有不符合你自己目的和信念的思想都有自然的免疫力，并且自然（停顿，摸索着），你被“接种”了一种对自己思想的健康信任和信念，高于他人。伏都教的旧观念承认了其中一些概念，但用对邪恶、灵性入侵、灵性杀戮等的恐惧，使它们复杂化和扭曲。你不能将，比如说，心智健康与身体健康分开，也不能将一个人的哲学与其身体状况分开。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 841, MARCH 14, 1979
9:08 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: You cooperate together to form the physical reality that you know, telepathically, through ways and means that are unknown to you. You weave webs of psychic reality that then coalesce into physical reality. You do not necessarily weave them alone, but together. Your thoughts intertwine with those of others. You are responsible for your own thoughts. You need to learn the power of thought and emotion, but this should fill you with the joy of creativity. Once you realize that your thoughts form reality, then you are no longer a slave to events. You simply have to learn the methods.
quotes.translation: 你们共同合作，通过自身未知的方式与手段，以心电感应构建出你们所熟知的物质实相。你们编织出心灵实相的网络，这些网络随后凝聚成物质实相。你们未必独自编织，而是共同完成。你们的思想与他人的思想相互交织。你们需为自己的思想负责，必须学习思想与情感的力量，但这应让你们充满创造的喜悦。一旦意识到思想塑造实相，你们便不再受制于事件，只需掌握方法即可。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Each person alive helps paint the living picture of civilization as it exists at any given time, in your terms. Be your own best artist. Your thoughts, feelings and expectations are like the living brush strokes with which you paint your corner of life's landscape. If you do your best in your own life, then you are indeed helping to improve the quality of all life. Your thoughts are as real as snowflakes or raindrops or clouds. They mix and merge with the thoughts of others, to form man's livingscape, providing the vast mental elements from which physical events will be formed.
quotes.translation: 每个活着的人，都在以你们的时间观念，协助描绘文明在任一特定时刻的鲜活图景。做自己最出色的艺术家吧。你们的思想、情感与期待，如同鲜活的笔触，用以描绘你们生命风景的角落。若在自己的生命中竭尽全力，便确实在帮助提升所有生命的品质。你们的思想与雪花、雨滴或云朵一样真实。它们与他人的思想混合交融，形成人类的生命景观，提供构成物质事件所需的广阔心灵元素。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Over two years ago, in Note 2 for Session 801, and in the opening notes for Session 805, I described our decision to add the writing room to the house. All four of us involved in the moving were quite amused, then -- for the friends who helped Jane and me carry the furniture yesterday are the father-and-son contracting team that built the room in the first place.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: If you will consider the projection of a thought, that is intangible, so that it affects another individual, and hence both directly and indirectly affects the action of physical matter, then you may consider the possibility of other such projections. We have here a rather delicate point. I have said that there are no duplicates. Yet you may say, are not some thoughts duplicates? The variations may indeed be slight, but the variations are always present. A thought transmitted knowingly or unknowingly by "A" is not precisely the same thought when it reaches receiver "B".
quotes.translation: 若你思考一个无形思想的投射，使其影响他人，从而直接或间接影响物质实相的行动，那么你便可考虑其他类似投射的可能性。此处有一个相当微妙之处。我曾说过不存在复制品。然而你或许会问：难道有些思想不是复制品吗？差异或许确实细微，但差异始终存在。由“A”有意或无意传递的思想，当它抵达接收者“B”时，已不再是完全相同的思想。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Man automatically began to form culture. He did not start with the rudiments of culture, as is thought. He did not learn (pause) through trial and error to think clear thoughts. He thought quite clearly from the beginning. He did learn through trial and error various ways of best translating those thoughts into physical action. The first cultures were as rich as your own. In your terms, reading and writing are great advantages, but it is also true that in the past the mind was also used to record information, and transmit it with an artistry that you do not now use.
quotes.translation: 人类自动开始形成文化。他并非如人们所想，从文化的雏形起步。他并非通过试错学习清晰思考。他从一开始就思考得相当清晰。他确实通过试错学习如何以最佳方式将这些思想转化为物质行动。最早的文化与你们的文化同样丰富。以你们的时间观念，读写能力是巨大优势，但同样真实的是，过去的心灵也被用来记录信息，并以你们现今不再使用的艺术性方式传递信息。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: As soon as the attempt is made to duplicate the original thought, then we find that the attempt itself strains and pulls: the impulses change minutely or to a greater degree. The point that I want to make here is that any attempt at such duplication actually forces, because of the nature of the attempt, the impulses to line up in a different pattern. When B receives the thought, it is already a new thought, bearing great resemblance to the original, but it is not the identical thought.
quotes.translation: 一旦试图复制原始思想，我们便会发现这种尝试本身造成紧张与拉扯：冲动发生细微或较大程度的改变。我在此想指出的是：任何此类复制尝试，实际上因其本质，迫使冲动以不同模式排列。当B接收到思想时，它已是一个新思想，与原始思想极为相似，但并非完全相同的思想。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: A note now concerning thought, as it is a form of action with which all men are familiar. Here you can see that your ego accepts thoughts as a part of its identity. Thought's actions are accepted by the ego, yet the ego seems to stand apart from them; and because of ego's nature it fears to plunge into the action of a thought. For it, the ego, has but recently pried itself from action, and so perceives action now as if action were a province of the ego, and not the other way around.
quotes.translation: 现在关于思想的一点说明：思想是一种所有人都熟悉的行动形式。在此你们可以看到，自我将思想视为其身份的一部分。自我接受思想的活动，却又似乎与之保持距离；由于自我的本质，它害怕投入思想的活动之中。因为自我刚刚才从行动中挣脱出来，所以现在将行动视为自我的领域，而非相反。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You must remember that you dwell always in a natural framework -- which means that your thoughts themselves are as natural, say, as the locks of your hair. In what may seem to you to be an odd analogy I will compare your thoughts with viruses,* for they are alive, always present, responsive, and possess their own kind of mobility. Physically speaking at least, thoughts are chemically propelled, and they travel through the universal body as viruses travel through your temporal form.
quotes.translation: 你必须记住，你始终存在于一个自然的架构中——这意味着你的思想本身就像你的头发一样自然。用一个在你看来或许奇怪的比喻，我将把你的思想比作病毒*，因为它们是有生命的、始终存在、有反应能力，并且拥有自己的移动方式。至少在物理层面上，思想是由化学驱动的，它们穿越宇宙之体，就像病毒穿越你暂时的形体一样。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In one respect, and in quite a legitimate and objective manner, all thoughts are also projections. They leave you in an objective actual manner, and exert an objective effect. They are a part of your psychic identity projected outward, yet you do not feel any loss. Nor are you aware of what happens to these thoughts. You could not retain simultaneously all and exert all the thoughts of your lifetime thus far in any normally conscious way. To do so would be psychological suicide.
quotes.translation: 从某个方面来说，以一种相当合理且客观的方式，所有的思想也都是投射。它们以一种客观实际的方式离开你，并产生客观的影响。它们是你向外投射的心理身份的一部分，然而你并未感到任何损失。你也不清楚这些思想发生了什么。你无法以任何正常意识的方式同时保留并发挥你迄今为止一生中的所有思想。这样做将是心理上的自杀。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Now. The nature of the thought that is received by our sender B is determined by many factors. We shall merely consider a few of these. These include, to begin with, the original intensity of the thought as A possesses it, As ability to duplicate the thought as far as possible, the relative stability of the electrical thought unit as it is formed by A, the familiarity or unfamiliarity of the range of frequencies that compose the new thought to any intended receiver.
quotes.translation: 现在。被我们的发送者B接收到的思想的性质由许多因素决定。我们只考虑其中几个。这些因素包括：首先，A所拥有的思想的原始强度；A尽可能复制该思想的能力；由A形成的电性思想单位的相对稳定性；以及构成新思想的频率范围对任何预期接收者的熟悉或不熟悉程度。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: "Any event, therefore, has an invisible thickness, a multidimensional basis. Your skies are filled with breezes, currents, clouds, sunlight, dust particles and so forth. The sky vaults above the entire planet. The invisible [vault of] Framework 2 contains endless patterns that change as, say, clouds do -- that mix and merge to form your psychological climate. Thoughts have what we will for now term electromagnetic properties. In those terms your thoughts mix and match with others in Framework 2, creating mass patterns that form the overall psychological basis behind world events. Again, however, Framework 2 is not neutral, but automatically inclined toward what we will here term good or constructive developments. It is a growth medium. Constructive or 'positive' feelings or thoughts are more easily materialized than 'negative' ones because they are in keeping with Framework 2's characteristics."
quotes.translation: "因此，任何事件都有一种无形的厚度，一个多维的基础。你的天空充满了微风、气流、云朵、阳光、尘埃粒子等等。天空穹顶覆盖着整个星球。框架二的无形穹顶包含着无尽的模式，它们像云一样变化——混合并融合，形成你的心理气候。思想具有我们暂且称之为电磁特性的东西。用这些术语来说，你的思想在框架二中与其他思想混合匹配，创造出大规模的模式，这些模式构成了世界事件背后整体的心理基础。然而，再次强调，框架二并非中性的，而是自动倾向于我们在此称之为善的或建设性的发展。它是一个生长的媒介。建设性或'正面'的感受或思想比'负面'的更容易物质化，因为它们与框架二的特性相符。"
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth speaks in Session 826, held for Mass Events on March 8, 1978:

quotes.text: All of these reality systems of which we have been speaking are closely connected. Every thought has this same electric potential. Thoughts are often translated into dream images by a simple transformation. There is little difference indeed, basically, between physical images and dream images, and apparitions, and very little difference between these and thoughts and emotions.
quotes.translation: 我们一直在讨论的所有这些实相系统都紧密相连。每一个思想都具有相同的电性潜能。思想常常通过简单的转化被翻译成梦境意象。基本上，物理意象、梦境意象和幻象之间几乎没有区别，而这些与思想和情感之间也几乎没有区别。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The more intense your imagination and inner experience, therefore, the more important it is that you realize the methods by which this inner experience becomes physically real. Your thoughts and emotions begin their journey into physical actualization at the moment of conception. If you happen to live in an area where the coordinate environment is strong, one of those areas I have spoken of as unusually conducive, then it will seem that you are deluged by illnesses or disasters, if these are the nature of your thoughts, because all thought is so fertile in this environment. If, on the other hand, your feelings and subjective experience are fairly well balanced, fairly optimistic and creative in a constructive manner, then it will seem to you that you have been blessed with unusual luck, for your pleasant suppositions will come to pass so quickly.
quotes.translation: 因此，你的想象和内在体验越强烈，你就越需要认识到这些内在体验变成物理现实的方法。你的思想和情感在受孕的那一刻就开始了它们进入物理实现的旅程。如果你恰好生活在一个坐标环境强大的区域——我称之为异常有利的区域之一——那么如果你的思想本质如此，你似乎就会被疾病或灾难淹没，因为在这个环境中所有思想都如此丰饶。另一方面，如果你的感受和主观体验相当平衡、相当乐观且以建设性的方式富有创造力，那么你似乎就会被异常的好运眷顾，因为你愉快的假设会如此迅速地实现。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Emotions are also, then, actualities within the overall electric system, existing not as representations of feeling, but as definite charged action. I want if possible to get the idea over that this system involves realities that are directly experienced and vivid, and also that in some ways emotions and thoughts are more directly perceived in that field than your physical field. They are more readily apparent, with more force behind them acting directly upon the electric environment, without any intermediary steps; that is, they, emotions and feelings and thoughts, exist immediately within the environment as electrical forces within that system. In your system they need to be translated and given reality by a method of interrelationships. Your field attains its actuality only as a result of the cooperating merger of many other independent systems.
quotes.translation: 情绪同样也是整体电力系统内的实相，它们并非作为感受的表征存在，而是作为明确的带电行动存在。如果可能的话，我希望传达这样的概念：这个系统涉及直接体验且生动鲜活的实相，并且在某些方面，情绪和思想在那个场域中比在你们的物质场域中更直接被感知。它们更显而易见，背后有更强的力量直接作用于电力环境，无需任何中间步骤；也就是说，情绪、感受和思想作为该系统中的电力力量直接存在于环境之中。而在你们的系统中，它们需要通过一种相互关系的方法被翻译并赋予实相。你们的场域只有通过许多其他独立系统的合作融合才能获得其实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The thoughts may rather faithfully, though never completely, translate such data, or they may considerably distort it. Behind thoughts are images, which are more basic but still physically oriented. Because they are more basic, they have a stronger effect. They are more Emotionally charged, more concise than thoughts, and they are directly connected with the mechanics involved in translating inner data to physical reality.
quotes.translation: 思想可能相当忠实地（尽管从不完全）翻译这类数据，也可能严重扭曲它。思想背后是意象，这些意象更基本但仍以物质为导向。因为它们更基本，所以具有更强的影响。它们比思想更具情绪电荷，更简洁，并且直接与将内在数据翻译为物质实相的机制相关联。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: You are familiar with such a limited portion of the actuality of your own thoughts. These thoughts are things, so to speak, as real as a chair; but you are acquainted only with their purely subjective reality. They have great force within the electric field, great attractions. If you could fully understand or comprehend directly the reality of a thought in its full actuality, you would be amazed at the power behind it.
quotes.translation: 你们对自己思想的实相只熟悉如此有限的部分。可以说，这些思想是事物，像椅子一样真实；但你们只了解它们纯粹的主观实相。它们在电力场中具有巨大的力量，巨大的吸引力。如果你们能完全理解或直接领悟一个思想在其完整实相中的真实面貌，你们会对它背后的力量感到惊讶。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Nor does the receiver receive the thought in the same condition. The thought, the original thought, is retained by A. A, however, forms a thought as nearly identical as his possibilities allow it to be. This he transmits to B. But B can't receive the thought in its present condition, for the act of receiving a thought also changes it. He forms a thought as nearly identical as possible, and interprets it.
quotes.translation: 接收者也不会以相同的状态接收思想。原始思想由A保留。然而，A会形成一个尽可能接近其可能性的思想。他将这个思想传递给B。但B无法以当前状态接收这个思想，因为接收思想的行为也会改变它。他会形成一个尽可能接近的思想，并加以解读。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The artist does not identify with the colors he uses. He knows he chooses them, and applies them with a brush. So you paint your reality with your ideas in the same manner. You are not your ideas, nor even your thoughts. You are the self who experiences them. If a painter finds his hands stained with pigment at the end of a day, he can wash the stain off easily, knowing its nature. If you think that limiting thoughts are a portion of you, permanently attached therefore, you will not think of washing them off. You would behave instead like a mad artist who says, "My paints are a part of me. They have stained my fingers, and there is nothing I can do about it."
quotes.translation: 艺术家不会与他使用的颜色认同。他知道自己选择它们，并用画笔涂抹它们。同样，你们用你们的观念描绘你们的实相。你们不是你们的观念，甚至不是你们的思想。你们是体验它们的那个自己。如果一位画家在一天结束时发现手上沾满了颜料，他了解其性质，可以轻松洗掉污渍。如果你们认为限制性的思想是你们的一部分，因此永久附着，你们就不会想到要洗掉它们。相反，你们会像一个疯狂的艺术家那样说："我的颜料是我的一部分。它们染污了我的手指，我对此无能为力。"
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The self that you are aware of represents only one "position" in which those invisible particles happen to intersect, gain mass, build up form. Scientists can only perceive an electron as it is to them. They cannot really track it. They cannot be certain of its position and its speed at the same time, and to some extent the same applies to your consciousness. The speed of your own thoughts takes those thoughts away from you even as you think them--and you can never really examine a thought, but only the thought of a thought (with quiet amusement).
quotes.translation: 你们所意识到的自己只代表一个"位置"，那些不可见的粒子恰好在这个位置相交、获得质量、构建形态。科学家只能感知到电子相对于他们的样子。他们无法真正追踪它。他们无法同时确定它的位置和速度，在某种程度上，这同样适用于你们的意识。你们自己思想的速度使那些思想在你们思考它们的同时就远离了你们——你们永远无法真正审视一个思想，只能审视一个思想的思想（带着安静的愉悦）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The thought originally held by A is still retained by A, yet a seemingly identical thought reaches B. A has lost nothing. That is, in sending the thought, in trying to duplicate the thought, he himself still retains it, so what is passed on to receiver B? This is rather important, since an explanation will do much to account for the frequent difference that occurs in telepathic communications.
quotes.translation: 甲原本持有的思想仍为甲所保留，但看似相同的思想却传到了乙那里。甲并未失去任何东西。也就是说，在传递思想、试图复制思想的过程中，他本人仍保有它，那么传递给接收者乙的又是什么呢？这一点相当重要，因为这一解释将大大有助于说明心灵感应交流中经常出现的差异。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Since you have all kinds of thoughts there are reasons for having them, as you have all kinds of geography. Within your reality it is as foolish to deny the existence of certain thoughts as it would be, say, to pretend that deserts do not exist. In following such a course you deny dimensions of experience and diminish your reality. This does not mean that you have to collect what you think of as negative thoughts, any more than it means that you should spend a month in a desert if you do not like them. Period. It does mean that within nature as you understand it, nothing is meaningless or to be pretended out of existence.
quotes.translation: 既然你有各种思想，那么拥有它们就有其原因，正如你有各种地理形态一样。在你的实相中，否认某些思想的存在，就如同假装沙漠不存在一样愚蠢。遵循这样的做法，你否定了经验的维度，削弱了你的实相。这并不意味着你必须收集你认为负面的思想，正如它并不意味着如果你不喜欢沙漠，就应该在那里待一个月一样。句号。它确实意味着，在你所理解的自然中，没有任何事物是无意义的，或应该被假装不存在。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: This time the psychic safety valve has had too much. The nicest thing that could happen would be that you suddenly blew your stack and kicked him. The worst thing that could happen would be that once again you restrain the acknowledgement of the pent up, perfectly natural aggression that is now ready to explode -- so you send out a thought-form out of all proportion to any of the events that have transpired. The thought-form causes your friend severe harm; and all of this because you were afraid that one stray aggressive thought of yours was more powerful than the vitality that resides in each of you.
quotes.translation: 这一次，心灵的安全阀已经承受了太多。可能发生的最好的事情是你突然爆发，踢他一脚。可能发生的最坏的事情是你再次压抑对那被压抑的、完全自然的、现在即将爆发的攻击性的承认——于是你发送出一个与已发生的任何事件完全不成比例的思想形态。这个思想形态给你的朋友造成了严重伤害；而这一切都是因为你害怕你那一个游荡的攻击性思想比你们每个人内在的生命力更强大。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This characteristic of materializing thoughts and emotions into physical realities is an attribute of the soul. Now in your reality, these thoughts are made physical. In other realities, they may be "constructed" in an entirely different fashion. So your soul, that which you are, constructs your physical daily reality for you from the nature of your thoughts and expectations.
quotes.translation: 这种将思想和情感物质化为物理实相的特性是灵魂的一种属性。现在，在你的实相中，这些思想被物质化了。在其他实相中，它们可能以完全不同的方式被“构建”。所以，你的灵魂，即你所是的那个，根据你思想和期望的本质，为你构建了你日常的物理实相。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Creation is constant. Due to the nature of action creation cannot be anything but simultaneous. Each act of creation brings forth another, and opens up further dimensions of activity. Within your own system, thought becomes materialized, and there is literally no end to the activity of thoughts. Thoughts however are connected with language and with highly organized ego development. They are translations and symbols for inner activity. As a rule they are highly physically oriented, their function being to acquaint the physically adapted ego with some inner data.
quotes.translation: 创造是持续的。由于行动的本质，创造只能是同时发生的。每一个创造行为都会引出另一个，并开启进一步的活动维度。在你自己的系统中，思想被物质化，而思想的活动确实没有尽头。然而，思想与语言以及高度组织的自我发展相关联。它们是内在活动的翻译和象征。通常，它们高度面向物理，其功能是让适应物理的自我了解某些内在数据。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: You are responsible for your thoughts. However your spontaneous thoughts are not nearly as destructive as he imagines, and in over inhibiting them, he also inhibits purely spontaneous, joyful thoughts. Psycho cybernetics helps him not to brood, not to worry in advance, and set up negative reactions. This is important. But honest reactions within the present are legitimate.
quotes.translation: 你要为自己的思想负责。然而，你的自发思想远没有他想象的那么具有破坏性，在过度抑制它们时，他也抑制了纯粹自发、喜悦的思想。心理控制论帮助他不去沉思，不提前担忧，并建立负面反应。这很重要。但在当下的诚实反应是正当的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: You cannot treat thoughts and imagination in such a literal manner, nor in a large respect should you try to "guard your thoughts" as if they were herds of animals that you wanted to keep purely bred. Your thoughts do form your reality. If you do not fear them, however, they create their own balances. The psyche dwells in a reality so different from the world you usually recognize that there good and evil, as you think of them, are also seen to be as operationally or relatively true as the difference between the perceiver and the object perceived.
quotes.translation: 你不能以如此字面的方式对待思想和想象，大体上也不应试图"守护你的思想"，仿佛它们是你要保持纯种的牲畜群。你的思想确实构成了你的现实。然而，如果你不惧怕它们，它们自会创造自身的平衡。心灵栖居的现实与你通常认知的世界如此不同，以至于你所认为的善恶，在运作上或相对意义上，就像感知者与被感知对象之间的差异一样真实。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is wrong to think that restraint under such circumstances or discussions is cowardly, though of course, it may be. To refrain from argument while simultaneously and deliberately forcing thoughts or pictures of peace, represents a positive action. The intensity of the peaceful images or thoughts unfortunately rarely match the intensity of the bitterness, you see. Thoughts of reconciliation or peace actually and practically do their bit to destroy the causes of war. Since Ruburt has somewhat come around, you may take a break and I shall continue.
quotes.translation: 认为在此类情境或讨论中保持克制是懦弱的，这是错误的——尽管当然也可能如此。在刻意强迫自己保持和平思想或画面的同时避免争论，这代表一种积极行动。可惜的是，和平意象或思想的强度往往难以匹配怨恨的强度。和解或和平的思想实际上确实在逐步摧毁战争的根源。既然鲁柏有所好转，你可以休息片刻，我将继续。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Such thoughts are like, now (underline "like, now"), electrical patterns that form their own magnets. (Long pause.) The ability to conceptualize is present in the fetus, and the fetus does conceptualize. The precise orientation of that conceptualizing, and the precise orientation of the thinking patterns, wait for certain physical triggers received from the parents and the environment after birth, but the processes of conceptualization and of thought are already established. This establishment takes place in genetic dreams (again, all intently).
quotes.translation: 这些思想就像（现在强调"就像"）形成自身磁场的电性模式。（长停顿）胎儿已具备概念化能力，并且确实在进行概念化。这种概念化的精确取向，以及思维模式的精确取向，需等待出生后从父母和环境接收的特定生理触发，但概念化与思维的过程已然建立。这种建立发生在基因梦境中（再次强调，全神贯注地）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Here in Dreams Seth uses the uncertainty principle as an analogy (and an excellent one), meaning that as the positions and motions of elementary particles, say, cannot be simultaneously measured precisely, so our genetic qualities and their motions can not always be specifically determined. In Dreams he's already said (in Session 909) that the human species has an "amazing interplay between genetic preciseness and genetic freedom," and (in Session 910) that "your genetic structure reacts to each thought that you have, to the state of your emotions, to your psychological climate." Choices and probabilities apply. Thus do we avoid genetic rigidity.

quotes.text: The same applies in the dream state; but there, the shadows of your thoughts may be projected outward into scenes of darkest desolation. In the physical world you have mass sense data about you. Each individual helps form that exterior environment. No matter how dark your mood on any given sunny day, your individual thoughts alone will not suddenly turn the blue skies into rainy ones. You alone do not have that kind of control over your fellows' environment. In the dream world, however, such thoughts will definitely form your environment.
quotes.translation: 梦境状态同样如此；但在那里，你思想的阴影可能向外投射成最荒凉凄惨的场景。在物质世界中，你周围有大量感官数据。每个个体都在帮助形成那个外在环境。无论某个晴朗日子里你的心情多么阴郁，单凭你个人的思想不会突然将蓝天变成雨天。你独自一人无法对同伴的环境拥有那种控制力。然而在梦境世界中，这类思想确实会形成你的环境。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 720
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: If you will remember the three creative dilemmas discussed in our past session, you will see that we have here the reason for our self-perpetuating universe, the reason for termination within it, and the inherent necessity for change. If one thought were held forever, no other thoughts would follow, no action would follow, and no identity. In your own intimate psychological experience, in the intimate psychological experience of every individual within your race, you will find recognition of the thought.
quotes.translation: 如果你记得我们上次课程讨论过的三个创造性困境，就会明白这里包含了我们宇宙自我延续的原因、其中终结的原因，以及改变的内在必要性。如果一个思想被永远持有，就不会有后续思想、行动或身份认同。在你自身亲密的心理体验中，在你种族每个个体的亲密心理体验中，你都会找到对该思想的认知。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Experience is the product of the mind, the spirit, conscious thoughts and feelings, and unconscious thoughts and feelings. These together form the reality that you know. You are hardly at the mercy of a reality, therefore, that exists apart from yourself, or is thrust upon you. You are so intimately connected with the physical events composing your life experience that often you cannot distinguish between the seemingly material occurrences and the thoughts, expectations and desires that gave them birth.
quotes.translation: 经验是心智、灵性、显意识思想与感受、潜意识思想与感受的产物。这些共同构成了你所知的现实。因此，你绝非受制于某个独立于你之外或强加于你的现实。你与构成生命经历的物质事件如此紧密相连，以至于常常无法区分看似物质性的事件与催生它们的思想、期待和渴望。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Thought cannot be seen or touched. Thought is action. A thought within your field must vanish, be terminated, disappear, before it can be replaced by another. The identical thought will not return. A very similar thought may return, but the two thoughts will not be identical, although you may perceive them as identical. This is an error of perception.
quotes.translation: 思想无法被看见或触摸。思想即是行动。你场域内的一个思想必须消失、终止、隐退，才能被另一个思想取代。完全相同的思想不会重现。极其相似的思想或许会重现，但这两个思想绝不会完全相同，尽管你可能认为它们相同——这是感知上的错误。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You often avoid this natural treatment, however, and run from frightening conscious thoughts that would in their turn lead you to the source of "negative" beliefs, where they could be faced; you could then travel through them, so to speak, into feelings of joy and victory. Instead, for example, many of you accept the way of drugs, where such feelings and thoughts are thrust upon you, or forced out of you while you are denied the stabilizing comforts of the conscious mind.
quotes.translation: 然而，你们常常回避这种自然处理方式，逃离那些令人恐惧的清醒思想——这些思想本会引导你们找到"负面"信念的源头，使你们得以面对它们；你们可以穿越这些信念，可以说，抵达喜悦与胜利的感受。相反，例如你们许多人接受药物之道，让这些感受与思想被强加于你，或被迫从你体内排出，而同时你被剥夺了清醒心智带来的稳定慰藉。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: There are more than your thought forms in other words. Your thought forms however can be used as definite aids when you are in the proper mental condition, and they can impede your progress if you are not. In physical reality a man in a desperate frame of mind is more apt to emphasize horrible aspects in the news, for example, and to see desperation rather than joy in the faces of those he meets. He will ignore the contented playful child on one side of the street, and notice instead a dirty ragged boy even though he be further away. So your frame of mind when projecting will largely determine the sort of experiences you have, and the environments you visit.
quotes.translation: 换言之，存在的远不止你的思想形态。然而，当你处于适当的心智状态时，你的思想形态可作为明确助力；若你状态不佳，它们则会阻碍你的进展。在物质实相中，一个绝望心境的人更易关注新闻中恐怖的一面，例如，在遇见的人脸上看到绝望而非喜悦。他会忽略街边满足嬉戏的孩童，反而注意到远处一个肮脏褴褛的男孩。因此，你投射时的心境将极大决定你所经历的经验类型，以及你所造访的环境。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Your thoughts are. You may approve or disapprove of them, in the way that you think of a storm, for example. Left alone, your thoughts are as various, magnificent, trivial, frightening, or glorious as a hurricane, a flower, a flood, a toad, a raindrop or the fog. Your thoughts are perfectly themselves. Left alone, they come and go.
quotes.translation: 你的思想就是存在。你可以赞同或不赞同它们，就像你看待一场风暴那样。若不加干预，你的思想会如飓风、花朵、洪水、蟾蜍、雨滴或迷雾般多样、壮丽、琐碎、可怖或辉煌。你的思想完全如其本然。若不加干预，它们自来自去。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The feelings and sensations give rise to the questions, to the thoughts, to the intellect. The child in a fashion feels -- feels -- its own thoughts rise from a relative psychological invisibility into immediate, vital formation. There is a process there that you have forgotten. The child identifies with its own psychic reality first of all -- then discovers its feelings, and claims those, and discovers its thoughts and intellect, and claims those (all quite intently).
quotes.translation: 感受与知觉引发问题、思想与智性。孩童在某种程度上感受——感受——自己的思想从相对的心理不可见性中升起，进入即刻、生动的成形。那里有一个你们已遗忘的过程。孩童首先认同自己的心灵实相——然后发现自己的感受，并认领它们；接着发现自己的思想与智性，并认领它们（全都相当专注）。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: For our guest: you should tell yourself frequently, "I will only react to constructive suggestions," for this gives you some protection against your own negative thoughts and those of others. You can observe these laws in operation where you work and also, privately, when you are alone. A negative thought, if it is not erased, will almost certainly result in a negative situation, a momentary despondency, a headache, according to the original intensity of the thought.
quotes.translation: 致我们的访客：你应时常告诉自己，"我只对建设性建议做出反应"，因为这会为你提供一些保护，抵御你自己及他人的负面思想。你可以在工作场所观察这些法则的运作，独处时也可私下观察。一个负面思想，若不被消除，几乎必然导致负面情境、短暂沮丧或头痛，具体取决于该思想的原始强度。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Do not think of the mind as a purely mental entity, and of the body as a purely physical one. Instead, think of both mind and body as continuing, interweaving processes that are mental and physical at once. Your thoughts actually are quite as physical as your body is, and your body is quite as nonphysical as it seems to you your thoughts are. You are actually a vital force, existing as a part of your environment, and yet apart from your environment at the same time.
quotes.translation: 不要将心灵视为纯粹的精神实体，而将身体视为纯粹的物质实体。相反，应将心灵与身体视为持续交织、同时兼具精神与物质属性的过程。你的思想实际上与你的身体一样物质化，而你的身体也如同你所认为的思想那般非物质化。你实际上是一股生命力，既作为环境的一部分存在，又同时独立于环境之外。
quotes.book: The Way Toward Health
quotes.source_file: 10-10-10-10The Way Toward Health - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Your question about transmigration (in Session 903 in Dreams) was an excellent one, and did indeed make the matter clear. It is a temptation at times to use more specific scientific terms, but these would be as confusing as the various definitions and classifications (with humor) that you read in the dictionary, so overall we try to hit a "happy medium." (With more humor.)

quotes.text: That is one of the body's primary functions. A sick body is performing that function then, in its way, as well as a healthy one. It is your most intimate feedback system, changing with your thought and experience, giving you in flesh the physical counterpart of your thought. So it is futile to become angry at a symptom, or to deride the body for its condition when it is presenting you with the corporeal replica of your own thought, as it was meant to do.
quotes.translation: 这正是身体的主要功能之一。生病的身体与健康的身体一样，都在以自身方式履行这一功能。它是你最亲密的反馈系统，随你的思想和经历而变化，以血肉之躯呈现你思想的物质对应物。因此，对症状发怒或嘲笑身体的状况是徒劳的——它只是如其所应然地，将你思想的物质复制品呈现给你。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Now: As stated, thoughts are as natural as any portion of the body. They are as much a part of nature as feelings are, but if you set up an arbitrary division -- considering thoughts mental as distinguished from the physical -- then your body may give a truer reflection of your being than your thoughts do.
quotes.translation: 现在：如前所述，思想与身体的任何部分一样自然。它们与情感一样是自然的一部分，但若你人为划分界限——将思想视为精神层面而区别于物质层面——那么你的身体反而可能比思想更真实地反映你的存在。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The difference is not in construction, for the methods of construction are the same. For example, Mr. A in his bedroom telepathically picks up the thought of his brother many miles distant. Now. If he merely picks up his brother's thought, and the thought is, quote: " I am dying. I wish I could say goodbye to my brother," then the receiver of the message could form the apparition of the dying brother, and then perceive it in his bedroom.
quotes.translation: 差异不在于构造方式，因为构造方法相同。例如，A先生在其卧室中通过心灵感应接收到数英里外兄弟的思想。若他仅接收到兄弟的思想，内容为："我快死了。真希望能向兄弟告别"，那么接收者就能形成濒死兄弟的幻象，并在卧室中感知到它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: We should perhaps differentiate between thought words and speech, or spoken words. Though words are used by many for some interval. I am speaking of those who have survived death in your terms. They do not need the spoken word. Even thought communication however need not necessarily follow the form of words. There can be thought without words (smile), in other words.
quotes.translation: 我们或许需要区分思想语言与言语或口头语言。虽然许多人会在一段时间内使用语言，但我指的是那些在你们看来已超越死亡的存在。他们不需要口头语言。但即使思想交流也未必遵循语言形式。换言之，存在无语言的思想（微笑）。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: ("It is a universal truth that whatever you impress on your subconscious mind is expressed on the screen of space as condition, experience, and event. Thought is incipient action. The reaction is a response from your subconscious mind which corresponds with the nature of your thought. Your subconscious mind can be likened to the soil which can grow all kinds of seeds, good or bad. Every thought is a cause and every condition an effect."
quotes.translation: "这是一个普遍真理：你铭刻在潜意识中的任何事物，都会作为状况、经历和事件显现在空间的屏幕上。思想是初期的行动。反应来自你的潜意识，其性质与你的思想相对应。你的潜意识可被比作能生长各种种子的土壤——无论好坏。每个思想都是因，每个状况都是果。"
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: If you perceived the constant change within your body with as much persistence as you attend to its seemingly permanent nature, then you would be amazed that you ever considered the body as one more or less constant, more or less cohesive, entity. Even subjectively you focus upon and indeed manufacture the idea of a relatively stable, relatively permanent conscious self. You stress those ideas and thoughts and attitudes that you recall from "past" experience as your own, completely ignoring those that once were "characteristic" and now are vanished -- ignoring the fact also that you cannot hold thought. The thought of a moment before, in your terms, vanishes away.
quotes.translation: 如果你像关注身体看似恒常的本质那样，执着地觉察其内在的持续变化，那么你会惊讶自己竟曾将身体视为一个或多或少恒定、或多或少连贯的实体。即便在主观上，你也聚焦并确实制造了一个相对稳定、相对恒常的意识自我的概念。你强调那些从"过去"经验中回忆起来、自认为属于你的想法、念头和态度，完全忽略了那些曾经"典型"而今已消失的部分——也忽略了你无法抓住念头的事实。用你们的话来说，前一秒的念头已然消逝。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Matter has little effect within the electrical system, but the effects of the electrical system upon physical matter are great. Now. Thoughts, human thought, has an electric reality; a human thought exists as an independent electrical action, and as such it continues to exist within the electrical system long after it has left its point of origin. This is another important piece of information that will also be referred to later.
quotes.translation: 物质在电性系统内影响甚微，但电性系统对物质实相的影响却极为巨大。现在。念头，人类的念头，具有电性实相；一个人类念头作为独立的电性活动存在，因此它在离开其起源点后，仍会在电性系统内持续存在很久。这是另一项重要信息，稍后也会被提及。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Children experiment with the creation of joyful and frightening events, trying to ascertain for themselves the nature of their control over their own experience. They imagine joyful and terrifying experiences. They are in fact fascinated by the effects that their thoughts, feelings, and purposes have upon daily events. This is a natural learning process. If they create "bogeymen," then they can cause them to disappear also. If their thoughts can cause them to become ill, then there is no real reason for them to fear illness, for it is their own creation. This learning process is nipped in the bud, however. By the time you are adults, it certainly seems that you are a subjective being in an objective universe, at the mercy of others, and with only the most superficial control over the events of your lives.3
quotes.translation: 儿童通过创造快乐与可怕的事件来实验，试图自行确认他们对自身经验掌控的本质。他们想象愉悦与恐怖的经历。事实上，他们着迷于自己的思想、情感和意图对日常事件产生的影响。这是一个自然的学习过程。如果他们创造了"妖怪"，那么他们也能让它们消失。如果他们的思想能让他们生病，那么他们就没有真正理由害怕疾病，因为那是他们自己的创造。然而，这个学习过程在萌芽阶段就被扼杀了。等到你们成年时，你们当然会觉得自己是客观宇宙中的主观存在，受他人摆布，对生活中的事件只有最肤浅的控制力。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 824, MARCH 1, 1978
9:40 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: The child knew "that it came from somewhere else" -- not by chance but by design. The child knew that in one way or another its most intimate thoughts, dreams, and gestures were as connected with the natural world as blades of grass are to a field. The child knew it was a unique and utterly original event or being that on the one hand was its own focus, and that on the other hand belonged to its own time and season. In fact, children let little escape them, so that, again, they experiment constantly in an effort to discover not only the effect of their thoughts and intents and wishes upon others, but the degree to which others influence their own behavior. To that extent, they deal rather directly with probabilities in a way quite foreign to adult behavior.
quotes.translation: 孩子知道"它来自别处"——不是偶然，而是有意为之。孩子知道，无论如何，他们最私密的思想、梦境和姿态都与自然世界相连，就像草叶与田野相连一样。孩子知道自己是一个独特且完全原创的事件或存在，一方面拥有自己的焦点，另一方面又属于自身的时代与季节。事实上，孩子们几乎不放过任何事物，因此他们不断实验，不仅试图发现自己的思想、意图和愿望对他人的影响，也试图发现他人影响自己行为的程度。就此而言，他们以一种与成人行为截然不同的方式相当直接地处理可能性。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 824, MARCH 1, 1978
9:40 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: He was filled with wonder as his own consciousness ever-newly came into being. He had not yet covered over that process with the kind of smooth continuity that your own consciousness has now achieved -- so when he thought a thought he was filled with curiosity: Where had it come from? His own consciousness, then, was forever a source of delight, its changing qualities as noticeable and apparent as the changing sky. The relative smoothness of your own consciousness -- in those terms, at least -- was gained at the expense of certain other experiences, therefore, that were possible otherwise. You could not live in your present world of time if your consciousness was as playful, curious, and creative as it was, for [then] time was also experienced far differently.
quotes.translation: 当他的意识不断焕然一新地涌现时，他充满了惊奇。他尚未用你们意识现已达到的那种平滑连续性来覆盖这个过程——所以当他产生一个念头时，他充满好奇：它从何而来？于是，他的意识永远是喜悦的源泉，其变化的特质如同变幻的天空一样显著而明显。你们自身意识的相对平滑——至少用那些术语来说——是以牺牲某些原本可能拥有的其他经验为代价的。如果你们的意识像过去那样充满嬉戏、好奇和创造力，你们就无法生活在当前的时间世界里，因为[那时]时间也被以截然不同的方式体验。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Regardless of your histories, those early men and women were quite healthy. They had strong teeth and bones. They dealt with the physical world through the purposeful use of the imagination, however, in a way now most difficult to understand. They realized they were mortal, and must die, but their greater awareness of Framework 2 allowed them a larger identification, so they understood that death was not only a natural necessity, but also an opportunity for other kinds of experience and development (see Note 1 for Session 803).
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Give us a moment.... You have taught yourselves to be aware of and to follow only certain portions of your own consciousnesses, so that mentally you consider certain subjects taboo. Thoughts of death and suffering are among those. In a species geared above all to the survival of the fittest, and the competition among species, then any touch of suffering or pain, or thoughts of death, become dishonorable, biologically shameful, cowardly, nearly insane. Life is to be pursued at all costs--not because it is innately meaningful, but because it is the only game going, and it is a game of chance at best. One life is all you have, and that one is everywhere beset by the threat of illness, disaster, and war--and if you escape such drastic circumstances, then you are still left with a life that is the result of no more than lifeless elements briefly coming into a consciousness and vitality that is bound to end.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……你们已教会自己只觉察并追随自身意识的某些部分，因此在心智上，你们将某些主题视为禁忌。死亡与受苦的念头便在其中。在一个首要追求适者生存、物种间竞争的物种中，任何对受苦或痛苦的触碰，或死亡的念头，都变得不光彩、在生物学上可耻、懦弱，近乎疯狂。生命要不惜一切代价追求——不是因为它天生有意义，而是因为它是唯一进行的游戏，而且充其量是一场机会游戏。你只有一条命，而这一条命处处受到疾病、灾难和战争的威胁——即使你逃脱了这些极端境况，你仍然拥有的生命，不过是无生命元素短暂进入注定终结的意识与活力所产生的结果。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is most difficult at this time to even hint at the myriad complexity and dimension of the electrical actuality as it exists, when you consider that each of your own thoughts is composed of a unique intensity of impulse, shared by nothing else, and that the same may be said for every dream that you will have in your lifetime; and that all your experience is gathered together in particular ranges of intensity, again completely unique, codified; and that the summation of all that you are exists in one minute range or band of intensities, then you will see how difficult it is. For all human beings are likewise so electrically composed, and everything else, with few exceptions within your physical field, whether or not it even exists as physical matter. Yet I tell you that this not only applies to your physical field but to all fields.
quotes.translation: 此刻要暗示电性实相那令人难以置信的复杂性与维度，即便只是稍作提示也极为困难——当你想到自己的每个念头都由独一无二的冲动强度构成，绝无重复；你一生中每个梦境亦是如此；所有经验都汇聚于特定强度范围，同样完全独特且被编码；而你所是的全部总和存在于一个微小的强度范围或波段中——你便会明白这有多难。因为所有人类同样以这种电性方式构成，其他一切事物（你物质场域中少数例外）无论是否以物质形态存在，皆如此。但我告诉你，这不仅适用于你的物质场域，更适用于所有场域。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: There are many other natural and spontaneous kinds of comprehension that can also result from the waking and sleeping rhythms that I have suggested. The unconscious, the color black, and death all have strongly negative connotations in which the inner self is feared; the dream state is mistrusted and often suggests thoughts of both death and/or evil. But changed wake-sleep habits can, again, bring about a transformation in which it is obvious that dreams contain great wisdom and creativity, that the unconscious is indeed quite conscious, and that in fact the individual sense of identity can be retained in the dream state. The fear of self-annihilation, symbolically thought of as death, can then no longer apply as it did before.
quotes.translation: 我所建议的清醒与睡眠节奏，还能带来许多其他自然自发的理解方式。无意识、黑色与死亡都带有强烈的负面含义，内在自体因此被恐惧；梦境状态被怀疑，常令人联想到死亡或邪恶。但改变醒睡习惯，同样能带来转变——届时将显而易见：梦境蕴含伟大智慧与创造力，无意识其实相当有意识，而个体身份感在梦境中也能得以保留。对自我湮灭的恐惧（象征性地被视为死亡）便不再如从前那般适用。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You make your own reality. So, programmed ahead of time, they perceived [data] according to the psychic conventions that had been established. There are tigers in Asia, but you can travel through Asia and if you do not want to you'll never see a tiger. It's according to where you go. In the unknown reality your thoughts are instantly made apparent and real, materialized according to your beliefs. There, if you believe in demons, you will see them -- without ever realizing that they are part of the environment of your psyche, formed by your beliefs, and thrown out as mirages over a very real environment that you do not perceive. You will believe the psychic tour books and go hunting for demons instead of tigers.
quotes.translation: 你创造自己的实相。因此，预先设定好程序后，他们便根据已建立的心灵惯例来感知[数据]。亚洲有老虎，但你游历亚洲时若不想见，便永远见不到老虎——这取决于你往何处去。在未知实相中，你的念头会瞬间显现为真实，根据信念物质化。在那里，你若相信恶魔，便会看见它们——却从未意识到它们是你心灵环境的一部分，由你的信念形成，如海市蜃楼般投射在你未能感知的真实环境之上。你会相信心灵旅行指南，转而猎寻恶魔而非老虎。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (There were clear-cut connections between her creative performance today and her reception of the outline for The Way Toward Health last March; see the notes at 10:45, as well as Note 8, for Session 713; and in Volume 1, see Appendix 7. But there was even more creative expression to come through Jane this evening, not only in the session itself of course, but after it, as I try to explain in the concluding notes.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The mind is always with you. The particular brain is the physical mechanism that translates the thoughts of the mind. It goes without saying then, that the brain belongs to the physical system, and here in clear terms you see the smooth cooperation that exists. Thoughts are initially psych electric patterns in pure form, productions of the inner self that must be translated in order to be used by the physical self.
quotes.translation: 心智始终与你同在。特定的脑是翻译心智念头的物质机制。不言而喻，脑属于物质系统，在此你清晰看到存在的顺畅协作。念头最初是纯粹形式的电性心理模式，是内在自体的产物，必须经过翻译才能被物质自体使用。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: That inner, all-pervasive existence becomes known to the extent that you grow more responsive to your own inner environment. This does not mean that you become entirely self-centered, blind to the rest of the world. It does not mean that you must meditate for hours, or study your own thought processes with such vigor that you ignore other activities. It simply means that you are aware of your own life as clearly as possible -- in touch with your thought processes, aware of them but without overdue concern or overanalysis. They are as much a part of your inner environment as trees are of your exterior world. There are different species of selves in the same fashion. There are different species of worlds.
quotes.translation: 当你对自己内在环境愈发敏感时，那内在、遍及一切的存在便逐渐显现。这并不意味着你变得完全以自我为中心，对世界其余部分视而不见；也不意味着你必须冥想数小时，或如此狂热地研究自己的思维过程以致忽略其他活动。它仅仅意味着你尽可能清晰地觉察自己的生命——与思维过程保持联结，觉察它们却不过度担忧或过度分析。它们是你内在环境的一部分，正如树木是你外在世界的一部分。同样，存在不同种类的自体，也存在不同种类的世界。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The fetus dreams. As its physical growth takes place in the womb, so the shaping of its consciousness is also extended by genetic dreams. These particular fetus-oriented dreams are most difficult to describe, for they are actually involved with forming the contours of the individual consciousness. Such dreams provide (pause) the subjective understanding from which thoughts are developed, and in those terms complete thoughts are possible before the brain itself is fully formed. It is the process of thinking that helps bring the brain into activity, and not the other way around (all quite intently).
quotes.translation: 胎儿做梦。当它在子宫中经历物质成长时，其意识的塑造也通过基因梦境得以延伸。这些特定于胎儿的梦境极难描述，因为它们实际上涉及个体意识轮廓的形成。这类梦境提供（停顿）发展念头所需的主观理解，就此而言，在脑本身完全形成之前，完整的念头便已可能。正是思考过程帮助激活脑，而非相反（语气相当专注）。

source_raw.title: Here in Dreams Seth uses the uncertainty principle as an analogy (and an excellent one), meaning that as the positions and motions of elementary particles, say, cannot be simultaneously measured precisely, so our genetic qualities and their motions can not always be specifically determined. In Dreams he's already said (in Session 909) that the human species has an "amazing interplay between genetic preciseness and genetic freedom," and (in Session 910) that "your genetic structure reacts to each thought that you have, to the state of your emotions, to your psychological climate." Choices and probabilities apply. Thus do we avoid genetic rigidity.

quotes.text: I have often stated that the mind-body relationship is one system. The thoughts are as necessary to the whole system as the body's cells are. Ruburt correctly interpreted an analogy I gave him in which I compared thoughts to individual cells, and belief systems to the physical organs, which are composed of cells. The organs obviously are stationary in the body, though the cells within them die and are reborn.
quotes.translation: 我常说过，心智与身体的关系是一个系统。思想对整个系统的必要性，就像身体的细胞一样。鲁柏正确解读了我给他的一个比喻：我将思想比作个体细胞，信念系统比作由细胞组成的身体器官。器官在身体中显然是固定的，尽管其中的细胞不断死亡与重生。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: "I could list hundreds of examples of what I mean. This is one of those obvious ideas that seem childish once it's thought of. I don't care whether or not it's a profound thought; it has meaning for me. But as far as I know, we humans are the only species that's obsessed with 'change', and 'progress', and 'controlling or mastering nature'; with learning about our past and with charting our future. We strive toward an impossible, or at least rosy, future in which we will have met all of our challenges, so that we'll live in some sort of unreal wonderland on earth. What do we do next -- or will we give up on that idea too? Perhaps we'll spend all of our time contemplating each other!
quotes.translation: "我可以举出数百个例子来说明我的意思。这是那些显而易见的想法之一，一旦想到就显得幼稚。我不在乎它是否深刻；它对我有意义。但据我所知，我们人类是唯一痴迷于'改变'、'进步'、'控制或征服自然'的物种；痴迷于了解过去和规划未来。我们努力追求一个不可能实现、或至少是美好的未来，在那里我们将克服所有挑战，从而生活在地球上某种不真实的仙境中。接下来我们做什么——还是连这个想法也会放弃？也许我们会把所有时间都用来互相凝视！
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth speaks in Session 826, held for Mass Events on March 8, 1978:

quotes.text: An examination of your conscious thoughts will tell you much about the state of your inner mind, your intentions and expectations, and will often lead you to a direct confrontation with challenges and problems. Your thoughts, studied, will let you see where you are going. They point clearly to the nature of physical events. What exists physically exists first in thought and feeling. There is no other rule.
quotes.translation: 检视你有意识的思想，会告诉你许多关于你内在心智、意图和期望的状态，并常常引导你直接面对挑战与问题。研究你的思想，会让你看清自己的方向。它们清楚地指向物质事件的性质。物质存在的事物，首先存在于思想与情感中。没有其他规则。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Ruburt, having interpreted your dream, looked wide-awake but relaxed through his studio into the kitchen. He thought of asking you to take a snapshot of the table with your camera, showing the partially-opened front door, so that later he could paint the scene. Your camera could not take in all of that, a fact he never thought of. Less than two minutes later, you came out into his studio with the camera that you had not used for months. Ruburt had also been thinking newly about the magical approach from ideas in your own notes2 that he had just read. You came out as if in answer. As if to say, "Yes, the magical approach does indeed operate, and this is how."
quotes.translation: 鲁柏在解读了你的梦境后，睁着眼睛却放松地透过工作室望向厨房。他想到请你用相机拍下那张桌子，展示半开的前门，这样他以后可以画下这个场景。你的相机无法拍下全部，这个事实他从未想过。不到两分钟后，你拿着几个月没用的相机走进了他的工作室。鲁柏当时也正从你刚读过的笔记中的想法，重新思考魔法之道。你走出来，仿佛是在回应。仿佛在说："是的，魔法之道确实在运作，就是这样。"
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth speaks in Session 826, held for Mass Events on March 8, 1978:

quotes.text: Prime identities cannot be duplicated. Duplication, exact duplication, is always merely an effect of insufficient knowledge. In some cases two thoughts may indeed appear identical, but whether or not examination can show it, such exact duplication is impossible. Now. When receiver B receives this transmitted thought, he may react and interpret that part of the thought that is similar to the original.
quotes.translation: 主要身份无法被复制。复制，精确复制，始终只是知识不足的结果。在某些情况下，两个思想可能看起来完全相同，但无论检视能否证明，这种精确复制是不可能的。现在。当接收者B接收到这个被传递的思想时，他可能会反应并解读那部分与原思想相似的内容。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The origin is not in others. For fifteen minutes a day agree to suspend critical judgment, and following the Maltz method imagine yourself vividly in the position you would like to be. The rest of the time consciously make an effort to control your attitude when you find yourself thinking in terms of want or poverty. Then switch your thoughts to ideas of plenty. This need not involve hypocrisy, indeed must not. You must feel that this is legitimate and practical, because the thoughts of plenty will automatically begin to attract plenty. This is sufficient. There should not be an overemphasis (underlined) on material possessions or security however.
quotes.translation: 源头不在他人身上。每天花十五分钟暂停批判性判断，按照马尔茨方法，生动地想象自己处于理想的位置。其余时间，当你发现自己以匮乏或贫穷的念头思考时，有意识地努力控制你的态度。然后将你的思想转向丰盛的念头。这不必涉及虚伪，确实不能。你必须感到这是合理且实际的，因为丰盛的念头会自动开始吸引丰盛。这足够了。然而，不应过度强调物质财产或安全感。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: This is difficult to put into words. There is no reason, again, why you can't achieve out-of-body proficiency, and when you do, you will be able to experiment with thought creations, trying out, forming, using or discarding, thought paintings. In an out-of-body state you can reach rather easily those environments which thoughts become a sort of plastic pseudo material, almost instantaneously.
quotes.translation: 这很难用言语表达。再说一次，你没有理由无法达成出体熟练度，而当你做到时，你将能实验思想创造，尝试、形成、使用或舍弃思想画作。在出体状态中，你可以相当容易地触及那些思想会变成一种近乎塑胶的伪物质的环境，几乎是瞬间完成。

quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: When the thought is thought, it is, say, broken down again to those components. Your thoughts have an emotional basis, also. The smallest c-e-l-l (spelled) within your body contributes to that emotional reality, and reacts instantly to your thoughts.
quotes.translation: 当思想被思考时，它可以说又被分解成那些成分。你的思想也有情感基础。你体内最小的细胞（拼写为c-e-l-l）也贡献于那个情感实相，并立即对你的思想做出反应。
