source_raw.title:
SESSION 841, MARCH 14, 1979
9:08 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: The initial thought or image exists within the mental enclosure of which I have spoken. It is not yet made physical. It appears within the physical system, but apart from it. Then it is sparked into physical materiaizationl. This is the general procedure. All thoughts or images are not completely materialized, however. The intensity may be too weak. There is a struggle of sorts among ideas for expression. The chemical reaction sparks certain electrical charges, some within the layers of the skin. There are radiations then through the skin to the exterior worlds containing highly codified information and instructions.
quotes.translation: 初始的思想或影像存在于我曾说过的心理围场中。它尚未物质化。它出现在物质系统内，却又与之分离。然后它被激发成物质具体化。这是普遍的程序。然而，并非所有思想或影像都完全物质化。强度可能太弱。各种观念之间存在着某种争夺表达的挣扎。化学反应会激发某些电荷，有些在皮肤层内。然后通过皮肤向外在世界辐射，包含高度编码的信息和指令。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: You must realize, and tell yourself, that creativity is timeless in a basic manner. You must completely cease inner speculations and regrets and questions as to why your abilities have not come to fruition. This must positively be done. The negative suggestion works constantly against you. When such thoughts come to mind, instead tell yourself that that trend of thought will not help your painting, but hinder it, and that it is constricting. Then immediately imagine a time when you painted very well and spontaneously, and tell yourself that you are now free to use and develop these abilities. (Pause.)
quotes.translation: 你必须明白，并告诉自己，创造力在根本上是无时间性的。你必须完全停止内在的揣测、悔恨和质疑，为什么你的能力尚未实现。这必须坚决做到。负面暗示持续与你作对。当这类念头出现时，反而告诉自己，那种思路不会帮助你的画作，反而会阻碍它，而且它是束缚性的。然后立刻想象你曾画得非常好且自发的一个时刻，并告诉自己你现在可以自由地使用和发展这些能力。（停顿）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The effect of any thought is a quite precise and definite one, that is set into motion specifically because of the nature of its own electromagnetic identity. The physical system operates best within certain electromagnetic patterns, and is adversely affected by others. These effects change the actual molecular structure of the physical cells of the organism, for better or for worse, and because of certain laws of attraction a habitual pattern will operate. A destructive type of thought, then, is dangerous not only for the present state of the organism, but dangerous in terms of the future.
quotes.translation: 任何思想的效果都是相当精确而确定的，它之所以被启动，正是由于其自身电磁身份的本质。物质系统在特定的电磁模式中运作最佳，而受到其他模式的负面影响。这些影响会改变有机体物质细胞的分子结构，不论好坏，而且由于某些吸引法则，习惯性模式会运作。因此，破坏性的思想不仅对有机体的当前状态危险，对未来也危险。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: You cannot escape your own attitudes, for they will form the nature of what you see. Quite literally, you see what you want to see, and you see your own thoughts, your own Emotional attitudes, materialized in physical form. If changes are to occur, they must be physical and psychic changes. These will be reflected in your physical environment. Negative, distrustful, fearful, or degrading attitudes toward anyone work against the self and against the individuals involved. Now if you would change an individual, change your thoughts toward him, and changes will appear in the Sense data world.
quotes.translation: 你无法逃避自己的态度，因为它们会形成你所见之物的本质。毫不夸张地说，你看到你想看到的，你看到自己的思想、自己的情感态度，以物质形式具体化。如果要发生改变，它们必须是物质和心灵上的改变。这些改变会反映在你的物质环境中。对任何人抱持负面、不信任、恐惧或贬低的态度，都会伤害自己和相关个体。现在，如果你想改变一个人，就改变你对他的想法，改变就会出现在感官资料的世界中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Because you identify your experience with the regular line of consciousness with which you are familiar, you are rarely able to "bring in" any "other-self" material and hold it while retaining your own sense of identity. Such material may at times bleed or intrude into your own thought, where it blends and is not recognized. In such cases, it takes on the coloration of your own thought patterns. It adds to the overall atmosphere of your being. Without understanding or training, you would have to "lose" your own consciousness in order to perceive the "other-consciousness."
quotes.translation: 因为你将自己的经验与你所熟悉的常规意识线索等同起来，你很少能在保持自我身份感的同时"引入"任何"其他自我"的资料并加以维持。这类资料有时会渗入或侵入你的思想，在其中混合而不被识别。在这种情况下，它会染上你自身思想模式的色彩，为你存在的整体氛围增添内容。没有理解或训练，你必须"失去"自己的意识才能感知"其他意识"。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 692
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Thoughts, as you know, contain their own electromagnetic reality. This means that each thought is an electromagnetic system in fact and not just in theory. Such a system is the basis for your physical system and these are highly valid. Theoretically only, with drugs it would be possible to be lost within an idea, caught in its electromagnetic reality, and forced to follow the ramifications and developments of the idea in various guises. The value fulfillment system would be changed. There would be development within that system, but you would be lost to your own system.
quotes.translation: 思想，如你所知，包含其自身的电磁实相。这意味着每个思想实际上都是一个电磁系统，而不仅仅是理论上的。这样的系统是你物质系统的基础，并且这些系统高度有效。仅在理论上，通过药物，有可能迷失在一个念头里，被困在其电磁实相中，被迫以各种伪装追随该念头的分支和发展。价值完成系统会被改变。在那个系统内会有发展，但你会迷失于你自己的系统。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: As creatures you are a part of nature. The change of thoughts, feelings and beliefs into physical, objectively perceived phenomena is as natural as water changing into ice, for example, or a caterpillar turning into a butterfly. You not only form the structure of your civilizations and social institutions through the transference of beliefs, thoughts and feelings; but in this natural exchange you also help on quite intimate levels in the "psychic manufacture" of the physical environment itself, with all of its great sweeping variety, and yet seasonal stability.
quotes.translation: 作为生物，你们是自然的一部分。思想、情感和信念转化为物质性的、客观感知的现象，就像水变成冰，或毛毛虫变成蝴蝶一样自然。你们不仅通过信念、思想和情感的转移来构建文明和社会制度的结构；在这种自然的交换中，你们还在相当亲密的层面上协助了物质环境本身的"心灵制造"，包括其所有丰富多彩的变化，以及季节性的稳定。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: He heard tomorrow's weather report (yesterday), groaned, thought of a very uncomfortable 90-degree temperature tomorrow [and] imagined himself miserable with the heat. Indeed, he began to feel warmer. In a flash he remembered previous days of discomfort, and in the next moment he projected those into the weekend. He felt trapped. Midway through this process he tried to catch himself, but he believed that his body could not handle the heat -- and that belief outweighed his intent to change his thoughts, so they kept returning for perhaps ten minutes.
quotes.translation: 他（昨天）听到了明天的天气预报，呻吟着，想到明天令人非常不舒服的90华氏度温度，想象自己在炎热中痛苦不堪。确实，他开始觉得更热了。一瞬间他记起了以前那些不适的日子，下一刻他又把这些投射到周末。他感到被困住了。在这个过程中途，他试图抓住自己，但他相信自己的身体无法承受炎热——这个信念压倒了他改变思想的意图，所以这些念头持续回来了大约十分钟。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: Objectively -- whatever the appearances -- storms, earthquakes, floods, et cetera, are quite necessary to the well-being of the earth. Both man's and nature's purposes are served, then, though generally speaking man's myths make him blind to those interactions. People's thoughts and emotions always give clear clues whenever illness is involved, yet most people ignore such information. They censor their own thoughts. Many therefore "fall prey" to epidemics of one kind or another because they want to, though they might deny this quite vigorously.
quotes.translation: 客观而言——无论表象如何——风暴、地震、洪水等，对地球的福祉是相当必要的。因此，人类和自然的目的都得到了服务，尽管一般来说，人类的神话使他们对这些互动视而不见。每当涉及疾病时，人们的思想和情绪总是提供清晰的线索，但大多数人忽视这类信息。他们审查自己的思想。因此，许多人"沦为"各种流行病的"牺牲品"，因为他们想要如此，尽管他们可能会激烈否认这一点。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 821, FEBRUARY 20, 1978
9:30 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Now. I have told you that emotions also possess an electrical reality. Thoughts formed and sent out within the impulse range of emotion often succeed because of the peculiar nature of emotional electrical impulses themselves. They have a particularly strong electrical mass. They also usually fall within powerful intensities. For reasons that we will not discuss, this evening, thoughts formed under a strong emotional impetus will carry greater vividness, have a greater tendency toward duplication, and are apt to be interpreted with some success.
quotes.translation: 现在。我告诉过你们，情绪也拥有电性实相。在情绪的冲动范围内形成并发送出去的思想常常成功，因为情绪电性冲动本身的特性。它们具有特别强大的电性质量。它们通常也落在强大的强度范围内。出于我们今晚不讨论的原因，在强烈情绪推动下形成的思想会携带更大的生动性，有更强的复制倾向，并且容易被成功解读。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: (Still to Warren:) So forget all of the histories, my dear friend, and listen to your own thoughts, which are today as alive and vital as those of any man ever born, in whatever time. Forget the dusty old records and feel your reality in the moment as you are. In that moment can you hear the insects sweeping across the continents and the voices of the leaves speak, and feel their echoes in your blood -- and that blood lives, beyond the time. It throbs beyond destiny, even as the masses of those people live beyond the beliefs of those gurus.
quotes.translation: （仍对沃伦说：）所以忘掉所有历史吧，我亲爱的朋友，倾听你自己的思想——这些思想在今天与任何时代任何人的思想一样鲜活有力。忘掉那些尘封的古老记录，感受你此刻当下的实相。在那一刻，你能听见昆虫掠过大陆的声音，听见树叶的言语，并在你的血液中感受它们的回响——而这血液，超越时间而存在。它超越命运而搏动，正如那些民众的生命超越上师们的信仰而存在。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (FOR SESSION 724)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: This will show you how the thoughts brought about the physical malady, and which ones were involved. The same thing should then be done with the feeling pattern. This may be perceived as bursts of dark or light colors in motion, or simply one particular emotion of great force may be felt. If it is very strong, one emotion may be felt in many such guises. In the case of both thoughts and emotions, with great confidence you pluck out those that are connected with the malady. In such a manner you have made adjustments on three levels.
quotes.translation: 这将向你展示思想如何引发身体疾病，以及哪些思想牵涉其中。对感受模式也应采取同样的方法。这可能会被感知为运动中明暗色彩的迸发，也可能只是感受到某种极其强烈的单一情绪。若情绪非常强烈，一种情绪可能以多种伪装被感知。在思想和情绪两种情况下，你要满怀信心地摘除那些与疾病相关的部分。通过这种方式，你已在三个层面上做出了调整。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: All of these systems are intimately connected and delicately balanced, and an alteration in one sets up an alteration in each of the others. There is no escaping this interrelationship. A mans thoughts, then, are far more reaching than he knows. They exist in more dimensions, they affect worlds of which he is unaware. They are as concrete, in effect, as any building. Thoughts then appear in many guises within many systems, and once created cannot be withdrawn, and once set into motion cannot be stopped. The same applies for dreams.
quotes.translation: 所有这些系统都紧密相连且微妙平衡，一个系统的改变会引发其他每个系统的改变。这种相互关系无可逃避。因此，一个人的思想远比他所知的更为深远。它们存在于更多维度中，影响着他未曾察觉的世界。实际上，它们与任何建筑物一样具体。思想随后以多种伪装出现在许多系统中，一旦被创造就无法收回，一旦被启动就无法停止。梦境也是如此。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: An outright lie may or may not be a violation. A sex act may or may not be a violation. A scientific expedition may or may not be a violation. Not going to church on Sunday is not a violation. Having normal aggressive thoughts is not a violation. Doing violence to your body, or another's, is a violation. Doing violence to the spirit of another is a violation -- but again, because you are conscious beings the interpretations are yours. Swearing is not a violation. If you believe that it is then in your mind it becomes one.
quotes.translation: 公然的谎言可能是也可能不是一种侵犯。性行为可能是也可能不是一种侵犯。科学考察可能是也可能不是一种侵犯。周日不去教堂不是一种侵犯。拥有正常的攻击性思想不是一种侵犯。对你的身体或他人的身体施加暴力是一种侵犯。对他人的精神施加暴力是一种侵犯——但再次强调，因为你们是有意识的存有，所以诠释权在你们手中。咒骂不是一种侵犯。如果你相信它是，那么在你的心智中它就变成了侵犯。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The dream experience is felt directly by the inner self. Dreams have an electric actuality, as I have told you. In this electrical actuality they then exist independently of the dreamer, although he still applies the dream to himself. In the same manner do thoughts and emotions have the electric reality of which I have spoken. Therefore, within the electric system, dreams, thoughts and emotions exist as actualities, and in what you may call a tangible form, though not in the form of matter as you are familiar with it.
quotes.translation: 内在自体直接感受梦境体验。如我曾告诉你们的，梦境具有电性实相。在这种电性实相中，它们独立于做梦者而存在，尽管做梦者仍将梦境应用于自身。同样地，思想和情绪也具有我曾提及的电性实相。因此，在电性系统内，梦境、思想和情绪作为实相存在，并以你们可称为有形体的形式存在，尽管并非你们所熟悉的物质形态。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: （This cancellation raised many questions, since Seth had said nothing about any such event; on the other hand, his forecasts had all been prefaced by the word "if," or similar ones. We thought briefly of asking for a special session to deal with the rather surprising turn of events, but decided against it, not wanting to cultivate any such dependent habits. My personal thought was that our expectations concerning the house had been in the process of change, the loss of the loan being the natural culmination of this.
quotes.translation: （这次取消引发了许多疑问，因为赛斯从未提及任何此类事件；另一方面，他的所有预测都以"如果"或类似词语作为前提。我们短暂考虑过要求进行一次特别课来讨论这个相当出人意料的事件转折，但最终决定放弃，不想养成任何此类依赖习惯。我个人的想法是，我们对那栋房子的期待一直在变化中，贷款的损失正是这种变化的自然结果。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Each thought or emotion therefore exists as an electromagnetic energy unit or as a combination of these under certain conditions, and often with the help of coordinate points, they emerge into the building blocks of physical matter. This emergence into matter occurs as a neutral "result" regardless of the nature of any given thought or emotion. Mental images, accompanied by strong emotion, are blueprints therefore upon which a corresponding physical object, or condition or event, will in your terms appear.
quotes.translation: 因此，每个思想或情感在特定条件下，或作为电磁能量单位，或作为这些单位的组合而存在，并常常借助坐标点，它们融入物质实相的构建模块中。这种融入物质的过程，无论任何思想或情感的性质如何，都作为一个中性的“结果”发生。伴随强烈情感的心理意象，因此成为蓝图，在此基础上，一个相应的物质对象、状态或事件，在你们的说法中，将会出现。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Thoughts are indeed events therefore. Wishes and desires are events. As you know, wishes and desires also influence those actions which will be perceived by you as actual events in the physical universe. The human system responds fully as much to these events as it does to physical events. In the dream state often portions of these probable events are experienced in a semiconscious manner. This amounts to a bleed-through, and I use the term purposely, for your recorder can serve us here in an analogy.
quotes.translation: 因此，思想确实是事件。愿望和渴望是事件。如你所知，愿望和渴望也影响那些将被你感知为物质宇宙中实际事件的行为。人类系统对这些事件的反应，与对物质事件的反应同样充分。在梦境状态中，这些可能事件的某些部分常常以半意识的方式被体验。这相当于一种渗透，我特意使用这个术语，因为你们的记录员可以在此处用类比为我们服务。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Now. I have just told you that one thought must terminate before another thought can appear. Although this might sound as if I am speaking in terms of continuity, I am not. The action of our imaginary sphere upon and within itself is simultaneous, and in all directions. All actions occur basically within the spacious present, but all action cannot be aware of itself except as it attempts further action, i.e., materializations.
quotes.translation: 现在。我刚才告诉过你，一个思想必须终止，另一个思想才能出现。虽然这听起来好像我在谈论连续性，但我并非如此。我们想象的球体在其自身内部和之上的行动是同时的，并且向所有方向进行。所有行动基本上都发生在广阔当下之内，但所有行动只有在试图进一步行动（即物质化）时，才能意识到自身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You consciously give the signals for reaction. It is not the other way around. Past events do not intrude in this manner unless they are beckoned by the conscious expectations and thoughts that exist within your mind. (Pause.) Those unconscious memories will be activated according to your current beliefs. You will be replenished and renewed as your thoughts motivate joyful body sensations and physical events, or you will be depressed as you bring into your awareness unpleasant past body happenings.
quotes.translation: 你有意识地发出反应的信号。情况并非相反。过去的事件不会以这种方式侵入，除非它们被你心智中存在的有意识的期望和思想所召唤。（停顿。）那些无意识的记忆将根据你当前的信念被激活。当你的思想激发愉悦的身体感觉和物质事件时，你将得到补充和更新；或者，当你将不愉快的过去身体经历带入你的意识时，你将感到沮丧。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: If its built-in instincts are left alone the body is basically self-regulating. It does not kill off all red blood cells if there are too many of them at a given time. It has better sense. But in your fear of negative thoughts you often attempt to deny all normal aggressiveness, and at the first glimpse of it bring up your mental antibodies prepared for action. In so doing you try to repudiate the validity of your own experience. If you do not feel your individual reality, then you can never realize that you form it, and so can change it. It is this denial of experience, and the energy blockages involved, that build up the accumulation of unnecessary "unnatural" guilt. The body itself cannot understand these blocked messages, and cries out to express its own corporeal knowledge of the moment as it experiences it. (Intently:) You mentally shout in such situations that you do not feel what you feel.
quotes.translation: 如果其内置的本能得到放任，身体基本上是自我调节的。如果在某个特定时间红细胞过多，它不会杀死所有红细胞。它更有智慧。但在你对负面思想的恐惧中，你常常试图否认所有正常的攻击性，并在其初现端倪时就调动你准备好的精神抗体。这样做时，你试图否认自己经验的有效性。如果你感受不到你个人的实相，那么你永远无法认识到你形成了它，因此可以改变它。正是这种对经验的否认，以及所涉及的能量阻塞，累积了不必要的“非自然”内疚。身体本身无法理解这些被阻塞的信息，并在体验当下时，呼喊出它自己对该时刻的肉体知识。（专注地：）在这种情况下，你在精神上大喊，你感受不到你所感受的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You must realize that Hitler believed that any atrocity was justified in the light of what he thought of as the greater good. To some extent or another, many of the ideals he held and advocated had long been accepted in world communities, though they had not been acted upon with such dispatch. The nations of the world saw their own worst tendencies personified in Hitler's Germany, ready to attack them. The Jews, for various reasons -- and again, this is not the full story -- the Jews acted as all of the victims of the world, both the Germans and the Jews basically agreeing upon "man's nefarious nature." For the first time the modern world realized its vulnerability to political events, and technology and communication accelerated all of war's dangers. Hitler brought many of man's most infamous tendencies to the surface. For the first time, again, the species understood that might alone did not mean right, and that in larger terms a world war could have no real victors. Hitler might well have exploded the world's first atomic bomb.
quotes.translation: 你必须认识到，希特勒相信，任何暴行在他所认为的更大善的光照下都是正当的。在某种程度上，他持有和倡导的许多理想早已被世界社会所接受，尽管它们没有以如此迅速的方式被付诸行动。世界各国在希特勒的德国身上看到了自己最糟糕的倾向，准备攻击它们。犹太人，出于各种原因——再说一次，这不是全部故事——犹太人充当了世界上所有受害者的角色，德国人和犹太人基本上都同意“人类邪恶的本性”。现代世界第一次意识到自己对政治事件的脆弱性，而技术和通讯加速了战争的所有危险。希特勒将人类许多最臭名昭著的倾向带到了表面。同样，物种第一次明白，力量本身并不意味着正义，从更大的角度来看，世界大战不可能有真正的胜利者。希特勒很可能已经引爆了世界第一颗原子弹。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 852, MAY 9, 1979
9:39 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Such personalities will concentrate their main activities in such a way that they can best affect conceptual thought, and bring about necessary changes. Many large families are representative of this group. They appear and reappear in your histories, always as dominant and significant entities. Here the family represents an entity who has taken upon itself the nurturing of your civilization. Knowing intuitively those changes which must come about in your future, they try to bring them about by appearing in your past; though to them your future and the past are the same they must still work within probabilities, you see, and what they are doing is affecting probabilities within your system. Even your time is basically plastic, though it does not seem so to you.
quotes.translation: 这类人格会集中其主要活动，以最能影响概念性思维并带来必要改变的方式运作。许多大家族正是这一群体的代表。他们在你们的历史中反复出现，始终作为主导性的重要实体。在此，家族代表着一个承担起培育你们文明使命的实体。他们凭直觉知晓你们未来必须发生的改变，便试图通过出现在你们的过去来促成这些改变；尽管对他们而言，你们的未来与过去并无区别，但他们仍须在概率中运作，而他们所做的正是影响你们系统内的概率。即便你们的时间本质上具有可塑性，尽管在你们看来并非如此。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Your thoughts are as natural as the cells within your body, and as real. They interact with one another as viruses do. While you are in this reality there is no division between the mental, the spiritual, and the physical. If you think there is, then you do not sufficiently understand the spirituality of the flesh or the physical reality of your thought.
quotes.translation: 你的思想如同你体内的细胞一样自然，也如细胞般真实。它们像病毒一样彼此互动。当你身处这个实相时，心智、灵性与物质之间并无分野。若你认为存在分野，那便是你尚未充分理解肉体的灵性本质，或你思想的物质实相。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: So does the personality, through negative charges, cause boundaries to be set about its own emotional system. It will accept most readily those thoughts and emotions, or electric actions, which attract it, which have a similarity to its own charged emotional climate; and it will have greater resistance to those which have the greatest dissimilarities. It is, therefore, most advantageous to study well those thoughts and emotions which are habitually accepted by any given personality.
quotes.translation: 同样地，人格会通过负面电荷为其自身情感系统划定边界。它最易接纳那些吸引它的、与其自身带电情感氛围相似的思想、情感或电性活动；而对那些差异最大的思想情感则会产生更大抗拒。因此，仔细研究任何特定人格习惯性接纳的思想与情感，将极为有益。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The "negative" subjective and objective events that you meet are meant to make you examine the contents of your own conscious mind. In their way the hateful or revengeful thoughts are natural therapeutic devices, for if you follow them, accepting them with their own validity as feelings, they will automatically lead you beyond themselves; they will change into other feelings, carrying you from hatred into what may seem to be the quicksands of fear -- which is always behind hatred.
quotes.translation: 你所遭遇的"负面"主观与客观事件，旨在促使你检视自身意识心智的内容。就其本质而言，憎恨或报复性的思想是自然的疗愈工具——若你跟随它们，承认它们作为感受的正当性，它们会自动引领你超越自身；它们会转化为其他感受，将你从憎恨带入看似恐惧的流沙地带——而恐惧始终潜藏于憎恨背后。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Your technology is one of the results of that reasoning mind. That "reasoning" is necessary, however, because of the lack of a larger, immediate field of knowledge. Thoughts are mental activity, scaled to time and space terms so that they are like mental edifices built to certain dimensions only. Your thoughts make you human (underlined).
quotes.translation: 你们的科技正是理性思维的产物之一。然而，这种"理性"之所以必要，是因为缺乏更广阔、更直接的知识场域。思想是心智活动，被缩放至时间与空间的维度，如同仅按特定尺寸建造的心智建筑。你的思想使你成为人（原文下划线标注）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: While Jane has been enjoying many periods of ease and relaxation, she's also had bouts of blueness over her general stiffness and walking difficulties [her "symptoms"]. But she's finally dispensed with the last stages of her cold, which had turned out to be much heavier than mine, and is feeling a resurgence of energy for the sessions. Her own work has been going well for the most part, however--she expects to finish Chapter 12 of God of Jane tomorrow. Because of her concentration on that hook she hasn't done much on her book of poetry, If We Live Again, since late February; and as I mentioned in the Preface for Dreams, she laid aside her third Seven novel, Oversoul Seven and the Museum of Time, in May 1979 when she began God of Jane. As for myself, I've progressed to working with Session 860 [for June 13, 1979], which bridges chapters 8 and 9 in Mass Events.

quotes.text: Various emotional factors within each personality cause what we may call a characteristic emotional climate. Certain types of thoughts and emotions are entertained therein. Since these are electrical actions, they set up strong electrical fields of attraction within the personality, easy electric paths. Uncharacteristic thoughts or emotions must then meet with some resistance. Habit, then, has not only a psychological, but an electrical and psychological nature and implication.
quotes.translation: 每个人格内部的各种情感因素，构成了我们可称之为"特征性情感氛围"的东西。特定类型的思想与情感在此氛围中被接纳。由于这些是电性活动，它们会在人格内部建立强大的电性吸引场，形成顺畅的电性路径。非特征性的思想或情感则必然遭遇某种阻力。因此，习惯不仅具有心理层面的本质与意涵，更兼具电性与心理的双重本质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You may perceive the thought patterns as quickly flashing sentences or words that are usually seen within your mind or within the other mind, or as black letters that form words. Or you may hear the words and thoughts being expressed, or you may see the earlier mentioned "landscape" in which the thoughts symbolically form into a picture.
quotes.translation: 你可能会将思想模式感知为快速闪过的句子或词语，这些通常出现在你或他人的心智中，也可能表现为组成词语的黑色字母。或者你可能听到被表达出来的词语和思想，也可能看到前面提到的"景观"，其中思想象征性地形成一幅画面。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Some of these may involve the thoughts of what you would call a reincarnational self, focused in another period of history as you know it. You may instead, "pick up" an event in which a probable self is involved, according to your inclination, your psychic suppleness, your curiosity, your desire for knowledge. In other words, you may become aware of a far greater reality than you now know, use abilities that you do not realize you possess, know beyond all doubt that your own consciousness and identity is independent of the world in which you now focus your primary attention. If all of that were not true, I would not be writing this book and you would not be reading it.
quotes.translation: 其中一些可能涉及你所谓的转世自我的思想，聚焦在你所知的另一段历史时期。根据你的倾向、心灵柔韧度、好奇心和对知识的渴望，你反而可能"捕捉"到涉及可能自我的事件。换句话说，你可能会意识到比你现在所知更广阔的现实，运用你尚未意识到自己拥有的能力，确凿无疑地知道自己的意识和身份独立于你目前主要关注的世界。如果这一切不是真的，我就不会写这本书，你也不会读它。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You understand the basic reality of subjective life. You will then still organize experience (underline organize), but you will not need artificial aids such as time to lean upon. You will not need to see thoughts materialized in physical matter, for you will have long since learned that the thoughts and not the matter are the basic reality. You will be able therefore to dispense with many seemingly permanent mass images, but when you form them you will realize why and how.
quotes.translation: 你理解主观生活的基本实相。那么你仍会组织经验（强调组织），但不再需要依赖时间这类人为辅助工具。你不再需要看到思想在物质中具体化，因为你早已明白思想而非物质才是基本实相。因此你将能够摒弃许多看似永恒的大众意象，但当你形成它们时，你会明白其缘由与方式。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: As living cells have a structure, react to stimuli and organize according to their own classification, so do thoughts. Thoughts thrive on association. They magnetically attract others like themselves, and like some strange microscopic animals they repel their "enemies," or other thoughts that are threatening to their own survival.
quotes.translation: 如同活细胞具有结构、对刺激做出反应并按其自身分类进行组织，思想也是如此。思想依靠联想而繁盛。它们磁性地吸引同类思想，像某些奇特的微生物一样排斥"敌人"，或那些威胁其自身生存的其他思想。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: We must use analogies. It can be thought of as the most exterior projection of the inner ego. When you find yourself, so to speak, watching what you think of as the ego, then you are in contact with this portion of the self. It is aware of both conscious and subconscious motivations and realities, and it is also aware of projections into other fields of actuality. I have hinted before of these matters, for when I say that you will use this other portion of the self to examine waking consciousness and probe beneath it, I already presuppose a you that uses this self. In other words, you are already magnifying the limitations of the self and extending them.
quotes.translation: 我们必须使用类比。这可以被视为内在自我的最外层投射。当你发现自己——可以说——在观察你所谓的自我时，你便与自我的这一部分建立了联系。它既意识到有意识和潜意识的动机与实相，也意识到向其他实相领域的投射。我之前已暗示过这些事，因为当我说你将运用自我的这一部分来检视清醒意识并深入探索其下时，我已经预设了一个运用此自我的"你"。换句话说，你已经在放大自我的局限并扩展它们。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: You visit with travelers from other systems. You plant thought forms in other systems, and you tend them. You hold congregation with the many segments of your own reality. Your world is like a thought form in my experience. Your physical system exists in many intensities, and can be perceived. Probable selves can be viewed by myself and others like me, for we see your reality in all of its dimensions, and you view it from the small present forms that you know. (Pause.)
quotes.translation: 你拜访来自其他系统的旅者。你在其他系统中种植思想形态并照料它们。你与自己实相的众多片段举行集会。你的世界在我体验中就像一个思想形态。你的物质系统以多种强度存在且能被感知。可能自我可以被我和像我这样的存在所观察，因为我们看到你实相的所有维度，而你则从你所知的微小当下形式中观看它。（停顿）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Psychological time adds duration. You will find something else here. From the framework of psychological time you will see that clock time is as dreamlike and fleeting as you once thought inner time was. And you will discover that inner time is as much a reality as you once thought outer time was. You will discover your whole selves in other worlds, peeping inward and outward at the same time, and finding that all time is one time, and that all divisions are illusion.
quotes.translation: 心理时间增加了持续性。你会发现另一件事。从心理时间的架构来看，你会看到钟表时间和你曾以为的内在时间一样如梦似幻、转瞬即逝。而你会发现内在时间和曾以为的外在时间一样真实。你会在其他世界发现完整的自己，同时向内向外窥探，发现所有时间都是同一时间，所有划分都是幻相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Now you do the same thing when you sit in your living room, but you do not realize what you are doing; and presently you are somewhat restricted. When my associates and I meet, we often translate each other's thoughts into various shapes and forms out of pure enjoyment in the practice. We have what you might call a game, demanding some expertise, where for our own amusement we see which of us can translate any given thought into the most numerous forms. (Pause.)
quotes.translation: 现在你坐在客厅里时也在做同样的事，只是你并未意识到自己在做什么；不久后你便受到某种限制。当我和同伴们会面时，我们常纯粹出于练习的乐趣，将彼此的思想转化为各种形状与形式。我们有一种可称为游戏的活动，需要一定技巧，为了自娱自乐，我们看谁能将某个特定思想转化为最多形式。（停顿。）
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The sound of your inner thoughts is the medium that you actually use. This is far more than an analogy, however, for in simple terms it explains quite clearly the way in which your beliefs form your reality. In quiet moments the word "O-O-O-O-O-M-M-M-M-M," said slowly, mentally or aloud, will be of benefit in toning up your general physical condition. The sounds contain within them a built-in impetus toward energy and well-being, as I will explain shortly.
quotes.translation: 你内在思想的声音是你实际使用的媒介。然而这远非比喻，因为简单来说，它相当清楚地解释了你信念如何形成你的实相。在安静时刻，缓慢地默念或出声念诵"O-O-O-O-O-M-M-M-M-M"这个字，将有助于调养你的整体身体状况。这些声音本身蕴含着朝向活力与福祉的内在动力，我稍后会解释。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Your overreliance upon physical norms, and your distorted concepts concerning survival of the fittest, help exaggerate the existence of any genetic defects, of course. Many religious dogmas consider such conditions, again, the result of a god's punishment. The survival of the species is far more dependent upon your subjective activities than your physical ones--for it is your subjective behavior that is responsible for your physical acts. Science of course looks at it the other way around, as if your physical acts are the result of a robot's mechanical, formalized behavior--a robot miraculously programmed by the blind elements of an accidental universe formed by chance. The robot is programmed only to survive at anyone's or anything's expense. It has no real consciousness of its own. Its thoughts are merely mental mirages, so if one of its parts is defective then obviously it is in deep trouble. But man is no robot, and each so-called genetic defect has an internal part to play in the entire picture of genetic reality. The principle of uncertainty must operate genetically, or you would have been locked into overspecializations as a species.2
quotes.translation: 你对物理规范的过度依赖，以及关于适者生存的扭曲观念，当然会夸大任何基因缺陷的存在。许多宗教教义再次将此类状况视为神惩罚的结果。物种的生存远更依赖你的主观活动而非物理活动——因为正是你的主观行为决定了你的物理行为。科学当然持相反观点，仿佛你的物理行为是机器人机械、形式化行为的结果——一个被偶然宇宙的盲目元素奇迹般编程的机器人。这个机器人被编程为不惜任何代价求生存。它没有真正的自我意识。它的思想只是精神海市蜃楼，所以如果它某个部件有缺陷，显然就陷入大麻烦。但人不是机器人，每个所谓的基因缺陷在基因实相的整体图景中都有其内在作用。不确定性原理必须在基因层面运作，否则作为一个物种，你早已陷入过度专门化的困境。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Belief systems are as necessary and natural as physical organs are. In fact, their purpose is to help you direct the functioning of your biological being. You give no conscious thought to the coming and going of cells within your organs. Left alone, your thoughts will come and go through your belief systems just as naturally; and ideally, they will balance out, maintaining their own health and directing your body so that its innate therapies take place.
quotes.translation: 信念系统就像身体器官一样必要且自然。事实上，它们的目的是帮助你引导生物体的运作。你不会有意识地思考器官内细胞的来去。顺其自然时，你的思想会像细胞一样自然地通过信念系统来去；理想情况下，它们会达到平衡，维持自身健康并引导你的身体，使其内在疗愈得以发生。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In the dream world, however, each feeling or idea can be immediately expressed and experienced. The physical world has buildings in it that you manufacture -- that is, they do not spring up naturally from the ground itself. In the same way, your thoughts are "manufactured products" in the dream world. They are a part of the environment and appear within its reality, though they change shape and form constantly, as physically manufactured objects do not.
quotes.translation: 然而在梦境世界，每个感受或想法都能立即被表达和体验。物理世界中有你制造的建筑——也就是说，它们并非自然从地面生长出来。同样地，你的思想在梦境世界中是"制造出来的产品"。它们是环境的一部分，出现在其实相中，尽管它们不断改变形状和形式，而物理制造物则不会如此。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 720
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: It is easy for you to see that seeds bring forth the fruit of the earth, each [of] their own kind. No seed is identical to any other, yet generally speaking there are species that serve to unite them. You do not mistake an orange for a grape. In the same way ideas or thoughts form general patterns, bringing forth in your world certain kinds of events. In this respect your thoughts and feelings "seed" physical reality, bringing forth materializations.
quotes.translation: 你很容易明白种子会在地里结出果实，每种种子各结其类。没有两颗种子是完全相同的，但大致来说，有物种的分类将它们联系起来。你不会把橘子误认作葡萄。同样地，观念或思想形成一般的模式，在你们的世界里引发某些事件。就此而言，你们的思想和情感"播种"了物质实相，促成了物质化。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 803, MAY 2, 1977
9:43 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: As I have said before, your thoughts are reality. They directly affect your body. It seems that you are highly civilized people because you put your ill into hospitals where they can be cared for. What you do, of course, is to isolate a group of people who are filled with negative beliefs about illness. The contagion of beliefs spreads. Patients are obviously in hospitals because they are ill. The sick and their doctors both work on that principle.
quotes.translation: 正如我以前说过的，你们的思想就是实相。它们直接影响你们的身体。你们似乎觉得自己是高度文明的人，因为你们把病人送进医院，让他们得到照顾。然而，你们所做的，其实是隔离一群充满负面信念的人。信念的传染会蔓延。病人显然是因为生病才住院的。病人和医生都遵循这个原则。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Where matter would exist there, a concentration of energy forms what you could think of as a body form, body meaning mass, yet it would not be matter in your terms. Such manifestations in the electric field are much more powerful, in terms of intensity per mass unit, than anything you know. This is a very important point, which will come up with us again in the future. Now again: regardless of current scientific thought, there are at least three different kinds of electric force which your scientists have not yet discovered, and one of these has much to do with the intensity of thoughts as they are formed in the intangible mind, and translated to the physical brain and then into action, as the case may be.
quotes.translation: 在物质存在之处，能量的集中会形成你们可以视为身体形态的东西——"身体"意味着质量，但它不会是你们所说的物质。这种电场中的显现，以单位质量的强度而言，比你们所知的任何东西都强大得多。这是一个非常重要的点，我们将来还会再提到。现在再说一次：不论当前的科学思想如何，至少存在三种你们科学家尚未发现的电力，其中一种与思想的强度密切相关——思想在无形的心灵中形成，然后转化为物质大脑，再根据情况转化为行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now it is easier perhaps for some of you to understand the simple stories and parables of beginnings of which I have spoken. But the time has come for mankind to take several steps further, to expand the nature of his own consciousness by trying to comprehend a more profound version of reality. You have outgrown the time of children's tales. When your own thoughts have a form and reality, when they have validity even in other systems of reality of which you are unaware, then it is not difficult to understand why other systems of probabilities are also affected by your own thoughts and emotions -- nor why the actions of the probable gods are not affected by what happens in other dimensions of existence.
quotes.translation: 现在，也许你们有些人更容易理解我讲过的那些简单的起源故事和寓言。但人类是时候再迈出几步，通过试图理解更深刻的实相版本，来扩展自身意识的本质了。你们已经超越了童话故事的年代。当你们自己的思想具有形态和实相，甚至在你们未知的其他实相系统中也有效力时，就不难理解为什么其他概率系统也会受到你们自己思想和情感的影响——也不难理解为什么可能的神祇的行动不会受到其他存在维度中发生的事情的影响。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Though this thought-image usually is not seen by others, it is quite possible that in the future scientific instruments may perceive it. As it is, such an image may be perceived by those who have developed use of the inner senses. Any intense mental act -- thought or emotion -- will not only be constructed in some physical or pseudophysical manner, but will also bear to some extent the imprint of the personality who originally conceived it.
quotes.translation: 尽管这个思想影像通常不被他人看见，但未来科学仪器很可能能感知到它。就目前而言，这种影像可能被那些已发展出内在感官运用能力的人所感知。任何强烈的精神活动——思想或情感——不仅会以某种物质或伪物质的方式被构建，还会在一定程度上带有最初构想它的人格的印记。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: When you are in touch with your psyche, you experience direct knowledge. Direct knowledge is comprehension. When you are dreaming, you are experiencing direct knowledge about yourself or about the world. You are comprehending your own being in a different way. When you are reading a book, you are experiencing indirect knowledge that may or may not lead to comprehension. Comprehension itself exists whether or not you have the words -- or even the thoughts -- to express it. You may comprehend the meaning of a dream without understanding it at all in verbal terms. Your ordinary thoughts may falter, or slip and slide around your inner comprehension without ever really coming close to expressing it.
quotes.translation: 当你与自己的心灵接触时，你会体验到直接知识。直接知识就是领悟。当你做梦时，你正在体验关于自己或世界的直接知识。你正以不同的方式领悟自己的存在。当你读书时，你体验的是间接知识，它可能导向领悟，也可能不会。领悟本身是存在的，无论你是否拥有表达它的词语——甚至思想。你可能领悟一个梦的意义，却完全无法用言语理解它。你平常的思想可能会踌躇，或在你内在领悟周围滑移，却从未真正接近表达它。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Each thought has a result, in your terms. The same kind of thought, habitually repeated, will seem to have a more or less permanent effect. If you like the effect then you seldom examine the thought. If you find yourself assailed by physical difficulties, however, you begin to wonder what is wrong.
quotes.translation: 每个思想都会产生结果，以你们的时间观而言。习惯性重复的同类思想，似乎会产生或多或少持久的效果。若你喜爱这效果，便很少检视那思想。但若发现自己受身体困境所扰，你便开始思索哪里出了差错。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The people who died at Jonestown believed that they must die. They wanted to die. How could their thoughts allow them to bring about their [bodily deaths]? Again, the question makes sense only if you do not realize that your thoughts are as physically a part of your body as viruses are (intently).
quotes.translation: 琼斯镇死去的人们相信他们必须死。他们渴望死亡。他们的思想如何允许他们促成自身的[肉体死亡]？再次强调，除非你尚未认识到：你们的思想在物理层面上与病毒一样，都是你们身体的一部分（专注地），否则这个问题毫无意义。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

SESSION 841, MARCH 14, 1979
9:08 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Feelings of violence accumulated early, but in his family there were no acceptable ways of releasing normal aggressive feelings. When these built up into felt, violent eruptions, Augustus was only the more convinced of his unacceptable nature. For some time in his normal state as a teenager, he tried harder and harder to be "good." This meant the banishing of thoughts or impulses that were sexually inspired along various lines, aggressive, or even just unconventional. Considerable energy was used to inhibit these portions of his inner experience. The denied mental events did not disappear, however. They increased in intensity and were kept apart from his "safer" usual thoughts.
quotes.translation: 暴力感很早就开始累积，但在他家中，释放正常攻击性情感的方式都不被允许。当这些情绪累积成可感知的暴力爆发时，奥古斯都反而更加确信自己本性不可接受。青少年时期，他在正常状态下曾一度拼命试图做个"好人"。这意味着要驱逐那些源于性、攻击性、甚至只是不合常规的思想或冲动。大量能量被用于抑制内在经验的这些部分。然而被否定的心智事件并未消失。它们强度递增，并被隔离在他"更安全"的惯常思想之外。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The fly is intensely conscious, at every moment engrossed in itself and its environment, precisely tuned to elements of which you are "unconscious." There are simply different kinds of consciousness, and you cannot basically compare one to the other any more than you can compare, say, a toad to a star to an apple to a thought to a woman to a child to a native to a suburbanite to a spider to a cat. They are varieties of consciousness, each focused upon its own view of reality, each containing experience that others exclude.
quotes.translation: 苍蝇具有高度意识，每时每刻都沉浸于自身与环境之中，精准调频于你们"无意识"的要素。只是存在不同种类的意识，你们根本无法相互比较，就像你无法比较蟾蜍与星辰、苹果与思想、女人与孩童、原住民与郊区居民、蜘蛛与猫。它们是意识的变体，每种都聚焦于自身对实相的观点，每种都包含其他意识所排除的经验。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: I said (in sessions 711 and 716, for instance) that your normal focus of consciousness can be compared to your home station. So far, exercises have been described that will gently lead you away from concentration upon this home base, even while its structure is strengthened at the same time. You can also call this home station or local program your world view, since from it you perceive your reality. To some extent it represents your personal focus, through which you interpret most of your experience. As I mentioned (in Session 715, for instance), when you begin to move away from that particular organization, strange things may start to happen. You may be filled with wonder, excitement, or perplexity. You may be delighted or appalled, according to whether or not your new perceptions agree or disagree with your established world view.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Say for example that our individual "A" wanted to transmit this thought to "B". The thought is as much a reality as the landscape. It is as much a part of individual "A" as the landscape is part of the physical earth. Our imaginary artist could not rip the landscape out of the earth, or bring it to his studio. He could not create an identical landscape because he did not have at his command the perspectives or materials necessary.
quotes.translation: 举例来说，假设我们的个体"A"想将某个思想传递给"B"。这思想与风景一样真实。它如同风景是物质地球的一部分那样，是个体"A"的一部分。我们假想的艺术家无法将风景从大地中剥离，或将其搬进画室。他无法创造完全相同的风景，因为他没有掌握必要的视角或材料。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Man is so highly verbal that he finds it difficult to understand that other species work with idea-complexes (with a hyphen) of a different kind, in which of course thought as you consider it is not involved. But an equivalent exists; using an analogy, it is as if ideas are built up not through sentence structure reinforced by inner visual images, but by like "mental" patterns structured through touch and scent -- in other words, thinking, but within a framework entirely different and alien to you.
quotes.translation: 人类如此依赖语言，以致难以理解其他物种运作着不同种类的观念复合体（带连字符），其中自然不涉及你们所认为的思想。但存在等价物；打个比方，这就像观念并非通过内在视觉影像强化的句子结构来构建，而是通过触觉与嗅觉构建的类似"心智"模式——换言之，这是思考，但发生在与你们完全相异且陌生的框架内。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: I told you that we do not experience your time sequence. We travel through various intensities. Our work, development, and experience all takes place within what I term the "moment point." Here, within the moment point, the smallest thought is brought to fruition, the slightest possibility explored, the probabilities thoroughly examined, the least or the most forceful feeling entertained. It is difficult to explain this clearly, and yet the moment point is the framework within which we have our psychological experience. Within it, simultaneous actions follow "freely" through associative patterns. For example, pretend that I think of you, Joseph. In so doing I immediately experience -- and fully -- your past, present, and future (in your terms), and all of those strong or determining emotions and motivations that have ruled you. I can travel through those experiences with you, if I choose. We can follow a consciousness through all of its forms, for example, and in your terms, within the flicker of an eye.
quotes.translation: 我曾告诉过你，我们并不经历你们的时间序列。我们穿越各种强度。我们的工作、发展和经验都发生在我所称的"片刻点"内。在此，在片刻点内，最微小的念头得以实现，最细微的可能性被探索，或然率被彻底检视，最微弱或最强烈的情感被体验。要清楚解释这一点很困难，然而片刻点是我们拥有心理经验的架构。在其中，同时性的行动通过联想模式"自由"地展开。例如，假设我想到了你，约瑟。这样做时，我立即且完整地体验了你的过去、现在和未来（以你们的说法），以及所有支配你的强烈或决定性情感与动机。如果我选择，我可以与你一同穿越那些经验。例如，我们可以跟随一个意识穿越其所有形态，以你们的说法，就在眨眼之间。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Since consciousness forms matter, and not the other way around, then thought exists before the brain and after it. A child can think coherently before he learns vocabulary -- but he cannot impress the physical universe in its terms. So this inner knowledge has always been available, but is to become physically manifest -- literally made flesh. The Speakers were the first to impress this inner knowledge upon the physical system, to make it physically known. Sometimes only one or two Speakers were alive in several centuries. Sometimes there were many. They looked around them and knew that the world sprang from their interior reality. They told others. They knew (pause) that the seemingly solid natural objects about them were composed of many minute consciousnesses.
quotes.translation: 既然意识形成物质，而非相反，那么思想存在于大脑之前和之后。一个孩子在学会词汇之前就能连贯地思考——但他无法以物质世界的术语来影响它。因此，这种内在知识一直可得，但需要变得物质显化——真正成为肉身。传讯者是最先将这种内在知识印刻到物质系统上、使其被物质世界知晓的人。有时几个世纪里只有一两位传讯者活着。有时则有很多。他们环顾四周，知道世界源自他们的内在实相。他们告诉他人。他们知道（停顿）周围看似坚固的自然物体是由许多微小的意识组成的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I am not saying that, nor implying it in the case just mentioned. In your system you have a direct hand in the formation of physical reality. Your natural given data is the result of individual, mass, and collective thoughts, feelings, and emotions, materialized. Your system in this respect is more creative than the systems just mentioned. On the other hand, within these other systems there is a strong innovative group consciousness developing, in which identity is retained but greater inner play allowed between individuals, a large creative interchange of symbol-pools, a drawing upon mental and psychic symbols with greater facility. Because of this, these individuals recognize more clearly the connection between creative images and given sense data. They purposely alter and change their given sense data, and experiment with it.
quotes.translation: 我并非在说，也并非暗示刚才提到的情况。在你们的系统中，你们直接参与了物质实相的形成。你们自然给定的感官资料是个体、群体和集体的思想、感受与情感物质化的结果。在这方面，你们的系统比刚才提到的系统更具创造力。另一方面，在这些其他系统中，有一种强大的创新性群体意识在发展，其中身份得以保留，但个体之间有更大的内在游戏空间，符号库之间有大量的创造性交流，更自如地汲取心智与心灵符号。正因如此，这些个体更清晰地认识到创造性意象与给定感官资料之间的联系。他们有意改变和调整给定的感官资料，并对其进行实验。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: We must unfortunately often deal with analogies, because they can form bridgeworks between concepts. There are units of consciousness,3 then, as there are units of matter. I do not want you to think of these units as particles. There is a basic unit of consciousness that, expressed, will not be broken down, as once it was thought that an atom was the smallest unit and could not be broken down. The basic unit of consciousness obviously is not physical. It contains within itself innately infinite properties of expansion, development, and organization; yet within itself always maintains the kernel of its own individuality. Despite whatever organizations it becomes part of, or how it mixes with other such basic units, its own identity is not annihilated.
quotes.translation: 不幸的是，我们常常不得不使用类比，因为它们可以在概念之间搭建桥梁。那么，有意识单位，就像有物质单位一样。我不希望你们把这些单位想象成粒子。有一种基本的意识单位，一旦表达出来就不会被分解，就像曾经人们认为原子是最小单位且不可分解一样。基本的意识单位显然是非物质的。它内在包含无限扩展、发展和组织的固有属性；然而它始终保持着自身个体性的核心。无论它成为何种组织的一部分，或如何与其他此类基本单位混合，其自身身份都不会被湮灭。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 682
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Your thoughts, for example, and your intents, have their own validity and force. You set them into motion, but then they follow their own laws and realities. All creativity comes from the psyche. I [recently] suggested a project to Ruburt's class -- one that will ultimately illuminate many of the points I am making in "Unknown" Reality. I suggested that Ruburt's students create a "city"9 at another level of reality. This is not to be a pie-in-the sky sort of thing, or some "heaven" hanging suspended above, but a very valid meeting place between worlds. A psychic marketplace, for example, where ideas are exchanged, a place of psychic commerce, a pleasant environment with quite definite coordinates, established as an "orbiting satellite" on the outskirts of your world.
quotes.translation: 例如，你们的思想和意图有其自身的有效性和力量。你们将它们启动，但它们随后遵循自己的法则和实相。所有创造力都来自心灵。我（最近）向鲁柏的班级建议了一个项目——一个最终将阐明我在《未知的实相》中所提许多要点的项目。我建议鲁柏的学生在另一个实相层面创造一个"城市"。这并非空中楼阁之类的东西，也不是悬浮在空中的某种"天堂"，而是一个世界之间非常有效的交汇点。例如，一个心灵市场，在那里交换想法，一个心灵商业之地，一个具有相当明确坐标的宜人环境，作为你们世界边缘的"轨道卫星"而建立。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 713
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: In a probable reality, a Ruburt and a Joseph now live there. In the world that you recognize as official, however, they moved into the hill house. To some extent both of them are aware of the inner processes involved in the final decision. I do not mean that they are simply familiar with the exterior thought processes involved, such as: "The hill house is better constructed," or "It has a fine view." I am speaking of deeper mechanisms of consideration (pause), in which correlations are made between interior and exterior realities. (Pause.) It is obvious that when you move from one place to another you make an alteration in space -- but you alter time as well, and you set into motion a certain psychological impetus that reaches out to affect everyone you know. (Long pause.) When a house is vacant all of the people in the neighborhood send out their own messages. To a certain extent any given inhabited area forms its own "entity." This applies to the smallest neighborhood4 and to the greatest nation. Such messages are often encountered in the dream state. Empty houses are psychic vacancies that yearn to be filled. When you move, you move into other portions of your own selfhood.5
quotes.translation: 在一个或然实相中，一个鲁柏和一个约瑟现在住在那里。然而，在你们视为官方的世界中，他们搬进了山丘上的房子。在某种程度上，两人都意识到最终决定所涉及的内在过程。我并非指他们仅仅熟悉外在的思考过程，例如："山丘上的房子建造得更好"，或"它有很好的视野"。我指的是更深层的考量机制（停顿），在其中内在与外在实相之间建立了关联。（停顿。）显然，当你从一个地方搬到另一个地方时，你改变了空间——但你也改变了时间，并且你启动了一种心理动力，它会向外延伸，影响你所认识的每一个人。（长停顿。）当一所房子空置时，附近的所有人都会发出他们自己的信息。在某种程度上，任何有人居住的区域都形成自己的"实体"。这适用于最小的社区和最大的国家。这些信息常常在梦境状态中被遇到。空房子是渴望被填满的心灵空缺。当你搬家时，你搬进了自己身份的其他部分。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 742
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: At cellular levels the world exists with a kind of social interchange, in which the birth and death of cells are known to all others, and in which the death of a frog and a star gain equal weight. But at your level of activity your thoughts, feelings, and intents, however private, form part of the inner environment of communication. This inner environment is as pertinent and vital to the species' well-being as is the physical one. It represents the psychic, mass bank of potential, even as the planet provides a physical bank of potential. When there is an earthquake in another area of the world, the land mass in your own country is in one way or another affected. When there are psychic earthquakes in other areas of the world, then you are also affected, and usually to the same degree.
quotes.translation: 在细胞的层面上，世界以一种社会性交流的方式存在，其中细胞的生与死为所有其他细胞所知，而一只青蛙的死亡与一颗恒星的消亡具有同等分量。但在你的活动层面上，你的思想、情感和意图，无论多么私密，都构成了内在沟通环境的一部分。这个内在环境对物种福祉的重要性与物理环境同样关键。它代表着心灵层面的潜能总库，正如地球提供了物理层面的潜能总库。当世界某处发生地震时，你所在国家的大地也会以某种方式受到影响。当世界其他地区发生心灵地震时，你同样会受到影响，而且程度通常相同。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 804, MAY 9, 1977
9:44 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Many of my readers are familiar with private meditation, when concentration is focused in one particular area. There are many methods and schools of thought here, but a highly suggestive state of mind results, in which spiritual, mental, and physical goals are sought. It is impossible to meditate without a goal, for that intent is itself a purpose. Unfortunately, many of your public health programs, and commercial statements through the various media, provide you with mass meditations of a most deplorable kind. I refer to those in which the specific symptoms of various diseases are given, in which the individual is further told to examine the body with those symptoms in mind. I also refer to those statements that just as unfortunately specify diseases for which the individual may experience no symptoms of an observable kind, but is cautioned that these disastrous physical events may be happening despite his or her feelings of good health. Here the generalized fears fostered by religious, scientific, and cultural beliefs are often given as blueprints of diseases in which a person can find a specific focus -- the individual can say: "Of course, I feel listless, or panicky, or unsafe since I have such-and-such a disease."
quotes.translation: 我的许多读者熟悉私人冥想，即注意力集中于某一特定领域。这里有许多方法和思想流派，结果会产生一种高度暗示性的心灵状态，在其中寻求精神、心智和身体的目标。没有目标就无法冥想，因为那个意图本身就是一种目的。不幸的是，你们许多公共卫生项目以及通过各种媒体发布的商业声明，为你们提供了最可悲的大众冥想。我指的是那些列举各种疾病具体症状的冥想，其中还进一步告诉个体要带着这些症状检查身体。我也指的是那些同样不幸的声明，它们具体指出个体可能没有明显症状的疾病，但警告说尽管个体感觉健康，这些灾难性的身体事件可能正在发生。在这里，由宗教、科学和文化信仰所滋生的普遍恐惧常常被作为疾病的蓝图，个体可以在其中找到具体的焦点——个体可以说："当然，我感到无精打采、恐慌或不安全，因为我患有某某疾病。"
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: They cast their light over historical events because they are responsible for those events. They mix and merge the inner, unseen but felt, eternal psychic experience of man with the temporal events of his physical days, and form a combination that structures thoughts and beliefs from civilization to civilization. In Framework 2 the interior power of nature is ever-changing. The dreams, hopes, aspirations and fears of man interact in a constant motion that then forms the events of your world. That interaction includes not only man, of course, but the emotional reality of all earthly consciousnesses as well, from a microbe to a scholar, from a frog to a star. You interpret the phenomena of your world according to the mythic characteristics that you have accepted. You organize physical reality, then, through ideas. You use only those perceptions that serve to give those ideas validity. The physical body itself is quite capable of putting the world together in different fashions than the one that is familiar to you.
quotes.translation: 它们将光芒投射到历史事件上，因为它们对这些事件负责。它们将人类内在、不可见但可感知的永恒心灵体验与物质岁月中的时间性事件混合并融合，形成一种从文明到文明构建思想和信念的组合。在架构二中，自然的内在力量不断变化。人类的梦想、希望、渴望和恐惧在持续的运动中相互作用，然后形成你们世界的事件。当然，这种相互作用不仅包括人类，还包括从微生物到学者、从青蛙到恒星的所有地球意识的情感实相。你们根据所接受的神话特征来解释你们世界的现象。因此，你们通过观念来组织物质实相。你们只使用那些能赋予这些观念有效性的感知。身体本身完全有能力以不同于你们所熟悉的方式将世界组合在一起。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: eth's statement at the end of dictation for the 815th session, "I will try to begin work on our book in a more predictable fashion," reflected his good intent, but things didn't turn out that way. Jane and I let the holiday season intervene to some extent, and Session 816, which came through the day after Christmas, didn't concern book work at all. Then, starting in early January, we held eight sessions having to do with questions we'd put off dealing with for a long time; some were personal and some were not. We stayed with our Monday-Saturday evening session routine, though, and kept busy with all of our other projects.1
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Much of this is difficult to explain, again, for information and knowledge is constantly transformed -- almost completely reborn, so to speak, through characteristics that are inherently a part of thought itself. Knowledge is changed automatically through the auspices of each consciousness who perceives it. It is magnified and yet refined. It is a constant language, yet one that transforms itself. When I "attend these sessions," or "speak," then I exchange with others a more complicated system of reality than any computer could handle. You do not understand or perceive the ways in which your reality contributes to the foundation of the mass-world reality that you experience. Unconsciously, each individual participates in forming that world. In my case, however, I am aware of the same kind of activities, only in regard to many realities rather than one.2
quotes.translation: 其中许多内容难以解释，再次说明，因为信息和知识不断被转化——可以说，通过思想本身固有的特性，几乎完全重生。知识通过感知它的每个意识的主持而自动改变。它被放大却又被精炼。它是一种恒常的语言，却是一种自我转化的语言。当我"参加这些课程"或"说话"时，我便与他人交换一种比任何计算机都能处理的更复杂的实相系统。你们不了解或感知不到你们的实相如何为你们所经历的大众世界实相的基础做出贡献。无意识地，每个个体都参与形成那个世界。然而，就我而言，我意识到同样类型的活动，只是涉及许多实相而非一个。
