quotes.text: Your genetic structure reacts to each thought that you have, to the state of your emotions, to your psychological climate. In your terms, it contains the physical history of the species in context with the probable future capabilities of the species. You choose your genetic structure so that it suits the challenges and capabilities of the species. You choose your genetic structure so that it suits the challenges and potentials that you have chosen. (Long pause.) It represents your physical reference point, your bodily framework. It is your personal physical property. It is a portion of physical matter that you have identified, filled out with your own identity. It is like a splendid ship, the body, that you have chosen ahead of time for a splendid challenging adventure--a ship that you have personally appointed that is equipped to serve as much as possible as a physical manifestation of your personhood.
quotes.translation: 你的基因结构对你拥有的每一个思想、你的情绪状态、你的心理气候做出反应。用你们的说法，它包含了物种的物理历史，并与物种可能的未来能力相关联。你选择你的基因结构，使其适合你所选择的挑战和潜能。（长停顿。）它代表你的物理参考点，你的身体框架。它是你的个人物理财产。它是你已识别、并用你自己的身份填充的那部分物质。它就像一艘华丽的船——身体——你提前为一次精彩的挑战性冒险所选择的船；一艘你亲自装备的船，尽可能作为你人格的物理表现而服务。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This is actually not as bad an analogy as I first thought, in that oftentimes a particular personality will be like the right arm of the entity as a whole, while another particular personality will have both feet on the ground. To get back however to the original point I wish to make, there is a point beyond which the most suggestible personality will be beyond reach, no matter what the circumstances are. Manifestations of the personality may follow the lines that suggestion commands. This is bad enough, but it only means that the personality has been forced to change its mode of action in the physical world. The personality seems shattered because the actions seem so changed. And here again we have your cause and effect, misapplied. The basic personality, that is the primary personality, has not been changed and will not change except through the personality itself.
quotes.translation: 这实际上并不像我最初想的那么糟糕的类比，因为通常一个特定人格会像整体实体的右臂，而另一个特定人格则脚踏实地。然而，回到我最初想说的要点，存在一个临界点，超过这个点，无论环境如何，最易受暗示的人格也无法被触及。人格的表现可能遵循暗示所命令的路线。这已经够糟了，但这只意味着人格被迫改变了它在物质世界中的行动模式。人格似乎破碎了，因为行动似乎变化如此之大。这里我们再次看到你们误用的因果关系。基本人格，即主要人格，并没有改变，也不会改变，除非通过人格本身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: The entity should therefore not be thought of as completed. The electric universe, of which you know so little, is a reality, and yet it is also a symbol of another reality. For behind even this electrical universe there is a reality which cannot be explored in terms of speech; for all consciousness, while having an electric reality, has a reality beyond even this. It is the vitality of the universe, and all universes, which causes the electrical reality, and the vitality of the universe, composing everything that is, cannot be touched. It is touch, but touch not as it is conceived of in your terms. This vitality is the most immediate and intimate aspect that composes the camouflage appearance within all fields. And yet it is by far more than the camouflage.
quotes.translation: 因此，存有不应被视为已完成。你们所知甚少的电性宇宙是一个实相，但它也是另一个实相的象征。因为即使在这个电性宇宙背后，也存在一个无法用言语探索的实相；因为所有意识，虽然具有电性实相，却拥有超越此层面的实相。正是宇宙及所有宇宙的生命力引发了电性实相，而构成万物的宇宙生命力是无法被触碰的。它就是触碰本身，但并非你们所认知的那种触碰。这股生命力是所有场域中构成伪装表象最直接、最内在的面向，然而它远不止是伪装。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: （Seth had some interesting things to say about using suggestion. The kernel of thought here is that it is important to use whatever suggestions we want to, daily; he stressed the importance of not missing a day. He explained that in a given period of say, thirty days, the suggestions on perhaps but three or four days within that period would be really effective, and that as of now we have no way of knowing the best days. So if we miss a day, we run a chance of missing out on a particularly effective day for suggestion. Seth said that on occasion suggestion will reach all levels of the personality, that our voice will be heard throughout the personality, and that effective action will follow. There was more here but the above is the main point made.
quotes.translation: （赛斯对使用暗示提出了一些有趣的观点。这里的核心思想是，每天使用我们想要的任何暗示很重要；他强调了一天都不能错过的重要性。他解释说，在比如三十天的特定时期内，可能只有三四天的暗示会真正有效，而目前我们无法知道哪些是最好的日子。所以如果我们错过一天，就有可能错过一个特别有效的暗示日。赛斯说，有时暗示会触及人格的所有层面，我们的声音会在整个人格中被听到，随后有效的行动就会发生。这里还有更多内容，但以上是主要观点。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Now I am only speaking because many of you have known that I was here. And it ill behooves me not to bid you welcome when you felt my presence. But in many of your discussions, there has only been surface thought and surface feelings... and we do not walk on the surface in this class. I would push your heads collectively and individually underneath... for you still do not understand that you create your physical reality! you form your blocks and shove them up upon the surface of the earth. You cannot solve the problems of your world on a surface level. If you could, you would have done it centuries ago. You must step out of your present perspective in order to see your world clearly. Here you are not to hide within the closet of 3 dimensionality and cry, "It is dark and I cannot see!" And refuse to use the inner light that can alone aid your vision.
quotes.translation: 我现在之所以说话，只是因为你们许多人已经知道我在这里。当你们感受到我的存在时，我不该不向你们表示欢迎。但在你们许多讨论中，只有表面的思想和表面的感受……而在这个课堂上，我们不浮于表面。我会把你们的头集体地、个别地按下去……因为你们仍然不明白你们创造了你们的物质实相！你们形成自己的障碍，并把它们推到地球表面。你们无法在表面层面解决世界的问题。如果你们能，几个世纪前就已经做到了。你们必须跳出当前的视角，才能看清自己的世界。在这里，你们不是要躲在三维空间的壁橱里哭喊：“太黑了，我看不见！”并拒绝使用唯一能帮助你们视力的内在之光。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: (Very intently:) You cannot do anything, literally, that is not natural. Nevertheless, over a period of time "artificial" chemicals taken with food into the body will form a new kind of nature, in your terms. Your bodies are beautifully equipped, and will turn almost anything to their advantage. According to many schools of thought, artificial drugs, so-called, or chemicals, are considered in a very negative light, cutting you off from nature. Yet such experiments represent a strong line of probability only in its "infancy," in which man could sustain himself without draining the earth, live without killing animals, and literally form a new kind of physical structure connected to the earth, while not depleting its substance.
quotes.translation: （非常专注地：）你们实际上无法做任何不自然的事情。然而，在你们看来，随着时间的推移，随食物进入体内的“人造”化学物质会形成一种新的自然。你们的身体装备精良，几乎能将任何东西转化为自己的优势。根据许多思想流派，所谓的人造药物或化学物质被认为具有非常负面的影响，会使你们与自然隔绝。然而，这类实验只代表一条强大可能性线的“婴儿期”，在这条线上，人类可以不耗尽地球资源而维持自身生存，不杀生而生活，并真正形成一种与地球相连的新型物理结构，同时不消耗其物质。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: In your terms, until now your consciousness has specialized in neurological patterning. As mentioned (in Session 682), this was extremely important while it learned the art of specialized focus. Now, however, it must begin to recognize that it can indeed expand, and bring into its awareness other quite legitimate realities. The nature of probabilities must be understood, for the time has come in the world as you experience it where the greatest wisdom and discrimination are needed. Your consciousness and neurological prejudice blind you to the full dimension of physical activity. The true implications of physical action are not as yet apparent to you.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: There are other much easier methods. I have explained to some degree the way images are constructed out of an available field of energy. You perceive only your own constructions. If a "ghost" wants to contact you therefore, he can do so through telepathy, and you can yourself construct the corresponding image if you desire. Or the individual might send you a thought-form at the same time that he telepathically communicates with you. Your rooms are full now of thought-forms that you do not perceive; and again, you are as much a ghostly phenomenon now as you will be after death. You are simply not aware of the fact.
quotes.translation: 还有其他更简单的方法。我在某种程度上已经解释了意象是如何从可用的能量场中构建出来的。你们只感知到自己的构建。因此，如果一个“鬼魂”想要联系你，他可以通过心灵感应做到，而如果你愿意，你可以自己构建相应的意象。或者，这个个体可能在你与他进行心灵感应沟通的同时，向你发送一个思想形态。你们的房间里现在充满了你们感知不到的思想形态；同样，你们现在和死后一样，都是一种幽灵现象。你们只是没有意识到这个事实。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Ruburt knew that Helper could be sent out to others, to their advantage, and in that regard the form stood for the great power of natural, positive desire and thought patterns. (To me:) You have the same kind of "form." These represent the greater source-selves out of which your present persons spring. I told you that you possessed far more knowledge about your own lives, and the lives of others, than you were intellectually aware of.2 You act on that knowledge, for one thing, when you are born physically, when you grow. The squirrel acts on that kind of knowledge when it buries nuts--as you saw, again, on a recent TV program--and the squirrel's greater knowledge includes the knowledge of its species as well.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏知道，“帮助者”可以被发送给他人，对他们有益，在这方面，这个形态代表了自然、积极欲望和思想模式的巨大力量。（对我说：）你也有同样类型的“形态”。这些代表了你们当前人格所源自的更大的源头自我。我告诉过你们，你们对自己生命及他人生命的了解，远比你们理智上意识到的要多得多。² 你们在物质出生时、在成长时，就是基于这种知识而行动。松鼠埋坚果时也是基于这种知识——正如你们最近在电视节目中看到的——而松鼠的更大知识还包括其物种的知识。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In such interrelationships, the steps or identities are each immeasurably enriched by the added perception of the others. As mentioned earlier, thoughts, containing their own electromagnetic reality, have form whether or not you perceive it. With each thought, then, you send out from yourself shapes and images that can be quite legitimate realities to those within the system of reality into which they are propelled.
quotes.translation: 在这种相互关系中，每一步或每个身份都因其他身份的额外感知而得到难以估量的丰富。如前所述，思想拥有其自身的电磁实相，无论你是否感知到，它都有形态。因此，每一个思想都会从你身上发出形状和影像，这些形状和影像对于它们被推入的实相系统中的存在而言，可能是完全合法的实相。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You do not understand the dimensions into which your thoughts drop, for they continue their own existence, and others look up to them and view them like stars. Now I am telling you that your own dreams and thoughts and mental actions appear to the inhabitants of other systems like the stars and planets within your own; and those inhabitants do not perceive what lies within and behind the stars in their own heavens.
quotes.translation: 你不理解你的思想落入的维度，因为它们继续着自己的存在，而其他存在仰望它们，视它们如星辰。现在我告诉你，你自己的梦、思想和心智活动，在其他系统的居民看来，就像你们系统中的星辰和行星；而那些居民并不感知他们自己天空中的星辰之内和之后的东西。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: I told you that thoughts are translated into this inner sound, but thoughts always attempt to materialize themselves also. As such they are incipient images, collectors of energy. They build up their own embryonic form until it is in one way or another physically translated.
quotes.translation: 我告诉过你，思想被转化为这种内在的声音，但思想也总是试图将自己物质化。因此，它们是初生的影像，是能量的收集者。它们构建自己的胚胎形态，直到以某种方式在物质上被转化。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Simple questions. It is because the man thinks differently that he has the ulcer, for the ulcer is the result of his characteristic method of thought. It is the result of his characteristic manner of viewing reality. This causes him to add to his self-image, in reality, that disability. The ulcer is formed intimately from the electrical reality that composes his thoughts. Do you find that a satisfactory answer?
quotes.translation: 简单的问题。正是因为这个人思维方式不同，他才得了溃疡，因为溃疡是他特有思维方法的结果。这是他看待实相特有方式的结果。这导致他在实相中，将那种残疾加诸于他的自我形象。溃疡是由构成他思想的电性实相紧密形成的。你觉得这个答案令人满意吗？
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

source_raw.title: （Tracing of the photographic negative used in the 7th envelope test, in Session 187, September 13,1965.）

quotes.text: Give us a moment. It seems to be something not completely surprising. Either you thought of this or they have thought of you, and some communications, though of an informal nature, may have happened in the past. Read what I have said more clearly, for there is an immediate issue involved. (After the session John told us that he has informally been approached to work for a rival drug concern--Abbott, recently.)
quotes.translation: 给我们一点时间。这似乎并非完全出人意料。要么你想到过这个，要么他们想到过你，而且过去可能发生过一些非正式性质的沟通。更仔细地阅读我所说的，因为涉及一个直接的问题。（课后约翰告诉我们，最近有人非正式地接触他，想让他为一家竞争对手的制药公司——雅培——工作。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The intensity is the core about which the electromagnetic energy units form. In your terms, the more intense the core, the sooner the physical materialization. This would apply whether the mental image was a fearful one or a joyful one. Now there is a very important problem here: If your turn of mind is highly intense and you think in vivid mental emotional images, these will be swiftly formed into physical events. If you are also of a highly pessimistic nature, given to thoughts and feelings of potential disaster, then these thoughts will be quite faithfully reproduced in experience.
quotes.translation: 强度是电磁能量单位围绕其形成的核心。用你们的话说，核心越强烈，物质化就越快。无论心智影像是一个恐惧的还是一个喜悦的，都适用。这里有一个非常重要的问题：如果你的心智倾向高度强烈，并且你以生动的心智情感影像思考，这些影像将迅速形成为物质事件。如果你同时具有高度悲观的本性，倾向于潜在灾难的想法和感受，那么这些想法将在经验中被相当忠实地再现。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Difficulty in the third and seventh vertebrae with our friend, Mark, causing pressure. (Pause.) A steady rather than too spasmodic application to his work is needed. He will always work quickly and intuitively, this is beneficial and characteristic of him. Thought and planning can be utilized however, when he is not working well. The thought and planning will then show itself even in the intuitional work.
quotes.translation: 我们朋友马克的第三节和第七节脊椎出现困难，造成压力。（停顿）他的工作需要持续进行，而非过度间歇性突击。他工作起来总是快速而直觉，这对他有益且是他的特质。不过当他工作不顺时，仍可运用思考与规划。这些思考与规划甚至会在直觉工作中显现出来。

quotes.text: Abruptly the candle flame shot up to a noticeable degree, at least twice its previous height. The increase in brightness was plainly noticeable, causing all of us to look at the candle. Seth then said he had caused the flame to grow. It stayed brighter for several minutes as he continued talking, then died down. Bill confirmed my own thought at the time, that a stray burst of wind had affected the fame. We had no way of knowing if wind was responsible or not; the kitchen window was perhaps fifteen feet away, and around a corner. Seth went on to say that the candle flame would not grow higher again, because Ruburt was alerted to the effect now, and was watching it.
quotes.translation: 烛焰突然明显窜高，至少达到原先的两倍高度。亮度显著增加，引得我们所有人都看向蜡烛。赛斯随后表示是他让火焰变大的。在他继续说话的数分钟里，烛光持续明亮，随后才减弱。比尔证实了我当时的想法——是一阵偶然的风影响了火焰。我们无从判断是否真是风的作用；厨房窗户大约在十五英尺外，还隔着转角。赛斯接着说烛焰不会再升高了，因为鲁柏现在已警觉到这种效应，正在观察它。

quotes.text: Now. In your system of reality you are learning what mental energy is, and how to use it. You do this by constantly transforming your thoughts and emotions into physical form. You are supposed to get a clear picture of your inner development by perceiving the exterior environment. What seems to be a perception, an objective concrete event, independent and apart from you the perceiver, is instead the physical materiaizationl of the perceiver's own inner emotions, energy and mental environment. As long as scientists insist upon considering the perceiver and the perceived event as entirely separate, then the true nature of perception will not be understood.
quotes.translation: 现在。在你们的实相系统中，你们正在学习什么是心灵能量，以及如何运用它。你们通过不断将思想和情感转化为物质形态来达成这一点。你们本应通过感知外在环境来清晰了解自己的内在发展。那些看似独立于感知者之外的客观具体事件——一种感知——实则是感知者自身内在情感、能量和心灵环境的物质显化。只要科学家坚持认为感知者与被感知事件完全分离，就无法理解感知的真实本质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: All such projections have an electromagnetic and chemical reality that has its origin within the field of the given personality. This projection of energy is one of the main characteristics of your kind. Dreams and dream images are then projected by the individual in sleep, and also in the waking state, although on a subconscious basis. So are thoughts constantly sent outward, and other projections which we have not yet discussed. Therefore as the individual sends out these projections, so does he receive the projections of others. As you know, telepathy operates constantly beneath the dictates of the ego, and so is your intellectual climate formed.
quotes.translation: 所有这类投射都具有电磁与化学实相，其根源在于特定人格的场域。这种能量投射是你们这类存在的主要特征之一。梦境与梦象由个体在睡眠中投射，也在清醒状态下（尽管基于潜意识层面）投射。思想同样不断向外发送，还有其他我们尚未讨论的投射形式。因此，当个体发出这些投射时，也同时接收他人的投射。如你所知，心电感应持续在自我指令之下运作，由此形成了你们的心智气候。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: We will then resume dictation. (Pause.) Your beliefs generate emotion. It is somewhat fashionable to place feelings above conscious thoughts, the idea being that emotions are more basic and natural than conscious reasoning is. The two actually go together but your conscious thinking largely determines your emotions, and not the other way around. Your beliefs generate the appropriate emotion that is implied. A long period of inner depression does not just come upon you. Your emotions do not betray you. Instead, over a period of time you have been consciously entertaining negative beliefs that then generated the strong feelings of despondency.
quotes.translation: 我们接着继续口述。（停顿）你们的信念产生情感。将感受置于意识思想之上在某种程度上已成风尚，认为情感比意识推理更基本、更自然。两者实则相辅相成，但你们的意识思考在很大程度上决定情感，而非相反。你们的信念会引发与之相应的情感。长期的内在抑郁并非凭空降临。你们的情感并未背叛你们。相反，在一段时间内，你们一直有意识地持有负面信念，这些信念随后产生了强烈的沮丧感。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Now, using an analogy only, let me explain that your thoughts and feelings also give off shadows (intently) that we will here call hallucinations.2 They are quite valid. They have as strong a part to play in dream reality as shadows do in the physical world. They are beautiful in themselves. They add to the entire picture. A shadow of a tree cools the ground. It affects the environment. So hallucinations alter the environment, but in a different way and at another level of reality. In the dream world hallucinations are like conscious shadows. They are not passive, nor is their shape dependent upon their origin. They have their own abilities.
quotes.translation: 现在，仅以类比方式说明：你们的思想和感受也会投射出阴影（专注地），我们在此称之为幻象。它们完全真实。它们在梦境实相中扮演的角色，如同阴影在物质世界中的作用一样重要。它们本身是美丽的，为整体画面增添色彩。树的阴影能冷却地面，影响环境。同样，幻象会改变环境，但以不同方式在另一实相层面运作。在梦境世界中，幻象如同有意识的阴影。它们并非被动存在，其形态也不取决于起源。它们拥有自身的能力。

quotes.text: Mental enzymes are connected with the pineal gland. Chemicals as you know them, body chemicals, are physical, but they are the propellants of this thought energy, containing within them all the codified data necessary for translating any thought or image into physical actuality. They cause the physical body to reproduce the inner image. They are sparks, so to speak, initiating the transformation.
quotes.translation: 心灵酵素与松果体相关联。你们所知的化学物质，即身体化学物质，虽是物质性的，却是这股思想能量的推进剂，其中包含将任何思想或意象转化为物质实相所需的一切编码数据。它们促使物质身体重现内在意象。可以说，它们是引发转化的火花。

quotes.text: （7th Question: "Can you tell me anything about what the handwriting says on the object?" "Not an invitation precisely at all, but reference to an occasion or visit." I thought it okay to ask this question since Seth had already mentioned handwriting in connection with the data. Seth's answer here is a good reference to the note Mother wrote inside the greeting card. It can actually apply just as well to the envelope object itself. The phone call on August 14 from my mother concerned a visit by us to Sayre, and one by her to us in Elmira. During this call arrangements were made for her to visit us here next weekend' on Saturday, August 20.
quotes.translation: （第七个问题："你能告诉我那件物品上的笔迹写了什么吗？" "完全不是邀请函，而是提及某个场合或拜访。" 我认为问这个问题没问题，因为赛斯在提供资料时已经提到了笔迹。赛斯这里的回答很好地对应了母亲在贺卡内写的便条。实际上，这个回答同样适用于信封这件物品本身。8月14日母亲打来的电话涉及我们去塞尔的拜访，以及她来埃尔迈拉看我们。通话期间，我们安排了她下周末——8月20日星期六——来此探望我们。

quotes.text: Even the duration of an event or object in space or time is determined by the intensity of the thoughts or emotions that gave it birth. Duration in space is not the same as duration in time, however, though it may seem that this is the case. I am speaking in your terms now. An event or object that exists briefly in space may have a much greater duration in time. It may have far greater importance and intensity, existing in your memory, for example, long after it has disappeared in space. Such an event or object does not merely exist symbolically within your mind or memory -- but in your terms its actual reality continues as a time event.
quotes.translation: 事件或物体在空间或时间中的存续时长，甚至也由赋予其生命的思想或情感的强度决定。然而，空间中的存续时长与时间中的存续时长并不相同，尽管表面上看似如此。我现在是用你们的说法。一个在空间中短暂存在的事件或物体，可能在时间中拥有更长的存续时长。它可能具有远为重要的分量和强度，例如，在空间消失后很久，仍存在于你们的记忆中。这样的事件或物体并非仅仅象征性地存在于你们的心智或记忆中——而是以你们的话说，其实际实相作为时间事件持续存在。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Here now once more, let me repeat that thoughts are definite electromagnetic realities. Therefore, being actions, they affect all other actions, and if I repeat this in session after session, I do so, so that it will never be forgotten. Therefore these secondary conditions are strengthened through the very act, or misact of thinking that they are primary rather than secondary elements in action.
quotes.translation: 现在让我再次重申，思想是明确的电磁实相。因此，作为行动，它们影响所有其他行动。如果我一次又一次地在课程中重复这一点，我这样做是为了让它永远不会被遗忘。因此，这些次要条件通过将行动中的次要元素而非主要元素视为主要元素的行为或错误行为而得到强化。

quotes.text: "I do not want to go into a history of culture here, but your organizations historically have largely been built upon your religious concepts, which have indeed been extremely rigid. The repressive nature of Christian thought in the Middle Ages, for example, is well known. Artistic expression itself was considered highly suspect if it traveled outside of the accepted precepts, and particularly of course if it led others to take action against those precepts. To some extent the same type of policy is still reflected in your current societies, though science or the state itself may serve instead of the church as the voice of authority.
quotes.translation: "我不想在此深入文化史，但你们历史上的组织很大程度上建立在你们的宗教观念之上，这些观念确实极为僵化。例如，中世纪基督教思想的压制性众所周知。艺术表达本身若超出公认的教条，便被视为高度可疑，尤其当它引导他人采取行动反对这些教条时。在某种程度上，同样的政策类型仍在你们当前的社会中有所反映，尽管科学或国家本身可能取代教会成为权威的声音。"

quotes.text: The experiences at these various levels may be interpreted symbolically. They may appear in the form of fantasy, fiction, or art work, without the conscious self realizing their origin. Now at any of these various stages of consciousness, other phenomena may also be perceived -- thought-forms for example, energy manifestations, projections from the personal subconscious, and projections from the collective unconscious. Any or all of these may take symbolic form, and may appear beneficial or threatening according to the attitude of the personality involved. They should be regarded as quite natural phenomena, often neutral in intent.
quotes.translation: 在这些不同层面的体验可能以象征方式被诠释。它们可能以幻想、虚构或艺术作品的形式出现，而意识自我并未意识到其起源。现在，在这些不同的意识阶段中，其他现象也可能被感知——例如思想形态、能量显化、来自个人潜意识的投射，以及来自集体潜意识的投射。这些现象中的任何一种或全部都可能采取象征形式，并根据涉及人格的态度而显得有益或具有威胁性。它们应被视为相当自然的现象，其意图通常是中性的。

quotes.text: Various kinds of seemingly contradictory characteristics may appear, then. One Sumari may have many deeply rewarding personal relationships. Another might find friends a distraction. One Sumari might enjoy performing in front of an audience, while another might not even be able to bear the thought. Since each person is unique, the various Sumari characteristics will then appear quite differently. Some live in cities, basking in the emotional nearness of others, content with a few flowerpots for a reminder of nature's beauty. Another might have a farm. In most cases, however, the slant of consciousness is primarily creative. Period.
quotes.translation: 随后可能出现各种看似矛盾的特质。一位苏马利可能拥有许多深刻而有益的人际关系，另一位则可能视朋友为干扰。一位苏马利可能享受在观众面前表演，而另一位甚至无法忍受这个念头。由于每个人都是独特的，各种苏马利特质会以截然不同的方式显现。有人住在城市里，沐浴在他人情感的亲近中，满足于几盆花来提醒自然之美；另一个人可能拥有农场。然而在大多数情况下，意识的主要倾向是创造性的。句号。

quotes.translation: 逻辑一步步建立它的纪念碑，一个念头接续另一个念头，形成一系列每个念头或推论都依赖前一个念头的序列。直觉则具有更自发的性质，较少依赖这种逐步推进的运动。

quotes.text: Any word, simply by being thought, written or spoken, immediately implies a specification. In your daily reality it is very handy to distinguish one thing from another by giving each item a name. When you are dealing with subjective experience, however, definitions can often serve to limit rather than express a given experience. Obviously the psyche is not a thing. It does not have a beginning or ending. It cannot be seen or touched in normal terms. It is useless, therefore, to attempt any description of it through usual vocabulary, for your language primarily allows you to identify physical rather than nonphysical experience.
quotes.translation: 任何词语，仅仅通过被思考、书写或说出，就立即暗示了一种限定。在你日常的现实里，通过给每个物品命名来区分一物与另一物非常方便。然而，当你处理主观体验时，定义往往起到限制而非表达特定体验的作用。显然，心灵不是一个东西。它没有开始或结束。在通常意义上它不能被看见或触摸。因此，试图通过常规词汇来描述它是无用的，因为你的语言主要允许你识别物理体验而非非物理体验。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The mother could not consciously control the bodily processes that lead to birth. In the truest sense, the birth magically happens, as miraculous in those terms as the so-called initial emergence of life upon the planet itself. Scientific analysis of the brain will tell you nothing about the power that moves your thoughts, or hint at the source of the brain's abilities. However, the constant activity between Frameworks 1 and 2 is constantly apparent in the very existence of your world, and in the relationships involving your imagination, feelings, and beliefs, and those private and shared events that compose your experience.
quotes.translation: 母亲无法有意识地控制导致分娩的身体过程。在最真实的意义上，分娩是神奇地发生的，用那些术语来说，其奇迹性不亚于所谓生命最初在地球上的出现。对大脑的科学分析不会告诉你任何关于驱动你思想的力量，也不会暗示大脑能力的来源。然而，架构一与架构二之间的持续活动，在你世界的存在本身中，以及涉及你想象力、感受和信念的关系中，还有构成你体验的那些私密与共享的事件中，始终显而易见。

quotes.text: These brief definitions are very general: A paranoid is afflicted with systematic, logically-reasoned delusions of grandeur or persecution; the personality can be relatively stable otherwise. A schizophrenic suffers from a division between thought processes and emotions. The cause of schizophrenia is unknown, and the victim usually ends up hospitalized because of the severity of symptoms, which can include motor malfunctions, perceptual distortions involving hallucinations and delusions, strange behavior, and a withdrawal from reality. Yet the schizophrenic can also keep the use of his or her primary intellectual capacities.
quotes.translation: 这些简要定义非常笼统：妄想症患者受制于系统性的、逻辑推理出的夸大或被害妄想；否则人格可能相对稳定。精神分裂症患者则遭受思维过程与情感之间的分裂。精神分裂症的病因未知，患者通常因症状严重而最终住院，症状可能包括运动功能障碍、涉及幻觉和妄想的感知扭曲、怪异行为以及脱离现实。然而，精神分裂症患者也可能保留其基本智力能力的使用。

quotes.text: In that respect the landscape has its center. Your own thoughts are like the outward spokes of a wheel. They are not at the center of yourself, but have traveled a long way from the center of yourself. You may therefore follow them backward. We have been dealing so with the diversity of the self that I want to assure you that within all this diversity the center can be found and recognized.
quotes.translation: 在这方面，风景有其中心。你自己的思想就像车轮向外辐射的辐条。它们并不在你自身的中心，而是从你自身的中心走了很远的路。因此，你可以沿着它们回溯。我们一直在如此处理自我的多样性，我想向你保证，在所有这些多样性之中，中心是可以被找到和辨认的。

quotes.text: As your cells have their own memories, so the conscious mind has a more overt kind of memory. Your conscious thoughts act as triggers, bringing both kinds of memory into activation. Within your physical being then each joyful, expanding, traumatic and tragic "past" event lies indelibly written. In your terms this is your working material, the memory of your physical being since the time of its conception in corporeal form. There are [in your memory] the most complex organizations and associative frameworks, that exist both in the depths of your cellular structure and in the highest reaches of your conscious activity.
quotes.translation: 正如你们的细胞拥有自己的记忆，意识心也拥有一种更显性的记忆。你们有意识的念头充当触发器，激活这两种记忆。在你们物质存有之内，每一个喜悦、扩展、创伤和悲剧性的"过去"事件都不可磨灭地铭刻其中。用你们的话说，这是你们的工作素材，是你们物质存有自其以肉体形式受孕以来的记忆。在你们的记忆中，存在着最复杂的组织结构和联想框架，既存在于你们细胞结构的深处，也存在于你们意识活动的最高领域。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: "Man thinks of acts, for example, and acting and doing, but he does not identify himself with those inner processes that make acting and doing possible. He identifies with what he thinks of as his logical thought, and the abilities of reasoning. These seem to suggest that he possesses an elegant, cool separation from nature, that the animals for example do not. He does not identify, again, with the processes that make his logical thinking possible. Those processes are spontaneous and 'unconscious,' so it appears that anything outside of his conscious control must be undisciplined or chaotic, and lacking in all logic.
quotes.translation: 例如，人思考行动、作为和做事，但他并不认同那些使行动和作为成为可能的内在过程。他认同于他所谓的逻辑思维和推理能力。这些似乎暗示他拥有一种优雅、冷静的与自然分离的状态，例如动物就没有。他同样不认同那些使他的逻辑思维成为可能的过程。这些过程是自发且"无意识"的，因此任何超出他有意识控制之外的事物似乎都必定是无纪律或混乱的，且缺乏一切逻辑。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: NOTES: SESSION 845
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: You might even make a reasonable, and I mean reasonable, Ruburt, list of preferred circumstances such as shelves built, rooms painted and so forth, and carry them out yourselves. Whatever if anything now daily aggravates you, change these things and you will be amazed at your added peace of mind. I would have thought that you would have hit upon the entryway idea yourselves; something to keep your psychic energies contained, and again it is the feeling that the entryway gives you rather than the entryway itself, and the feeling that a desk and chair in another room gives Ruburt, rather than the desk and chair itself.
quotes.translation: 你甚至可以列出一份合理的——我指的是合理的，鲁柏——偏好情境清单，比如搭好架子、粉刷房间等等，然后自己动手执行。无论现在每天有什么事情让你烦恼，改变这些事情，你会惊讶于内心增加的平静。我本以为你们自己会想到入口通道这个主意；某种能收束你们心灵能量的东西，再说一次，是入口通道带给你的感觉，而非入口通道本身，以及另一个房间里的书桌和椅子带给鲁柏的感觉，而非书桌和椅子本身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Its dimensions are in terms of values and value fulfillments. The apparent shapes, if you could see them within the electrical system, would seem to appear and disappear as pulses became stronger or weaker. Each and every thought and dream and experience that any human being has had exists as an individual, distinct electrical impulse of particular, unduplicated intensity. It exists not only isolated for example, or detached, but it exists as a part of the electrical pattern of that personality who originally created it. It is still a part of his electrical pattern, but it does also exist independently of him.
quotes.translation: 它的维度是以价值及价值实现来衡量的。如果你们能在电性系统中看到它们，其显见的形状会随着脉冲的强弱而似乎出现和消失。每一个人类曾有过的念头、梦境和体验，都作为一个个独特、特定且不可复制的强度的电性脉冲而存在。它并非孤立或分离地存在，而是作为最初创造它的那个人格的电性模式的一部分而存在。它仍然是那个人格电性模式的一部分，但也确实独立于他而存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now give us a moment ... One brief note: Ruburt was momentarily upset before the session -- cranky. He thought he did not feel like having a session at 9:30 P.M. to try to solve the world's problems. He just wanted to watch television and forget it all, and hidden in that crankiness is a good point: The sessions are an expression of your private and joint curiosity, a high and excellent curiosity about the nature of reality, a result of your desire to know; to know whether or not the knowledge can be held in your hands like a fruit, whether or not the knowledge can be dosed out to an ailing world as medicine.
quotes.translation: 现在给我们一点时间……一个简短的说明：鲁柏在课前短暂地心烦意乱——脾气暴躁。他觉得自己不想在晚上9点30分上课来解决世界的问题。他只想看电视，把这一切都抛在脑后，而隐藏在这暴躁脾气中的是一个好观点：这些课是你们私人及共同好奇心的表达，一种对实相本质的高尚而卓越的好奇心，是你们求知欲的结果；想知道知识是否能像果实一样握在手中，想知道知识是否能像药物一样配给给一个病态的世界。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 840, MARCH 12, 1979
9:28 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Now. You had better end the session. I will however give you a few notes. First of all, within your system, relatively speaking, there can be and usually is, some lapse between my own thought and Ruburt's verbalization. The control over the physical system is not as complete as when Ruburt is in control of it. When I am deeply considering a matter therefore, there is a pause on my part added to the natural lapse mentioned earlier. We will discuss this more fully for it will lead us into some other matters in which you will be interested. I do not operate Ruburt's controls as automatically as he does, you see.
quotes.translation: 好了。你们最好结束这节课。不过我会给你们一些说明。首先，在你们的系统内，相对而言，在我自己的念头和鲁柏的语言表达之间，可能存在且通常存在一些时间差。对物质系统的控制不如鲁柏自己控制时那么完整。因此，当我深入思考某件事时，在我这边会有一个停顿，加上前面提到的自然时间差。我们会更充分地讨论这一点，因为它会引导我们进入一些你们会感兴趣的其他事情。你看，我操作鲁柏的控制装置并不像他自己操作那样自动化。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: The authors instead tell you to be positive, compassionate, strong, optimistic, filled with joy and enthusiasm, without telling you what to do to get out of the predicament you may be in, and without understanding the vicious circle that may seem to entrap you. Such books, again, while sometimes of value, do not explain how thoughts and emotions cause reality. They do not take into consideration the multidimensional aspects of the self or the fact that ultimately each personality, while following definite general laws, must still find and follow his or her own way of adapting these to personal circumstances.
quotes.translation: 这些作者反而告诉你要积极、慈悲、坚强、乐观、充满喜悦与热忱，却不告诉你如何摆脱可能身陷的困境，也不理解那看似将你困住的恶性循环。这类书籍虽有时也有价值，却未能解释思想与情感如何创造实相。它们既不考虑自我的多维度面向，也不考虑一个事实：每个个体在遵循特定普遍法则的同时，终究必须找到并遵循自己将这些法则运用于个人境遇的方式。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Telepathy on this level is the intangible, nonmaterial communication of inner energy, or inside energy, to the physical materialization of itself within the physical field. This elementary telepathy is not the communication of thought as such, but is the communication of intent, desire and purpose. It is therefore the communication of inner energy to various still-forming aspects of itself, a blazing, so to speak, of invisible paths or bridges. And at the same time the laying down of these intangible paths serve as the inner framework over which or upon which future physical lines or structures will be laid.
quotes.translation: 此层面的心灵感应，是内在能量（或内在能量）在物质场域内对自身进行物质化显现时那种无形无质的沟通。这种基本的心灵感应并非思想本身的沟通，而是意图、渴望与目的的沟通。因此，它是内在能量与其自身各种仍在成形中的面向之间的沟通，可以说是无形路径或桥梁的炽燃铺设。同时，这些无形路径的铺设构成了内在架构，未来的物质线条或结构将在此架构之上或之中被铺设。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Your ego gains assurance from what seems to be the memory of its immediate past. A man who loses memory of past events feels insecure and lost, but other types of personality gestalts operate far differently. Instead of a time sequence that governs or seems to govern thought, mental activity of any kind, and overt action, you have associative processes, offshoots, and possibilities. To some extent you can gain an idea of this through an examination of your own stream of consciousness. But the comparison is highly superficial, for here too continuity reigns, even though associations are given greater play.
quotes.translation: 你的自我从看似对直接过去的记忆中获取安全感。一个失去过往事件记忆的人会感到不安与迷失，但其他类型的人格完形运作方式则大相径庭。并非由时间序列支配或看似支配着思想、任何心智活动及外在行为，你拥有的是联想过程、分支与可能性。在某种程度上，你可以通过检视自己的意识流来获得一些概念。但这种比较极为肤浅，因为即便联想被赋予更大的发挥空间，连续性依然在此主导。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The first "object" was an almost unendurable mass, though it had no weight, and it exploded, instantaneously beginning processes that formed the universe--but no time was involved. The process that you might imagine took up eons occurred in the twinkling of an eye, and the initial objective materialization of the massive thought of All That Is burst into reality. In your terms this was a physical explosion--but in the terms of the consciousnesses involved in that breakthrough, this was experienced as a triumphant "first" inspirational frenzy, a breakthrough into another kind of being (most intently).
quotes.translation: 第一个"物体"是一团几乎难以承受的质块，虽无重量，却瞬间爆炸，开启了形成宇宙的过程——但其中并无时间介入。你可能以为耗费了数劫的过程，实则发生于眨眼之间，而"一切万有"那庞大思想的首次客观物质化，便爆发为实相。用你们的话说，这是一场物理爆炸——但以参与那次突破的意识而言，这被体验为一次胜利的"首次"灵感狂潮，一次向另一种存有形态的突破（极其专注地）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Religious, scientific, medical, and cultural communications stress the existence of danger, minimize the purpose of the species or of any individual member of it, or see mankind as the one erratic, half-insane member of an otherwise orderly realm of nature. Any or all of the above beliefs are held by various systems of thought. All of these, however, strain the individual's biological sense of integrity, reinforce ideas of danger, and shrink the area of psychological safety that is necessary to maintain the quality possible in life. The body's defense systems become confused to varying degrees.
quotes.translation: 宗教、科学、医学及文化的传播都强调危险的存在，贬低物种或任何个体成员的目的，或将人类视为本应有序的自然界中那个反复无常、半疯癫的成员。上述信念中的任何一种或全部，都为各种思想体系所持有。然而，所有这些都扭曲了个体生物性的完整感，强化了危险观念，并压缩了维持生命可能品质所必需的心理安全区域。身体防御系统因此陷入不同程度的混乱。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 805, MAY 16, 1977
9:28 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Now. In this existence, when you see a picture in your mind, and when there is strong emotion and vivid desire behind it, it will be constructed. There will be a time lapse within this system, but in other systems there may be no time lapse... and your thought may be instantly transposed into reality. Therefore, now you must learn the nature of your thoughts and how to handle energy.
quotes.translation: 现在。在此存在中，当你心中浮现一幅画面，且背后带有强烈情感与生动渴望时，它将被建构出来。在此系统内会有时间延迟，但在其他系统中可能没有时间延迟……你的思想可能瞬间转化为实相。因此，现在你必须学习你思想的本质，以及如何处理能量。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The basic source of your difficulty was not new. It did not even, except superficially, represent a dangerous lapse or relapse on your part, into those truly dangerous and quite disastrous negative battles of thoughts, which eventually in any individual can and often do lead the integrated self into annihilation. The illness did represent, however, a needed warning, materialized into physical reality as illness. A warning that after all there had been a recent tendency on your part, though slight, to slide into negative thinking. The illness was meant to bring you up short, to make you think.
quotes.translation: 你困境的根本源头并非新事。即便表面上看似危险，也并非你真正陷入那些最终会导致个体自我湮灭的、灾难性的负面思想战役——这种思想战役在任何个体身上都可能且经常发生。这场疾病确实是一个必要的警告，以疾病的形式物质化为物理实相。这个警告提醒你：毕竟你最近有滑向负面思维的倾向，尽管很轻微。这场疾病旨在让你猛然醒悟，促使你思考。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The type of telepathy which is most commonly thought of as telepathy is of course not the elemental type of which I spoke earlier, but a different variety. This particular, more advanced telepathy could not and did not occur in its most complicated form until the development within your plane of psychically developed personalities. This development involved separations and concentrations of energy into self-conscious identities. This development was an advancement, yet this advancement led also to a separation and objectifying of consciousness, into dual segments of subject and object.
quotes.translation: 通常被视为心电感应（telepathy）的那种类型，当然不是我之前谈到的元素型，而是另一种变体。这种更高级的心电感应，在你们层面发展出具有心灵能力的人格之前，不可能也无法以其最复杂的形式发生。这种发展涉及将能量分离并集中为自我意识的本体。这是一种进步，但此进步也导致了意识的分裂与客体化，分裂为主体和客体的二元部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Poor health is indeed caused mainly by habitual destructive thought patterns which directly affect the physical system, because of the particular range within the electromagnetic system in which they fall; and despite any objections I will stick by this statement. The bad health, for example, does not occur first, resulting in unhealthy thoughts. It is indeed the other way around.
quotes.translation: 健康不佳确实主要由习惯性的破坏性思维模式引起，这些模式因其在电磁系统内所落入的特定范围而直接影响身体系统；无论有何异议，我坚持这一说法。例如，并非先有健康不佳，然后才产生不健康的想法。事实恰恰相反。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Before you can be allowed into systems of reality that are more extensive and open, you must first learn to handle energy, and see, through physical materiaizationls, the concrete result of thought and emotion. As a child forms mud pies from dirt, so you form your civilization out of thoughts and emotions, and then see what you have created, and you must deal with it on its terms.
quotes.translation: 在你们被允许进入更广阔、更开放的实相系统之前，必须先学会处理能量，并通过物质显化看到思想与情感的具体结果。如同孩子用泥土捏泥饼，你们用思想和情感塑造出你们的文明，然后看到自己所创造之物，并必须按其规则与之相处。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The differences between any of those systems of thought and Christianity may be so apparent that the similarities escape you. You may follow one of the schools of Buddhism in which great stress is laid upon the denial of the body, discipline of the flesh, and the avoidance of desire. These elements are quite characteristic of Christianity also, of course, but they may appear more palatable, exotic, or reasonable coming from a source foreign to your childhood education. So you may leap from one to the other, shouting emancipation and feeling yourself quite free of old limiting ideas.
quotes.translation: 这些思想体系与基督教之间的差异可能如此明显，以至于相似之处被你们忽略。你们可能追随某个强调否定身体、克制肉体、避免欲望的佛教流派。这些元素当然也是基督教的典型特征，但当它们来自与你们童年教育相异的源头时，可能显得更易接受、更具异国情调或更合理。于是你们可能从一个跳到另一个，高喊解放，感觉自己完全摆脱了旧的限制性观念。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The officials of the Roman Catholic Church altered many records -- cleansing them, in their terms, of anything that might suggest pagan practices, or nature worship as they thought of it. In terms of your civilization, nature and spirit became divided so that you encounter the events of your lives largely in that context. To some degree or another, then, you must feel divorced from your bodies and from the events of nature. The great sweeps of emotional identification with nature itself do not sustain you, therefore. You study those processes as if you somehow stood apart from them.
quotes.translation: 罗马天主教会的官员篡改了许多记录——用他们的话说，是清除了任何可能暗示异教习俗或他们所谓的自然崇拜的内容。就你们的文明而言，自然与精神被割裂开来，以至于你们在很大程度上是在这种背景下遭遇生活中的事件。因此，你们或多或少必然感到与自己的身体及自然事件脱节。与自然本身产生情感认同的宏大浪潮不再支撑你们。于是你们研究这些过程，仿佛自己置身其外。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 821, FEBRUARY 20, 1978
9:30 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: They were not trying any experiments at the time, but their energies were free, and reaching outward. Often you are trying your psychic wings, so to speak, when you do not realize it. This is rather important. What you call the astral body does much of its traveling when you are not consciously trying to experiment. Telepathically you receive thoughts when you are not trying to receive them. This does not mean that conscious experimentation will not work however. It simply means that what you are trying to do. consciously. you have been doing without knowing it all the while.
quotes.translation: 他们当时并未刻意进行任何实验，但他们的能量是自由的，并向外延伸。通常，当你在尝试你的心灵翅膀时，你并未意识到这一点。这相当重要。当你没有有意识地尝试实验时，你所谓的星光体已经进行了大量旅行。当你没有试图接收思想时，你已通过心灵感应接收到了思想。但这并不意味着有意识的实验不会奏效。它只是意味着你正在有意识地尝试去做的事，其实你一直在不知不觉中做着。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Man's thoughts no longer seemed to have any effect upon nature because in his mind he saw himself apart from it. In an ambiguous fashion, while concentrating upon nature's exterior aspects in a very conscious manner, he still ended up denying the conscious powers of his own mind. He became blind to the connection between his thoughts and his physical environment and experience.
quotes.translation: 人类的思想似乎不再对自然产生任何影响，因为在他们的心中，他们将自己视为与自然分离的。以一种模糊的方式，当他们非常刻意地专注于自然的外在面貌时，他们最终却否定了自己心灵的有意识力量。他们对自己思想与物理环境及经验之间的联系变得视而不见。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Ruburt does extremely well with interior sound, and so I use that method rather than, say, an image to make my independent existence known. Now Ruburt called me originally (last Saturday night) at unconscious levels because he was upset with "earth programming." He thought that you needed some help from the outside, so to speak. That intent set up certain signals that reached into other realities or stations, and I answered. I was not speaking to Ruburt personally when he heard me, but addressing myself to the world at large in a program that was indeed picked up by others.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏在处理内在声音方面做得非常好，所以我使用那种方法，而不是说，用一个图像来表明我独立的存在。现在，鲁柏最初（上周六晚上）在无意识层面召唤我，是因为他对“地球编程”感到不安。他认为你需要一些来自外界的帮助，可以这么说。那个意图设定了某些信号，这些信号到达了其他实相或站点，而我回应了。当他听到我时，我并非在对鲁柏个人说话，而是在一个确实被其他人接收到的节目中，向整个世界发表讲话。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 711
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: "Nothing in the universe is ever lost, or mislaid, or wasted, so the energy of your own thoughts, while they are still your own thoughts, helps to form the natural attributes of physical realities that you do not perceive. So is your own world formed by units of consciousness. Its natural elements are the glistening remnants of other units of consciousness that you do not see."
quotes.translation: “宇宙中没有什么是永远丢失、放错地方或被浪费的，所以你思想本身的能量，当它们仍然是你的思想时，有助于形成你无法感知的物理实相的自然属性。你的世界就是这样由意识单位形成的。它的自然元素是你看不见的其他意识单位的闪闪发光的残余。”

quotes.text: （His mothers home was dark, of course, and not wanting to disturb her merely to leave the tomatoes, Jimmy left them on the back porch. His father had died a few months ago, and while he stood in the backyard, a place he knew and had loved since childhood, Jimmy thought to himself "Now, if I could see my fathers apparition, then I could tell Ma, and she'd feel a lot better," or words to that effect. Jimmy waited beside the dark and quiet house, staring out into the backyard, but he saw and heard nothing unusual. He is well acquainted with the literature on psychic phenomena.
quotes.translation: （他母亲的房子当然很暗，为了不打扰她而只是放西红柿，吉米把它们留在了后门廊。他父亲几个月前去世了，当他站在后院——一个他从小就知道并喜爱的地方——吉米心想：“现在，如果我能看到我父亲的鬼魂，那我就可以告诉妈妈，她会感觉好多了，”或者类似的话。吉米在黑暗而寂静的房子旁等待，凝视着后院，但他没有看到或听到任何不寻常的东西。他对灵异现象的文献非常熟悉。）

quotes.text: You can automatically choose good friends through the use of your abilities... Don't bother now with thoughts of the origins of your abilities... Avoid cultism. Also don't let your imagination follow along the lines of possession... Fear of this creates a state in which a semblance of that can be caused. Most cases of possession are not caused by spirits... although it does exist in those terms... usually caused by those who request it or are too lazy to live their own lives within... Such people then issue a blank invite to control the body they no longer wish to control.
quotes.translation: 你可以通过运用你的能力自动选择好朋友……现在不要费心去想你的能力的起源……避免崇拜主义。也不要让你的想象力沿着附身的方向发展……对此的恐惧会创造一种状态，在这种状态下，可以引发类似附身的情况。大多数附身案例并非由灵体引起……尽管它确实以那些术语存在……通常是由那些请求附身或太懒惰以至于不愿活出自己内在生命的人引起的……这样的人随后会发出一个空白邀请，来控制他们不再希望控制的身体。

quotes.text: You can readily see, therefore, how important your subjective feelings really are. This knowledge -- that your universe is idea construction -- can immediately give you clues that enable you to change your environment and circumstances beneficially. When you do not understand the nature of the soul, and do not realize that your thoughts and feelings form physical reality, then you feel powerless to change it. In later chapters of this book, I hope to give you some practical information that will enable you to alter practically the very nature and structure of daily life.
quotes.translation: 因此你很容易看出，你的主观感受有多么重要。这个认知——即你的宇宙是意念的建构——能立即给你线索，让你得以有利地改变你的环境和处境。当你不了解灵魂的本质，也不明白你的思想和情感形成物质实相时，你就会感到无力改变它。在本书后面的章节中，我希望给你一些实用的信息，让你能实际改变日常生活的本质和结构。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: It is impossible to tell you of the emotional reality of such an experience. You will have to discover it for yourself. Such bridge beliefs often allow you to perceive the "invisible" beliefs mentioned this evening, and these can then appear to you as a revelation. On second thought, however, you will realize that another belief blocked that one from your view, but that you were always aware of it; and that in a strange way it was also invisible because you took it for granted. You did not consider it a belief about reality but as reality itself, and never questioned it.
quotes.translation: 我无法告诉你这种体验的情感实相。你必须自己去发现它。这种桥梁信念常常让你能感知今晚提到的"隐形"信念，而这些信念会像启示一样出现在你面前。然而再一想，你会意识到是另一个信念挡住了这个信念的视线，但你其实一直都知道它；而且奇怪的是，它之所以隐形，是因为你把它视为理所当然。你不把它看作关于实相的信念，而当作实相本身，从未质疑过它。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: （As John explained company politics to us now, he said he thought he saw a connection with Seth's mention of an "apartment, "on page 235. It will be remembered that at first John could offer nothing for this data. Mr. McKeown, John said, had gone so far as to invite him up to his private room at the Sheraton-Hilton, for more secluded talks involving the company. At the time John was somewhat surprised that a high company official would pay particular attention to him. It was during such meetings as this, and those at the Manger, that the sounding-out process commenced.
quotes.translation: （当约翰现在向我们解释公司政治时，他说他认为自己看到了与赛斯在第235页提到的"公寓"之间的联系。记得起初约翰对这个资料无法提供任何信息。约翰说，麦基翁先生甚至邀请他到自己位于喜来登-希尔顿酒店的私人房间，进行更私密的公司会谈。当时约翰有点惊讶，一位公司高层官员会特别关注他。正是在这样的会议，以及在曼格酒店的会议中，试探过程开始了。

quotes.text: New paragraph (as Seth often declares). When you identify with only one particular level of your thought processes, however, the others -- when you sense them -- appear alien. You begin to feel threatened, determined to uphold your old ideas of selfhood. Plants grow many leaves. One leaf does not threaten the existence of others, and the plant is not jealous of its own foliage. So there is no need to protect your own individuality because it may send out other shoots into probable realities. This is simply the self growing in different directions, spreading its seeds.
quotes.translation: 新段落（正如赛斯经常宣称的那样）。然而，当你只认同自己思维过程的某一个特定层次时，其他层次——当你感觉到它们时——就会显得陌生。你开始感到受威胁，决心维护你原有的自我观念。植物长出许多叶子。一片叶子不会威胁其他叶子的存在，植物也不会嫉妒自己的枝叶。所以没有必要保护你的个体性，因为它可能会向可能实相中伸出其他枝芽。这只是自我在不同方向上的生长，播撒它的种子。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: [Pause.] A loss of an advantage, or period of poor luck." See the explanation're the black cat on page 328. Seth's additional data here conjures up the thought that the neighbor's black cat also serves as the classic symbol of bad or poor luck; the connection here being the failing health of my father and the failing health in a more drastic way of Mr. Meeker, the father-in-law of my brother Loren. It was while at Lorens that my mother sent us the greeting card. When she called us on August 14, she, of course, discussed the health both of my father and Mr. Meeker.
quotes.translation: [停顿。]"失去优势，或运气不佳的时期。"参见第328页关于黑猫的解释。赛斯在这里的额外资料让人联想到邻居的黑猫也象征着厄运或坏运气；这里的联系是我父亲日益恶化的健康，以及我兄弟洛伦的岳父米克先生更急剧恶化的健康。正是在洛伦家时，我母亲寄来了那张问候卡。当她8月14日打电话给我们时，当然讨论了父亲和米克先生的健康状况。

quotes.text: I have said before that thoughts exist in such a dimension. Constructive thoughts do not simply affect the system for good in some sort of a generalized fashion, nor do destructive thoughts simply happen to affect the system directly.
quotes.translation: 我以前说过，思想存在于这样的维度中。建设性的思想并非仅仅以某种笼统的方式对系统产生好的影响，破坏性的思想也并非恰好直接影响到系统。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: The "true facts" are that you exist in this life and outside it simultaneously. You are "between lives" and "in lives" at once. The deeper dimensions of reality are such that your thoughts and actions not only affect the life you know, but also reach into all of those other simultaneous existences. What you think now is unconsciously perceived by some hypothetical 14th-century self. The psyche is open-ended. No system is closed, psychological systems least of all. Your life is a dreaming experience to other portions of your greater reality which focus elsewhere.
quotes.translation: “真实情况”是，你同时存在于这一生及其之外。你同时“处于生死之间”和“处于生命之中”。实相的更深维度使得你的思想和行为不仅影响你所知的人生，也触及所有那些同时存在的其他存在。你现在的想法会被某个假设的14世纪自我无意识地感知。心灵是开放的。没有封闭的系统，心理系统尤其如此。对你更大实相中聚焦于别处的其他部分而言，你的人生是一场梦的经历。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Ruburt was well prepared for the call by then, and for the visit. Now the visit and Ruburt's earlier feelings and thoughts were part of the same event, except that his subjective experience gave him clues as to the inner processes by which all events take place. More is involved than the simple question: Did he perceive the visit precognitively? More is involved than the question: Did he perceive his information directly from the minds of his friends, or from the letter itself, which had already been mailed, of course, and was on its way to Ruburt at the time?
quotes.translation: 到那时，鲁柏已经为那个电话和那次来访做好了充分准备。如今，那次来访和鲁柏早先的感受与思想是同一事件的一部分，只不过他的主观体验为他提供了线索，让他了解所有事件发生的内在过程。这涉及的不只是简单的问题：他是否预知了那次来访？也不只是这个问题：他是否直接从朋友们的思想中，或从信件本身获取了信息？那封信当然已经寄出，当时正在寄往鲁柏的途中。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: They are all interwoven and yet separate, and if you look closely at your own physical universe you will see that here also matter is interbound with matter. You see but one form of matter. There are diversities within each system as there are diversities within your own, that are chemical universes where thoughts are patterned in ways that would be incomprehensible to you.
quotes.translation: 它们全都交织在一起，却又各自独立。如果你仔细观察自己的物质宇宙，你会发现这里的物质也是相互关联的。你只看到一种物质形态。每个系统内部都有多样性，就像你自己的系统内部也有多样性一样，那里是化学宇宙，思想以你无法理解的方式被模式化。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: The most rejuvenating idea of all, and the greatest step to any true illumination, is the realization that your exterior life springs from the invisible world of your reality through your conscious thoughts and beliefs, for then you realize the power of your individuality and identity. You are immediately presented with choices. You can no longer see yourself as a victim of circumstances. Yet the conscious mind arose precisely to open up choices, to free you from a one-road experience, to let you use your creativity to form diversified, varied comprehensions.
quotes.translation: 最具恢复活力的想法，也是通往任何真正启迪的最大一步，是认识到你的外在生活源于你实相的无形世界，通过你有意识的思想和信念而来，因为这样你就能意识到你个体性和身份的力量。你立刻面临选择。你不能再把自己视为环境的受害者。然而，有意识心智的产生正是为了开启选择，将你从单一道路的体验中解放出来，让你运用创造力形成多样化、多角度的理解。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: X has lost its strength and Y is under the siege of Planck. Planck's forces (Pause), triumph over the old values but zero gobbles some of Planck's men, the integer minus 7, and to work out, psi must be confused for a moment with 8. The mistake is found, and Y is free. Too bad you neglected the psi factor. You thought 7 gobbled it up. 37I will not stand alone in that location. It is besieged by truth to the 3rd power, truth being one, hand in hand with 7. Three C (E?), 3C, over 9 to the 7th power, will temporarily equate with 9 over 137, might give you truth.
quotes.translation: X已失去力量，Y正受普朗克围攻。普朗克的力量（停顿）战胜了旧价值，但零吞噬了普朗克的一些人马，整数减7，要解决这个问题，ψ必须暂时与8混淆。错误被发现，Y获得自由。可惜你忽略了ψ因子。你以为7吞噬了它。37不会在那个位置孤立无援。它被真理的3次方围攻，真理为一，与7携手。3C（E？），3C，除以9的7次方，将暂时等于9除以137，或许能给你真理。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: You can request that the thought content of your mind be translated into an intense image, symbolically representing individual thoughts and the overall mental landscape, then take out what you do not like and replace it with more positive images. This does not mean that this inner landscape must always be completely sunny, but it does mean that it should be well balanced.
quotes.translation: 你可以请求将你头脑中的思想内容转化为一个强烈的意象，象征性地代表个体思想和整体心灵景观，然后取出你不喜欢的部分，用更积极的意象替换。这并不意味着这个内在景观必须总是阳光明媚，但它确实意味着它应该保持良好平衡。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is not to say that you cannot understand the nature of the universe to some extent, but the answers lie in the natures of your own minds, in the processes of individual creativity, in studies that ask questions like: "Where did this thought come from? Where does it go? What effect does it have upon myself or others? How do I know how to dream, when I have never been taught to do so? How do I speak without understanding the mechanisms? Why do I feel that I have an eternal reality, when it is obvious that I was physically born and will physically die?"
quotes.translation: 这并非说你无法在一定程度上理解宇宙的本质，但答案存在于你心灵的本性中，存在于个体创造力的过程中，存在于那些提出以下问题的研究中：“这个想法从何而来？它去往何处？它对我或他人有何影响？我从未被教导如何做梦，却知道如何做梦，这是为什么？我如何在不理解机制的情况下说话？为何我明明有物理上的出生和死亡，却感觉自己拥有永恒的真实？”
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... Historically, of course, you follow a one-line pattern of thought, so you see a picture in which fish left the oceans and became reptiles; from these mammals eventually appeared, and apes and men. That is, I admit, a simple statement, but it is the way most people think evolution occurred. The terms of "progression" are tricky. You never imagine the situation being reversed, for example. Few of you ever imagine a conscious reptilian man. It seems to you that the direction you took is the only direction that could have been taken.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……从历史上看，当然，你们遵循一条线性的思维模式，所以你们看到一幅画面：鱼离开海洋变成爬行动物；从这些爬行动物中最终出现了哺乳动物，然后是猿和人。我承认，这是一个简单的陈述，但这是大多数人认为进化发生的方式。“进步”这个术语很微妙。例如，你们从未想象过情况会颠倒过来。很少有人想象过一个有意识的爬行动物人。在你们看来，你们所走的方向是唯一可能的方向。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: I was about your establishment earlier, and Ruburt was right. There was a purpose in his misreading. Actually, subconsciously he picked up my thoughts on this occasion. He picked them up a few minutes earlier but his mind waited for a suitable occasion to transpose the word spacious in connection with the present. The spacious present is an excellent term. In actuality there is only a spacious present, so spacious that it cannot be explored all at once in your terms, hence your arbitrary division of it into larger rooms of past, present and future.
quotes.translation: 我之前提到过你们的“建立”，鲁柏是对的。他误读是有目的的。实际上，他在潜意识中捕捉到了我这次的想法。他早了几分钟捕捉到，但他的心灵等待一个合适的时机，将“广阔”一词与当下联系起来。“广阔的当下”是一个极好的术语。实际上，只有一个广阔的当下，如此广阔以至于在你们的术语中无法一次性探索完毕，因此你们武断地将其分割成过去、现在和未来这些更大的房间。
