quotes.text: The psyche is not male or female. In your system of beliefs, however, it is often identified as feminine, along with the artistic productions that emerge from its creativity. In that context, the day hours and waking consciousness are thought of as masculine, along with the sun -- while the nighttime, the moon, and the dreaming consciousness are considered feminine or passive. In the same manner, aggression is usually understood to be violent assertive action, male-oriented, while female elements are identified in terms of the nurturing principle.
quotes.translation: 心灵没有男性或女性之分。然而，在你们的信仰体系中，它常被认定为女性特质，连同从其创造力中产生的艺术产物。在这种背景下，白天和清醒意识被认为是男性特质，与太阳一起——而夜晚、月亮和做梦意识则被认为是女性或被动特质。同样，攻击性通常被理解为暴力的、自信的行动，以男性为导向，而女性元素则被认定为滋养原则。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: A transitory period is currently taking place, in which women seem to seek the promiscuous sexual freedom more generally granted to men. It is believed that males are naturally promiscuous, aroused by sexual stimuli almost completely divorced from any complementary "deeper" response. The male, then, is thought to want sex whether or not he has any love response to the woman in question -- or sometimes to desire her precisely because he does not love her. In such cases, sex becomes not an expression of love, but an expression of derision or scorn.
quotes.translation: 目前正经历一个过渡时期，女性似乎寻求通常更普遍赋予男性的滥交性自由。人们相信男性天生滥交，被几乎完全脱离任何互补“更深”反应的性刺激所激发。因此，男性被认为无论是否对所涉女性有爱的反应都想要性——或者有时恰恰因为不爱她而渴望她。在这种情况下，性不再是爱的表达，而是嘲弄或蔑视的表达。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The thought may be rejected, but there will be something to reject. The thought may be relatively meaningless to any given probable self and it may be very fleeting. The probable selves in one way or another will react, forming other action events. Constant creativity applies to every system, then, and what you create in one system has its effects in other systems also.
quotes.translation: 这个想法可能被拒绝，但总会有东西可拒绝。这个想法对任何给定的可能自我可能相对无意义，并且可能非常短暂。可能自我会以某种方式反应，形成其他行动事件。持续的创造力适用于每个系统，因此你在一个系统中创造的东西也会在其他系统中产生影响。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Dogmas or systems of thought that tell you to rise above your emotions can be misleading -- even, in your terms, somewhat dangerous. Such theories are based upon the concept that there is something innately disruptive, base, or wrong in man's emotional nature, while the soul is always depicted as being calm, "perfect," passive and unfeeling. Only the most lofty, blissful awareness is allowed. Yet the soul is above all a fountain of energy, creativity, and action that shows its characteristics in life precisely through the ever-changing emotions.
quotes.translation: 告诉你要超越情绪的教条或思想体系可能具有误导性——甚至，用你们的话来说，有些危险。这类理论基于这样的概念：人类情感本质中存在着某种天生具有破坏性、低劣或错误的东西，而灵魂则总被描绘成平静、"完美"、被动且无情的。只允许最崇高、最极乐的觉知存在。然而，灵魂首先是能量、创造力和行动的源泉，正是通过不断变化的情感来展现其特性。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Now: It is true that habitual thoughts of love, optimism and self-acceptance are better for you than their opposites; but again, your beliefs about yourself will automatically attract thoughts that are consistent with your ideas. There is as much natural aggressiveness in love as there is in hate. Hate is a distortion of such a normal force, the result of your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 现在：的确，习惯性的爱、乐观和自我接纳的想法比其对立面对你更好；但同样，你对自己的信念会自动吸引与你观念一致的想法。爱中蕴含的天然攻击性与恨中一样多。恨是对这种正常力量的扭曲，是你信念的结果。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The individuals continue to exist, and express themselves according to their development in other fields not connected with yours, when they are finished on your plane. No particular identity is ever lost. If so the whole process would be meaningless. And in other ways to lesser degrees, through abstract thought, through art of any kind, the physical human being, having been formed by consciousness, in his own way then working through and with matter, constructs other fields or planes of attraction, which according to their abilities expand.
quotes.translation: 个体在你们的层面结束后，会继续存在，并根据他们在其他与你们无关领域的发展来表达自己。没有任何特定的身份会丢失。如果那样，整个过程将毫无意义。通过抽象思维、通过任何形式的艺术，以其他方式在较小程度上，由意识形成的物质人类，以自己的方式通过物质运作并与物质合作，构建其他吸引力的场域或层面，这些场域根据其能力而扩展。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Often you quite consciously decide to bury a thought or an idea that might cause you to alter your behavior, because it does not seem to fit in with limiting ideas that you already hold. Listen to your own train of thought as you go about your days. What suggestions and ideas are you giving yourself? Realize that these will be materialized in your personal experience.
quotes.translation: 你常常有意识地决定埋葬一个可能促使你改变行为的想法或观念，因为它似乎不符合你已经持有的限制性观念。在你日常活动中倾听自己的思绪。你在给自己什么建议和想法？要认识到这些将在你的个人经验中具体化。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: There is no separate field that combines all of that information, or applies the facts of one discipline to the facts of another discipline, so overall, science, with its brand of rational thought, can offer no even, suggestive, hypothetical, comprehensive ideas of what reality is. It seems that each individual is in effect isolated in certain vital regards -- given, say, a genetic heritage and a certain amount of unspecified energy with which to run the body's machinery (intently). Intent, purpose, or desire do not apply in that picture.
quotes.translation: 没有一个独立的领域能够整合所有这些信息，或将一个学科的事实应用于另一个学科的事实，因此总体而言，科学以其理性思维的品牌，无法提供任何关于现实是什么的、哪怕是暗示性的、假设性的、全面的概念。似乎每个个体在某些关键方面实际上都是孤立的——比如说，被赋予一份遗传遗产和一定量的未指定能量来运转身体的机制（专注地）。意图、目的或欲望在那幅图景中并不适用。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 1. From my dream notebook: (See my painting at end of Session 4.)

quotes.text: （Correct on both points. Bill went upstairs to talk to the men in the Ad department. Though I have been in the newspaper building, I did not know that these offices were upstairs. Bill's own office is downstairs, as I knew, and if I thought about it at all I assumed that all his business took place there. I had no idea that Bill had any connection with the upstairs offices at all, since the editorial work is done there, and he has nothing to do with that at all. Peg, who works up there, has told me often that she never sees him upstairs.）
quotes.translation: （两点都正确。比尔上楼去和广告部的人谈话。虽然我去过报社大楼，但我不知道那些办公室在楼上。如我所知，比尔自己的办公室在楼下，如果我曾想过的话，我会假设他所有的工作都在那里进行。我完全不知道比尔与楼上办公室有任何关联，因为编辑工作在那里进行，而他与此完全无关。在楼上工作的佩格经常告诉我，她从未在楼上见过他。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: You must remember, of course, that the use of clocks is a fairly recent phenomenon. (Pause.) Men thought in terms of rhythms of the time, or of flowing time, not of time in sections that were arbitrary. So as far as creaturehood is concerned, you have adapted to a time environment that you yourselves have formed. Creative people, again, are often aware of those connections, at least at certain levels, and Ruburt in particular has always felt that way to some extent. You have largely buried your own natural feelings in that direction.
quotes.translation: 你必须记住，当然，时钟的使用是相当晚近的现象。（停顿。）人类过去以时间的韵律或流动的时间来思考，而非以任意分割的时间段落来思考。因此就生物性而言，你们已经适应了自己所形成的时间环境。有创造力的人往往至少在某些层面上能觉察到这些关联，而鲁柏尤其一直有某种程度的这种感受。你们在很大程度上埋葬了自己在这方面的自然感受。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: Earlier I compared your thoughts to viruses (in the last session). Think of them now as living electromagnetic cells, differing from the physical cells in your body only in the nature of their materialization. Your thoughts direct the overall functioning of your body's cells, even though you do not consciously know how those cells operate. That work is unconscious.
quotes.translation: 早些时候（在上一节中）我曾将你们的思想比作病毒。现在把它们想象成活的电磁细胞，与你们身体中的物质细胞仅在其物质化的性质上有所不同。你们的思想指导着身体细胞的整体运作，即使你们并未有意识地知道这些细胞如何运作。那项工作是无意识的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Not at all. You could counter such an attack in several ways that do not involve killing. You would not be in such a hypothetical situation to begin with unless violent thoughts of your own, faced or unfaced, had attracted it to you. But once it is a fact, and according to the circumstances, many methods could be used. Because you consider aggression synonymous with violence, you may not understand that aggressive -- forceful, active, mental or spoken -- commands for peace could save your life in such a case; yet they could.
quotes.translation: 完全不是这样。你可以通过几种不涉及杀戮的方式来应对这样的攻击。你一开始就不会处于这种假设情境中，除非你自己那些面对或未面对过的暴力思想将其吸引到你身边。但一旦它成为事实，并且根据具体情况，可以使用许多方法。因为你们将攻击性与暴力视为同义词，所以可能不明白——在这种情况下，对和平发出攻击性的、有力的、主动的、精神或言语上的命令可以拯救你的生命；然而它们确实可以。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Nor do such projections involve necessarily journeys through space as you know it. There are systems, extremely vivid in intensity, that have no existence in physical reality. It is now thought, I believe, that time and space are basically one, but they are both a part of something else. They are merely the camouflage patterns by which you perceive reality. Space, as you experience it in the dream state, comes much closer to reality.
quotes.translation: 这种投射也不一定涉及你们所知的穿越空间的旅程。有些系统极为生动强烈，在物质实相中并不存在。我相信，现在人们认为时间与空间基本上是一体的，但它们都是某种其他事物的一部分。它们只是你们感知实相所凭借的伪装模式。你们在梦境状态中所体验到的空间，要接近实相得多。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: These secondary personalities, known to psychologists, have a much more important place within reality than is suspected. No psychic action is invalid. Every psychic action exists, has an effect and has durability in terms of value fulfillment. Every psychic action, and a psychic action is any psychic happening such as a dream, or thought, that may have no existence in terms of space and time, every psychic action then contains within it the potentialities of value fulfillment, transference, and even energy transformation.
quotes.translation: 心理学家所知的这些次要人格，在实相中的地位远比人们所猜想的更为重要。没有心灵行动是无效的。每个心灵行动都存在、都有其效果，并且在价值完成方面具有持久性。每个心灵行动——而心灵行动是指任何心灵事件，如可能在时空方面没有存在的梦或思想——那么每个心灵行动都包含着价值完成、转移甚至能量转化的潜能。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: You always know what you are doing, even when you do not realize it. Your eye knows it sees, though it cannot see itself except through the use of reflection. In the same way the world as you see it is a reflection of what you are, a reflection not in glass but in three-dimensional reality. You project your thoughts, feelings, and expectations outward, then you perceive them as the outside reality. When it seems to you that others are observing you, you are observing yourself from the standpoint of your own projections.
quotes.translation: 你总是知道自己在做什么，即使你并未意识到。你的眼睛知道它在看，尽管它除了通过反射之外无法看见自己。同样地，你们所看到的世界是你们自身的一个反映，不是玻璃中的反映，而是三维实相中的反映。你们将自己的思想、情感和期望向外投射，然后将其感知为外在实相。当你们似乎觉得别人在观察你们时，你们其实是在从自己的投射角度观察自己。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You ignore certain temperature variations and stirrings of air as imagination, that are instead indicative of such thought-forms. You thrust into the background telepathic communications that often accompany such forms, and you turn aside from all clues that other realities exist quite validly with your own, and that in the midst of one existence you are surrounded by intangible but valid evidence. The very words "life" and "death" serve to limit your understanding, to set up barriers where none intrinsically exist.
quotes.translation: 你忽略某些温度变化和空气扰动，视之为想象，实则这些正是这类思想形态的征兆。你将常伴随这些形态的心灵感应通讯推入背景，回避所有暗示其他实相与你的实相同样有效存在的线索——在一种存在状态中，你被无形却确凿的证据所环绕。"生"与"死"这两个词本身就在限制你的理解，在本无藩篱之处筑起屏障。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Did those "genetically inferior," for example, have the right to reproduce?1 Illness was thought to come like a storm, the result of physical forces against which the individual had little recourse. The "new" Freudian ideas of the unsavory unconscious led further to a new dilemma, for it was then--as it is now--widely believed that as the result of experiences in infancy the subconscious, or unconscious, might very well sabotage the best interests of the conscious personality, and trick it into illness and disaster.
quotes.translation: 例如，那些"基因低劣者"是否有权繁衍？疾病曾被视为如风暴般降临，是个人几乎无法抗拒的物理力量所致。弗洛伊德关于"不洁潜意识"的"新"观念进一步引发了新的困境——因为当时（正如现在）普遍认为，基于婴儿期经历，潜意识或下意识很可能破坏意识人格的最佳利益，诱使其陷入疾病与灾难。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Vitamins, better food, medical attention, may temporarily rejuvenate the body, but unless you change your beliefs it will quickly become swamped again by your feelings of depression. In such a case you must realize that you make your own loneliness, and resolve to change through both thought and action. Action is thought in physical motion, outwardly perceived.
quotes.translation: 维生素、更好的食物、医疗护理或许能暂时焕活身体，但除非你改变信念，否则它很快会再次被你的抑郁感淹没。在这种情况下，你必须认识到自己制造了孤独，并决心通过思想与行动来改变。行动是思想在物质层面的运动，是外在可感知的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: It is useless then to say, "When this life is over I will look back upon my experience and mend my ways." This is like a young man saying, "When I grow old and retire, I will use all those abilities that I am not now developing." You are setting the stage for your "next" life now. The thoughts you think today will in one way or another become the fabric of your next existence. There are no magic words that will make you wise, that will fill you with understanding and compassion, that will expand your consciousness.
quotes.translation: 因此说"此生结束后，我会回顾经历并改正错误"毫无意义。这就像年轻人说"等我年老退休时，会运用所有现在未发展的能力"。你此刻就在为"下一世"铺陈舞台。今日所思所想，终将以某种方式编织成你下一存在的织锦。没有魔法咒语能让你变得智慧，让你充满理解与慈悲，或扩展你的意识。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You must be aware of the contents of your own reasoning mind. Find the ambiguities. Regardless of the nature of your beliefs they are indeed made flesh and material. The miracle of your being cannot escape itself. Your thoughts blossom into events. If you think the world is evil, you will meet with events that seem evil. There are no accidents in cosmic terms, or in terms of the world as you know it. Your beliefs grow as surely in time and space as flowers do. When you realize this you can even feel their growing.
quotes.translation: 你必须觉察自身理性思维的内容。找出其中的矛盾。无论你的信念性质如何，它们确实会化为血肉与物质。你存在的奇迹无法逃避自身。你的思想绽放为事件。若你认为世界是邪恶的，便会遭遇看似邪恶的事件。从宇宙层面或你所知的世界层面而言，没有意外。你的信念在时空中的生长，如同花朵般确凿无疑。当你意识到这一点，甚至能感受到它们在生长。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: "Or, I thought suddenly, it was like seeing a new corner of your own psyche, transformed into trees, grass, flowers, sky and fog ... I felt as if I were viewing that part of myself that I'm always pursuing, the part that is as clear-eyed as a child, fleet, at one with its own knowing. That part of us exists apart from our concerns about careers or business, money, fame, the opinion of family, friends, or the world. It's our direct connection with the universe ... from which we emerge in each moment of our lives.
quotes.translation: "或者，我突然想到，这就像看见自己心灵中某个崭新的角落，化身为树木、青草、花朵、天空与迷雾……我感觉自己仿佛在审视那始终追寻的自我部分——那部分如孩童般目光清澈，迅捷灵动，与自身认知浑然一体。我们体内的那个部分，超脱于对事业、金钱、名声、家人亲友或世界看法的忧虑。它是我们与宇宙的直接联结……我们生命的每个瞬间都从中涌现。"
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: NOTES: SESSION 860
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: They are brought into existence in a subjective manner, but they then are independent action, and as such may continue to exist in duration within the physical field, according to their original electric potential. In turn these thoughts or emotions, as electric actions, can affect other actions; and influence patterns can be set up, and are set up. The subjective habits of individuals are largely responsible for their own attractions to various types of such electric actions, and here indeed like attracts like.
quotes.translation: 它们以主观方式被带入存在，但随后成为独立的行动，并可能根据其原始电位在物质场域内持续存在。这些思想或情感作为电性行动，反过来又能影响其他行动；影响模式可以被建立，也确实被建立了。个体对各类电性行动的不同吸引力主要取决于其主观习性，而在此确实遵循同类相吸的法则。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: These "period pieces," overall, have a particular purpose. By the very nature of consciousness, it seeks to materialize itself in as many dimensions as possible -- to create from itself new levels of awareness, new offshoots. In doing so it creates all reality. Reality, therefore, is always in a state of becoming. The thoughts that you think, for example, in your actor's roles, are still completely unique and lead to new creativity. Certain aspects of your own consciousness could be fulfilled in no other way.
quotes.translation: 这些"时代作品"整体上具有特定目的。意识的本性使其力求在尽可能多的维度中物质化自身——从自身创造出新的意识层次、新的分支。在此过程中它创造了所有实相。因此实相始终处于"形成"状态。例如，你在演员角色中所思所想的思想，依然完全独特并导向新的创造力。你意识的某些面向无法通过其他方式得到实现。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: "It should remind you of the true effortlessness that is in a fashion responsible for your very existence. When you become overly concerned or worried in any area, remember that you are thinking those thoughts while the process of thinking is utterly effortless. That realization alone can further remind you that the conscious mind does not have to have all (underlined) the information required. It only needs to have the faith that means are available--even if those means are beyond its own scope of activity."
quotes.translation: "这应提醒你注意那种真正的毫不费力——它某种程度上正是你存在本身的成因。当你在任何领域过度担忧或焦虑时，请记住：你正在思考这些思想，而思考过程本身完全不费力气。仅此认知就能进一步提醒你：意识心不必掌握所有（划线强调）必要信息。它只需相信方法必然存在——即便这些方法可能超出其自身活动范围。"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: He thought of that realm as eternal and unchanging, a perfect but frozen composite that must indeed inspire men toward achievement on the one hand, and on the other reproach them for their failure, since their achievements must necessarily seem puny in contrast. Plato then saw Framework 2 as a splendid, absolute model in which all the works of man had their initial source. Man himself, according to this concept, could not affect that ideal world one whit. He could, however, use it as a source of inspiration.
quotes.translation: 他将那个领域视为永恒不变、完美却僵化的综合体——它必然一方面激励人类追求成就，另一方面又因成就与理想相比显得渺小而谴责他们的失败。柏拉图因此将框架二视为辉煌的绝对范本，人类所有作品皆源于此。根据此概念，人类自身丝毫无法影响那个理想世界，但可将其作为灵感源泉。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 822, FEBRUARY 22, 1978
9:27 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: My definition of magic is this: Magic is nature unimpeded, or magic is life unimpeded. It is true that your thoughts and emotions and beliefs form the reality that you experience -- but it is also true that this creative construction is, in a manner of speaking, magically formed. That is, the construction of your body and the construction of a world (pause) are produced with the greatest combination of order and spontaneity -- an order and spontaneity that seems hidden rather than apparent (all intently).
quotes.translation: 我对魔法的定义是：魔法即不受阻碍的自然，或魔法即不受阻碍的生命。你的思想、情感与信念确实构成你所体验的实相——但同样真实的是，这种创造性建构在某种意义上是以魔法方式形成的。也就是说，你身体的建构与世界的建构（停顿）都是在秩序与自发性最完美的结合中产生的——这种秩序与自发性看似隐晦而非显明（全神贯注地）。
quotes.book: The Way Toward Health
quotes.source_file: 10-10-10-10The Way Toward Health - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Your question about transmigration (in Session 903 in Dreams) was an excellent one, and did indeed make the matter clear. It is a temptation at times to use more specific scientific terms, but these would be as confusing as the various definitions and classifications (with humor) that you read in the dictionary, so overall we try to hit a "happy medium." (With more humor.)

quotes.text: Your own kind of conscious mind is splendid and unique. It causes you, however, to interpret all other kinds of life according to your own specifications and experience. The complex nature of other animal consciousness escapes you completely. And when you compare your technologies, learning, logical thought, cultures and arts with what you understand of animal experience, there seems no doubt that you are superior and "the Flower of Evolution" -- that all other kinds of life are topped by your existence.
quotes.translation: 你们这类意识心确实卓越而独特。但这导致你们根据自身规格与经验诠释所有其他生命形式。其他动物意识的复杂本质完全超出你们的理解。当你们将自身的技术、学识、逻辑思维、文化与艺术与所理解的动物经验相比较时，似乎毫无疑问你们是优越的"进化之花"——所有其他生命形式都被你们的存在所超越。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This material is leading up to some future discussions, and the nature of action will be most important. Action is as valid whether the act is conscious and voluntary, or whether it occurs within a dream or within a thought. It is as much a reality either way. Again, it is not an outside force. It arises from within the inner vitality of which all camouflage is composed. To some degree it is a result of inner vitality's attempt to completely express itself in materializations, and its inability to do so.
quotes.translation: 这份资料将引导至未来的某些讨论，而行动的本质将至关重要。无论行动是有意识且自愿的，还是发生在梦境或思想中，它都同样有效。无论哪种方式，它都是同样真实的现实。再次强调，它并非外在力量。它源于内在活力，所有幻相皆由这内在活力构成。在某种程度上，它是内在活力试图在物质化中完全表达自身却无法做到的结果。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: He is told the nature of those who participate with him. He realizes they are thought-forms, for example, and his own; but again, thought-forms do possess a certain reality and consciousness. They are not cardboard actors for him to simply push around at will. He must, therefore, take them into consideration, and he has a certain responsibility toward them.
quotes.translation: 他被告知那些与他共同参与者的本质。例如，他意识到他们是思想形态，且是他自己的思想形态；但再次强调，思想形态确实拥有某种真实性和意识。他们并非他可以随意摆布的纸板演员。因此，他必须将他们纳入考量，并对他们负有某种责任。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your thoughts and beliefs and desires form the events that you view on television. If you want to change your world, you must first change your thoughts, expectations, and beliefs. If every reader of this book changed his or her attitudes, even though not one law was rewritten, tomorrow the world would have changed for the better. The new laws would follow.
quotes.translation: 你的思想、信念和欲望形成了你在电视上看到的事件。如果你想改变你的世界，你必须首先改变你的思想、期望和信念。如果这本书的每位读者都改变自己的态度，即使没有一条法律被重写，明天世界也会变得更好。新的法律将会随之而来。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Over two years ago, in Note 2 for Session 801, and in the opening notes for Session 805, I described our decision to add the writing room to the house. All four of us involved in the moving were quite amused, then -- for the friends who helped Jane and me carry the furniture yesterday are the father-and-son contracting team that built the room in the first place.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: In Framework 2 thoughts instantly form patterns. They are the "natural elements" in that psychological environment that mix, merge, and combine to form, if you will, the psychological cells, atoms, and molecules that compose events. In those terms, the physical events that you perceive or experience can be compared to "psychological objects" that appear to exist with a physical concreteness in space and time. Such events usually seem to begin somewhere in space and time, and clearly end there as well.
quotes.translation: 在架构二中，思想瞬间形成模式。它们是那个心理环境中的“自然元素”，混合、融合并组合，若你愿意，可形成构成事件的心理细胞、原子和分子。用这些术语来说，你感知或体验到的物质事件可比作“心理物体”，它们在空间和时间中似乎以物质的具体性存在。这类事件通常似乎始于空间和时间的某处，并同样明确地在那里结束。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: [All That Is] did not separate itself from those worlds, however, for they were created from its thoughts, and each one has divine content. The worlds are all created by that divine content, so that while they are on the one hand exterior, they are on the other also made of divine stuff, and each hypothetical point in your universe (pause) is in direct contact with All That Is in the most basic terms. The knowledge of the whole is within all of its parts--and yet All That Is is more than its parts.
quotes.translation: [一切万有]并未与那些世界分离，因为它们是由其思想创造的，且每一个都含有神圣内容。这些世界都由那神圣内容创造，因此，虽然它们一方面外在，另一方面也由神圣材料构成，而你们宇宙中的每个假设点（停顿）在最基本的层面上都与一切万有直接接触。整体的知识存在于其所有部分之中——然而一切万有又大于其部分。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: For simplicity's sake, let us say that there is a process of change. Those alive in your terms rely at least outwardly on the spoken word. Those who have left and survive, use thought words but do not need to speak, though they may (underlined). As proficiency grows and as the inner senses are more fully used, the necessity for even thought words vanishes.
quotes.translation: 为简单起见，我们假设存在一个变化过程。在你们术语中活着的人至少在外在依赖言语。那些已离开并存活者使用思想语言，但无需说话，尽管他们可以（强调）。随着熟练度的增长以及内在感官的更充分运用，甚至连思想语言的需求也会消失。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: (11:00.) Some of the events that I see connected very clearly with these persons in the future may not, in your physical system, occur. They exist as probabilities, as potentials, actualized in thoughts but not turned into definite physical form. I told you that no events were predetermined. I would have to tune into a future date, in your terms, and probe it with all of its ramifications in order to ascertain which of the probable actions I saw in your earlier would be actualized in your later.
quotes.translation: （11:00。）我看到的某些与这些人未来清晰相关的事件，在你们的物质系统中可能不会发生。它们作为可能性、作为潜能而存在，在思想中得以实现，但尚未转化为明确的物质形态。我曾告诉过你们，没有任何事件是预先注定的。我必须调频到你们时间概念中的某个未来日期，并探查其所有分支脉络，才能确定我在你们早期时间点看到的哪些可能行动会在后期得以实现。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: （I then saw very clearly in my upper center field of vision an open, fulllipped and sensuous pair of red and feminine lips, with a triangular kind of tongue moving between them. I saw only the mouth and tongue, and for some reason thought of a cardboard tongue. The mouth spoke the words "Oh now please, please bear with me, " or words to that effect. As usual this vision was over before I realized I had seen it, yet during the sighting I was for the first time unaware of my body lying on the bed.
quotes.translation: （随后我在上视野中心清晰看到一张张开、丰唇且性感的红色女性嘴唇，一个三角形舌头在双唇间移动。我只看到嘴唇和舌头，不知为何联想到纸板做的舌头。那嘴唇说出"哦现在请，请包容我"或类似的话语。和往常一样，这个异象在我意识到之前就已结束，但在目睹过程中，我第一次完全感觉不到自己躺在床上的身体。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: （Thursday, November 12, 7 AM: This morning, again while dressing for work, I had an experience very similar to the one of Tuesday, November 3. This time I did not have a clear-cut thought that I should look out my studio windows. Instead I found myself walking back to them, and looking out I saw that, again, a car was blocking the exit of my car from its garage. It was also the same car as before, belonging to a tenant living downstairs. This is, I repeat, not the usual system for parking here.
quotes.translation: （11月12日星期四上午7点：今早穿衣准备上班时，我再次经历了与11月3日星期二非常相似的体验。这次我没有明确产生"应该看看工作室窗外"的念头，而是发现自己走回窗边，向外望去——果然又有一辆车堵住了我车库的出口。和上次是同一辆车，属于楼下租户。我重申，这并非此处的常规停车方式。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: I have also discussed reincarnation in terms of environment because many schools of thought over-emphasize the effects of reincarnational existences, so that often they explain present-life circumstances as a result of rigid and uncompromising patterns determined in a "past" life. You will feel relatively incompetent to handle present physical reality, to alter your environment, to affect and change your world, if you feel that you are at the mercy of conditions over which you have no control.
quotes.translation: 我也从环境角度讨论过转世，因为许多思想学派过分强调转世存在的影响，以至于他们常将今生境遇解释为"前世"刻板僵化模式的结果。若你觉得自己受制于无法掌控的条件，便会感到无力应对当前物质实相、无力改变环境、无力影响和改造你的世界。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In this case action forces change, and by the very nature of action no such duplications can occur. It may be said, for practical reasons, that A and B have identical thoughts. But the thoughts are not identical.
quotes.translation: 在此情况下，行动迫使改变，而基于行动的本质，不可能发生完全相同的复制。可以说，出于实际考虑，A和B拥有相同的想法。但这些想法并不相同。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: （Peggy Gallagher has been bothered in recent weeks by what she calls bursitis in her left shoulder. In the last few days the discomfort has moved down into her left arm; periodically she said the arm tingles, in a sensation similar to that felt when a limb has gone to sleep. The feeling is not that drastic though. Seth, without being asked, told Peggy that she had a disturbance in the area of the third to fifth vertebrae, and that this, basically, was the cause of her back and arm disturbances. Peggy had thought circulation was at the seat of the trouble. Seth said the circulation was healthy, but interfered with somewhat by tension.
quotes.translation: （佩吉·加拉格尔近几周一直被她称为左肩滑囊炎的病症困扰。最近几天不适感已蔓延至左臂；她说手臂会周期性刺痛，类似肢体麻木时的感觉，但程度没那么剧烈。赛斯未等询问便告知佩吉，她的第三至第五节脊椎区域存在紊乱，这基本就是背部和手臂不适的根源。佩吉原以为血液循环是问题所在。赛斯表示她的血液循环健康，只是受到紧张的些许干扰。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: If you examine your thoughts for five minutes at various times during the day for several times a month, you will indeed receive a correct impression of the kind of life you have so far arranged for yourself in the next existence. If you are not pleased with what you discover, then you had better begin changing the nature of your thoughts and feelings.
quotes.translation: 如果你每个月在不同时段花五分钟检视自己的思想，你确实会对自己为来世安排的生活类型获得正确印象。若你不满意所发现的内容，最好开始改变思想与感受的本质。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is always because you do not trust the natural self that you resort to such drug therapy. The individuals who seek out treatment fear the nature of their own identity more than anything else. They are then only too willing to sacrifice it. (Pause, then smiling:) Your thoughts and beliefs form your reality. There is, as Joseph (Seth's name for me) said in our break, no magic therapy -- only an understanding of your own great creativity, and the knowledge that you yourself make your world.
quotes.translation: 你之所以诉诸药物疗法，始终是因为不信任自然自我。寻求治疗者最恐惧的是自身身份的本质，他们太愿意牺牲它了。（停顿，微笑）你的思想和信念形成你的实相。正如约瑟（赛斯对我的称呼）在休息时所说，没有神奇的疗法——只有理解自身伟大的创造力，以及明白你亲手创造自己的世界。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Because you do not understand that your thoughts create illness you will continue to undergo it, however, and new symptoms will appear. You will again return to the doctor. When you are in the process of changing beliefs -- when you are beginning to realize that your thoughts and feelings cause illness -- then for a while you may not know what to do.
quotes.translation: 由于你不明白思想创造疾病，你会继续承受它，新症状将出现，你会再次求医。当你处于改变信念的过程中——开始意识到思想和感受导致疾病时——你可能会暂时不知所措。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In man, conscious thoughts are highly important as the directors of unconscious activity. You become more responsible, then, in a particular way for physical effects that, comparatively speaking, are "instinctive" in the animal. This gives you both a conscious and unconscious feedback system against which to test your experience and alter its nature.
quotes.translation: 在人类身上，有意识思想作为无意识活动的指导者至关重要。因此你以特定方式对肉体效应承担更多责任——这些效应在动物身上相对而言是"本能的"。这赋予你意识与无意识的双重反馈系统，用以检验经验并改变其本质。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: If Ruburt had done the same thing from the dream state, this could have resulted. A very slight amount of physical weight was lost on Ruburt's part. This will always occur when any kind of projection is experienced. Much of your intimate existence implies projections of various kinds. Thoughts are indeed small projections of yourself, sent outward.
quotes.translation: 若鲁柏从梦境状态做同样的事，也可能产生此结果。鲁柏身上流失了极少量体重——任何投射经验都会如此。你亲密存在的许多部分都涉及各种投射。思想确实是你向外发送的微小投射。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: You begin by accepting your own worth as a part of the universe, and by granting every other being that same recognition. You begin by honoring life in all of its forms. You begin by changing your thoughts toward your contemporaries, your country, your family, your working companions. If the ideal of loving your neighbor like yourself seems remote, you will at least absolutely refrain from killing your neighbor -- and your neighbor is any other person on the face of the planet (louder).
quotes.translation: 你从接纳自身作为宇宙一部分的价值开始，并给予每个存有同样的认可。你从尊重所有生命形态开始。你从改变对同代人、国家、家人、同事的思想开始。若爱邻如己的理想显得遥远，你至少绝对不杀害邻人——而你的邻人就是地球上的任何其他人（提高音量）。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Such action serves as a safeguard so that you do not overemphasize the gaps that may exist in yourself or in society, between the reality and the ideal condition. Many people want to change the world for the better, but that ideal seems so awe-inspiring that they think they can make no headway unless they perform some great acts of daring or heroism, or envision themselves in some political or religious place of power, or promote an uprising or rebellion. The ideal seems so remote and unreachable that, again, sometimes any means, however reprehensible, eventually can seem justified (see Session 850, for example). To change the world for the better, you must begin by changing your own life. There is no other way.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Such an individual may find himself in ten different environments within the flicker of an eyelash, for example, with no idea of the reason behind the situation. He will see no continuity at all, and feel himself flung without rhyme or reason from one experience to another, never realizing that his own thoughts are propelling him quite literally.
quotes.translation: 这样的个体可能在一眨眼的功夫发现自己身处十个不同的环境，却完全不明白这背后的原因。他看不到任何连续性，感觉自己毫无道理地被从一个经历抛向另一个经历，从未意识到正是自己的思想在实实在在地推动着他。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There are obviously as many kinds of ghosts and apparitions as there are people. They are as alert or as unalert to their situation as you are to your own. They are not fully focused in physical reality, however, either in personality or in form, and this is their main distinction. Some apparitions are thought-forms sent by survival personalities out of lingering deep anxiety. They portray the same compulsive-type behavior that can be seen in many instances in your ordinary experience.
quotes.translation: 显然，有多少种人就有多少种鬼魂和幻影。它们对自己处境的认识程度，就像你对自己处境的认识程度一样。然而，它们无论是人格还是形态，都没有完全聚焦在物质实相中，这是它们的主要区别。有些幻影是生存人格出于挥之不去的深层焦虑而发送的思想形态。它们描绘了你在日常经验中常见的那种强迫性行为模式。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: What interrupts this inner impetus could be compared to what you call poor suggestion. It is indeed distortive. It represents a blocking of impetus. It interrupts what should be a simultaneous and easy flow of inner impetus outward, in your case outward to physical construction. The particular negative words spoken or thought are but indications of this break of impetus. The break occurs first, and it is for this reason that what you call negative suggestions therefore are acted upon.
quotes.translation: 打断这种内在冲动的，可以比作你所谓的负面暗示。它确实是扭曲的。它代表了冲动的阻塞。它打断了内在冲动本应同时且顺畅地向外流动的过程——在你这里，是向外流向物质建构。那些被说出或想到的特定负面词语，只是这种冲动断裂的迹象。断裂首先发生，正因如此，你所谓的负面暗示才会被付诸行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: I also want to stress here that all aspects of life experience not only sensations but emotional feelings. Therefore, there is a kind of innate gallantry that operates among all segments of life -- a gallantry that deserves your respect and consideration. You should have respect, then, for the cells of your body, the thoughts of your mind (pause), and try to understand that even the smallest of creatures shares with you the emotional experience of life's triumphs and vulnerabilities.
quotes.translation: 我还想在此强调，生命体验的方方面面不仅包括感官感受，还包括情感感受。因此，在生命的各个部分之间，存在着一种天生的勇毅——一种值得你尊重和体谅的勇毅。那么，你应该尊重你身体的细胞、你心灵的思想（停顿），并试着去理解，即使是最微小的生物，也与你共享着生命胜利与脆弱的情绪体验。
quotes.book: The Way Toward Health
quotes.source_file: 10-10-10-10The Way Toward Health - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (I talked about the first session for Jane's symptoms -- the private portion of Session 208, for November 15, 1965 -- yet she said that for her the whole thing really started the day before we went to a party in June 1966 regarding How to Develop Your ESP Power. Our friends wanted to celebrate the publication of her first "psychic" book, her first mention of Seth, but she read poetry at the party and wouldn't talk about the ESP book -- too embarrassed. A strange, protective way to behave.

quotes.text: In the same way, each of my readers has a connection with the same level of psychic reality. In greater terms, all of this is happening at once. Ruburt is contributing and forming a certain portion of my experience, even as I am contributing to his. Your identities are not something already completed. Your most minute action, thought, and dream adds to the reality of your psyche, no matter how grand or austere the psyche may appear to you when you think of it as a hypothetical term.
quotes.translation: 同样地，我的每一位读者都与同一层级的灵性实相相连。从更大的角度来说，这一切都在同时发生。鲁柏正在贡献并形成我经验的某一部分，正如我也在贡献于他的经验一样。你的身份并非某种已经完成的东西。你最微小的行动、思想和梦境，都为你心灵的实相增添了内容，无论当你把它当作一个假设性术语来思考时，这个心灵在你看来是多么宏大或庄严。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: When you believe in a universe accidentally formed, and when you think you are a member of a species accidentally spawned, then private life seems devoid of meaning, and events can seem chaotic. Disastrous events thought to originate in a god's wrath could at least be understood in that context, but many of you live in a subjective world in which the events of your lives appear to have no particular reason -- or indeed sometimes seem to happen in direct opposition to your wishes....
quotes.translation: 当你相信宇宙是偶然形成的，当你认为自己是一个偶然繁衍的物种的成员时，那么私人生活似乎就失去了意义，事件也可能显得混乱。那些被认为源于神之愤怒的灾难性事件，至少在那个语境下是可以理解的，但你们许多人生活在一个主观世界里，其中你生命中的事件似乎没有特别的原因——或者有时甚至似乎与你的愿望直接相悖……
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 832, JANUARY 29, 1979

quotes.text: （"A connection with the letter M", referred, I thought, to the initials, MCI, which are used to designate the modern new office building here in Elmira, at which I bought my membership in the AAA. Later note by RFB: Signed by Merrill. Note on the object that the address of this new building was added later to the card, beneath the old address which was blocked out. This building was barely in operation at the time I joined AAA, in August 1964; the top floors were not yet finished.
quotes.translation: （“与字母M的关联”，我想，指的是MCI这几个首字母缩写，它们被用来指代埃尔迈拉这里新建的现代办公大楼，我正是在那里购买了AAA的会员资格。后来由RFB备注：由梅里尔签署。关于该物品的备注：这座新建筑的地址后来被添加到卡片上，位于被涂掉的旧地址下方。我于1964年8月加入AAA时，这座建筑才刚刚投入使用；顶层尚未完工。

quotes.text: The brain looks out upon the physical universe, and consciousness then reacts, again creatively, to that environment. Information is now carried in reverse fashion back to the inner self, in an instantaneous and automatic procedure. Thus thought becomes an inner image which is translated into a thermal image, and then into intuitional form, into highly condensed and codified data, and then into a pure and direct sort of experience which you cannot understand as physical creatures.
quotes.translation: 大脑向外审视物质宇宙，然后意识再次以创造性的方式对该环境做出反应。信息现在以反向的方式被携带回内在自我，这是一个即时且自动的过程。因此，思想变成了一种内在影像，这种影像被转化为热影像，然后转化为直觉形式，转化为高度浓缩和编码的数据，最后转化为一种纯粹而直接的体验，这种体验是你们作为物质生物所无法理解的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: （See the tracing of the envelope object on page 82. I felt Seth was on the track with his data, yet because ! had so little background concerning it myself the data seemed opaque to me. I found myself feeling the same way about the answers to my questions, so I did not continue to ask them. I also thought the data was mixed up with the planned visit of my parents to the apartment for Easter Sunday, April 10, and a very short reminder my mother had written in connection with this.
quotes.translation: （参见第82页的信封物品描摹图。我觉得赛斯提供的数据方向是对的，但由于我自己对此几乎毫无背景知识，这些数据对我来说显得晦涩难懂。我发现自己对问题的答案也有同样的感觉，所以我没有继续提问。我还认为这些数据与我父母计划于4月10日复活节周日来公寓拜访一事，以及我母亲为此写的一则简短提醒混在了一起。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: You are not putting your full powers into this however, and may as well do so for you will regret it if you do not. This is the field in which you have decided to test your own capabilities and strength, so do so. The company is plastic. If I thought you might do otherwise then I would not give you this particular advice, but you have made your decision so you may as well throw all of your energy into it. You are an old feudal lord, and the company is your territory. (Humorous.)
quotes.translation: 然而，你并未将自己的全部力量投入其中，但你应该这样做，因为如果你不这样做，你会后悔的。这是你决定测试自己能力和力量的领域，所以放手去做吧。这家公司是可塑的。如果我认为你可能会另作打算，我就不会给你这个特别的建议，但你已经做出了决定，所以你不妨将全部精力投入其中。你是一位老封建领主，而这家公司就是你的领地。（幽默地。）

quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: As you know, you create or construct all physical matter. What you see are your own constructions. This does not mean however that there is not something within or behind what you perceive. All thoughts are composed of energy, and from our discussions you realize that there are electrical intensities within whose range all reality exists.
quotes.translation: 如你所知，你创造或构建了所有物质。你所看到的是你自己的构建。但这并不意味着你所感知的事物背后或之内不存在某种东西。所有思想都由能量构成，从我们的讨论中你意识到，存在一些电性强度，所有现实都存在于其范围之内。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Now I speak to you honestly, and while you may find my words harsh they are spoken with both love and compassion, and (underlined) understanding of your innermost thoughts. Your salvation lies in giving up personal male love and marriage as (underlined) a condition for existence. Because you do often misinterpret me, I repeat: this does not mean you will not find such a male and relationship. But it does mean you will not find this while you hold it as a condition of existence.
quotes.translation: 现在我坦诚地对你说，虽然你可能会觉得我的话刺耳，但它们是带着爱与慈悲，以及（下划线）对你内心最深处的思想的理解说出的。你的救赎在于放弃将个人的男性之爱与婚姻作为（下划线）生存的条件。因为你常常误解我，我重复一遍：这并不意味着你不会找到这样一位男性并建立关系。但这确实意味着，当你将其视为生存的条件时，你是找不到的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: And so in the present circumstance the overcrowded conditions will still be further accelerated, until a new synthesis is formed--and it will be. The psychic activity engendered will finally form the basis for a greater understanding. It will be quite obvious that telepathic thoughts travel quicker than good news or faster than a pestilence. Man will be so shoved together that the impact will literally explode into a new level of consciousness, a new development in those terms.
quotes.translation: 因此，在当前情况下，拥挤的状况将进一步加速，直到形成新的综合——而这必将发生。由此引发的灵性活动最终将为更深刻的理解奠定基础。显而易见，心电感应思想的传播速度比好消息或瘟疫更快。人类将被如此紧密地挤压在一起，以至于这种冲击将真正引爆出新的意识层次，即在此类术语中的新发展。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Now with the various people at Prentice, you will have such tendencies often appearing separately, so that one person will be highly conventional and dislike changes, while another might be responsive to work that was emotionally exciting, avant garde. The publishing house -- that publishing house -- represents in capsule form the extremes of thought of your time, from the most conventional to the most bizarre. It therefore represents the public's ideas in their great variety.
quotes.translation: 现在，在普伦蒂斯出版社的各位人士中，你们会发现这类倾向常常单独显现，因此一个人可能高度传统且厌恶变化，而另一个人则可能对情感上令人兴奋、前卫的作品产生共鸣。那家出版社——那家出版社——以浓缩的形式代表了你们时代思想的极端，从最传统到最怪诞。因此，它代表了公众思想极其多样的面貌。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: At this point many variations in behavior emerge, each the result of individual background, knowledge, and habit. The surroundings in which the dead find themselves will often vary. Vivid hallucinations may form experience quite as real as any in mortal life. Now, I have told you that thoughts and emotions form physical reality, and they form after-death experience. This does not mean that the experiences are not valid, any more than it means that physical life is not valid.
quotes.translation: 此时，行为中会出现许多变化，每一种都是个人背景、知识和习惯的结果。死者所处的环境往往各不相同。生动的幻觉可能形成与尘世生活同样真实的体验。现在，我告诉过你们，思想和情感形成了物质实相，它们也形成了死后体验。这并不意味着这些体验无效，正如它并不意味着物质生活无效一样。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The conscious physically oriented portions of the self become acquainted with the inner information. To some extent the reality of thought is consciously perceived as the innovator behind physical matter. Such an individual then can understand the nature of hallucinations at the point of death, and with full conscious awareness enter into the next plane of existence. The information made conscious is then passed on to others where it can be physically recognized and applied.
quotes.translation: 自我中面向物质的有意识部分开始熟悉内在信息。在某种程度上，思想的实相被有意识地视为物质背后的创新者。这样的个体随后能够理解死亡时刻幻觉的本质，并以完全清醒的意识进入下一层存在。这些被意识化的信息随后传递给他人，使其能在物质层面被识别和应用。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Remember, these mental associations are living things. They are formations of energy assembled into invisible structures, through processes quite as valid and complicated as the organization of any group of cells. Comparing them with cells, they are of briefer duration, generally speaking, though under certain conditions this does not apply. But your thoughts form structures as real as the cells. Their composition is different in that no solidity is involved in your terms.
quotes.translation: 记住，这些心智联想是活生生的存在。它们是通过与任何细胞群组织同样真实且复杂的过程，将能量组合成不可见结构而形成的。一般来说，与细胞相比，它们的持续时间较短，尽管在某些条件下这并不适用。但你们的思想形成的结构与细胞一样真实。它们的组成不同，因为以你们的术语来说，不涉及任何固态。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: （Back home and ready to being typing, I found it so windy and noisy I thought it best to close the windows. The tree that lost the branch stands perhaps thirty feet away on my right; actually it sits on a neighbor's property. It is an elm that died a few years ago, and possessed a very beautiful and symmetrical shape. I have drawn it several times, the most elaborate drawing being one I intended to incorporate in a tempera painting last winter. I did not get the work done.
quotes.translation: （回到家准备打字时，我发现风太大、噪音太响，觉得最好关上窗户。那棵断枝的树大约在我右侧三十英尺处；实际上它位于邻居的地产上。那是一棵几年前死去的榆树，曾拥有非常美丽对称的形状。我画过它好几次，最精细的一幅是我打算去年冬天融入蛋彩画中的作品。但我未能完成那幅画。）

quotes.text: From this layer many of the deepest inspirations come. These ideas and concepts, having their own electromagnetic identity, nevertheless appear as "the symbolic landscape" at this level of consciousness. This is difficult to explain. The thoughts do not appear as pseudoimages for example, or assume any pseudomaterialization, yet they are felt vividly, perceived and picked up by portions of the brain -- those seemingly unused portions for which science has found no answer.
quotes.translation: 许多最深刻的灵感都源自这一层次。这些思想和概念拥有自身的电磁身份，却在此意识层面显现为"象征性风景"。这很难解释。这些思想并不以伪图像形式出现，也不呈现任何伪物质化形态，但大脑的某些部分——那些看似未被使用、科学尚未找到答案的部分——却能生动地感知、接收它们。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The power that fuels your thoughts has the same source. In a manner of speaking the universe as you understand it, with all the events that it includes, functions "automatically" in its important processes, as your own body does. Your individual desires and intents direct that activity of your body's spontaneous processes -- that is, your body walks across the floor at your command as a result of your wishes, even though the processes involved must happen "by themselves."
quotes.translation: 滋养你思想的能量源自同一源头。可以说，你所理解的宇宙及其包含的所有事件，在其重要进程中都是"自动"运作的，就像你自己的身体一样。你个人的欲望和意图引导着身体自发过程的运作——也就是说，你的身体因你的意愿而听从指令走过地板，即便其中涉及的过程必须"自行"发生。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There are obviously many kinds of identities. Now in our last session I told you that our imaginary sender "A" does not transmit a given thought. He does not even send an exact duplicate. Action, the very action of transmission, alters the nature, the electrical reality of the thought itself. This is an extremely important point.
quotes.translation: 显然存在多种身份。在上次课程中我曾告诉你，我们假想的发送者"A"并不传递某个既定思想。他甚至不发送精确的复制品。行动本身——传递的行动——会改变思想本身的本质和电磁实相。这是极其重要的一点。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now. Moment points are indeed composed of action, action experiencing itself. What you see and experience physically represents but a small portion, a mere fraction of what reality is. There are universes within universes. Action is, and yet forms its own experience. Thoughts take electromagnetic patterns that have their own materializations as universal systems. Your physical body is a series of actions, though the word series is being used for simplicity's sake only.
quotes.translation: 现在。瞬间点确实由行动构成，是行动体验自身。你从物质层面看到和体验到的，只是实相中极小的一部分，一个微不足道的片段。宇宙之中还有宇宙。行动存在，并形成自身的体验。思想采取电磁模式，这些模式作为宇宙体系拥有自身的物质化形态。你的物质身体是一系列行动，虽然"系列"一词仅为简便起见而使用。

quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: The form was generally a figure. The pyramid for example could have been a robe, though no robe was present. The lights were my thoughts (Pause), in their electromagnetic form. Yet the form of the pyramid represented in other areas the three of us. The form had its own meaning therefore. The form did not disappear when he awakened. He simply switched to physical sight mechanisms that did not perceive it. The freedom of his dream activity will now return. (Long pause.)
quotes.translation: 那个形态大致是一个人形。比如金字塔本可以是件长袍，尽管现场并无长袍。那些光是我的思想（停顿），以电磁形式呈现。然而金字塔的形态在其他领域代表了我们三人。因此这个形态有其自身意义。当他醒来时，形态并未消失。他只是切换到了无法感知它的物质视觉机制。他梦境活动的自由现在将恢复。（长停顿。）

quotes.text: They are taught, in other words, the methods that allow them to translate emotion and thought into physical actuality. There is no time lag, as there must be in the three-dimensional system, between the initiation of such thoughts and their materialization.
quotes.translation: 换言之，他们被教导如何将情感和思想转化为物质实相的方法。在三维系统中，这类思想的启动与其物质化之间必然存在时间差，而这里则没有。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Your theories of time are connected with your usual neurological pulses. It is one thing to play with concepts of multidimensionality, or probabilities, and quite another to be practically presented with them, even briefly, when your thought patterns and neurological habits tell you that they cannot be translated. So Ruburt felt frustrated, and he told me in no uncertain terms (see Appendix 4) that his consciousness could not contain the information he was receiving.
quotes.translation: 你们的时间理论与你惯常的神经脉冲有关。玩弄多维性或或然性的概念是一回事，而当你的思维模式和神经习惯告诉你这些概念无法被转化时，实际面对它们——哪怕只是短暂地——则完全是另一回事。因此鲁柏感到沮丧，他用毫不含糊的语气告诉我（见附录4），他的意识无法容纳他正在接收的信息。

quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Some of your environment under such conditions and at certain stages does represent what many occultists call thought forms. In one legitimate projection of yours, Joseph, you formed a vehicle, I believe. Perhaps it was a plane, so that you would not be frightened. The flying was a fact, you see, the plane was a thought form .
quotes.translation: 在这种条件下及某些阶段，你们环境中的一部分确实代表了众多神秘学家所称的"思想形态"。约瑟，在你一次合法的投射中，我相信你曾构建过一个载具。或许那是一架飞机，这样你就不会感到害怕。飞行是事实，你看，飞机则是一个思想形态。

quotes.text: He is well aware of your mother's vitality, and she is well aware subconsciously that her vitality still goads him into a semblance of reaction. She still acts as a stimuli, as you know. In one way she forces him to stay when he would retire, and when her thoughts are not strongly with him he will attempt to escape further.
quotes.translation: 他非常清楚你母亲的活力，而她潜意识里也明白，她的活力仍在刺激他做出某种反应。如你所知，她仍然是一种刺激源。从某方面来说，当他想要退缩时，她迫使他留下；而当她的思想没有强烈地与他同在时，他会试图进一步逃离。

quotes.text: If you thought or felt in such a fashion, then you would appreciate the fact that biologically your body is yours by virtue of the mineral, plant and animal life from which it gains its sustenance. You would not feel imprisoned as you often do within one corporal form, for you would understand that the body itself maintains its relative stability because of its constant give-and-take with the materials of the earth that are themselves possessed of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 如果你以这样的方式思考或感受，那么你就会认识到一个生物学事实：你的身体之所以属于你，是因为它从矿物、植物和动物生命中汲取养分。你不会像常感到的那样被禁锢在一个肉身之中，因为你会明白，身体之所以保持相对稳定，正是因为它与地球本身拥有意识的物质之间不断进行着给予与索取。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: （At first Peggy saw no connection here. Then she remembered that when she arrived in Newark, NJ from Elmira, she transferred to Kennedy Airport on Long Island by helicopter. During the trip, at an altitude of no more than 600feet, she thus flew over many of the docks, piers and shipyards lining the Brooklyn waterfront. She thought at the time that Bill would have enjoyed the sight, since he likes ships. The helicopter flight traveled past the Statue of Liberty.）
quotes.translation: （起初佩吉看不出其中的关联。随后她想起，当她从埃尔迈拉抵达新泽西州纽瓦克时，她转乘直升机前往长岛的肯尼迪机场。在飞行途中，高度不超过600英尺，她飞越了布鲁克林滨水区沿岸的许多码头、栈桥和造船厂。她当时想，比尔会喜欢这景色，因为他喜欢船只。直升机飞行经过了自由女神像。）

quotes.text: These were meant as checks against impulsive or intuitive behavior. He must then behave, he thought, in a measured, reasoned, quiet, calm and disciplined way: and to see that he did, he slowed down the motions the body could make. Now, this is the heart of the matter, and he would not let me give it earlier. He should, and indeed, must, be himself. Those qualities of his of which he became suspicious are those that made our sessions and his development possible.
quotes.translation: 这些本意是对冲动或直觉行为的制衡。他当时认为，自己必须以审慎、理性、安静、冷静且有纪律的方式行事：为了确保做到这一点，他放慢了身体所能做出的动作。现在，这正是问题的核心，而他之前不让我提前说出这一点。他应该，而且确实必须，做他自己。他所怀疑的那些特质，恰恰是让我们的课程和他的发展成为可能的关键。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Creative distortion, in its relationship with action, affects therefore the creation of thoughts. The principle of creative distortions is mainly responsible for the fact that no identical thought is ever transmitted from one individual to another. Here the material that I have given you on telepathy should be considered.
quotes.translation: 创造性扭曲在与行动的关系中，因此影响着思想的创造。创造性扭曲的原理主要解释了为何没有完全相同的思想能从一个人传递给另一个人。在此，我提供给你们关于心灵感应的资料应予以考虑。

quotes.text: Ruburt's mother knew that the child could be taken away were it proven that she was an unfit mother in any way, or unable to give the child proper care. Well over a year before this picture was taken, in fact, Ruburt was sent to a Catholic home.5 There, unconventional thought was not tolerated. The inflexibility of dogma conscientiously applied to daily action was experienced, and within it Ruburt tried to apply himself and to focus his deeply mystical nature.*
quotes.translation: 鲁柏的母亲知道，若她被证明在任何方面是不称职的母亲，或无法给予孩子适当照顾，孩子就可能被带走。事实上，在这张照片拍摄前一年多，鲁柏就被送到了一家天主教寄养机构。在那里，非传统思想是不被容忍的。教条的僵化被一丝不苟地应用于日常行动中，鲁柏在其中努力约束自己，并试图聚焦于他深层的灵性本质。

quotes.text: When you follow that so-called rational approach, however, you are bound to feel threatened, divorced from your body. Your thoughts and your body seem separate. Divisions seem to appear between the mental and the physical, where again each are supported by those magical processes. That rational approach goes against what I can only call life's directives and life's natural rhythms. It is contradictory to biological integrity, and again, it does not make sense.
quotes.translation: 然而，当你遵循那种所谓的理性途径时，你必然会感到威胁，与你的身体分离。你的思想和身体似乎分离了。心智与物质之间似乎出现了分裂，而实际上两者都受到那些神奇过程的支撑。那种理性途径违背了我只能称之为生命指令和生命自然韵律的东西。它与生物完整性相矛盾，而且再次说明，它毫无道理。

quotes.text: The emotional climate, though intangible, is intimately known by each individual as it exists within himself, and it is the best indication of his physical condition, for thoughts and emotions as independent electrical actions have great influence directly upon the physical mechanism, acting indeed as electric storms which flash through the entire nervous system; or as great stabilizers as the case may be, and with of course many middle varieties of influence.
quotes.translation: 情绪氛围虽无形，但每个人都能深切感知其存在于自身之内，它是身体状况的最佳指标，因为思想和情绪作为独立的电性活动，对物理机制有直接影响，确实像电风暴般闪遍整个神经系统；或者视情况而定，成为强大的稳定器，当然还有许多中间类型的影响。

quotes.text: Now... Within three years there will be entirely new openings for John, and also many changes of attitude. A strange connection in this respect, not at all clear; having to do with bricks or the laying of bricks, and a prior incident leading up to this that happened in 1963. There will be some relationship or venture, first thought of or encountered in 1963, that will find fruition within I believe a three-year period. Break, as we look in another direction.
quotes.translation: 现在……三年内，约翰将迎来全新的机遇，也会有许多态度上的转变。在这方面有一个奇怪的关联，目前尚不清晰；与砖块或砌砖有关，以及1963年发生的一件先导事件。会有某种关系或事业，最初在1963年被想到或遇到，我相信将在三年内结出果实。暂停，让我们看向另一个方向。

quotes.text: You cannot do this by ignoring the validity of the thoughts as your experience, however (very intensely), by trying to shove them under the rug of a superficial optimism. Such habitual, unhappy thoughts will bring about the same kind of physical experience, but it is your own system of beliefs that you must examine.
quotes.translation: 然而，你不能通过忽视这些思想作为你经验的真实性（非常强烈地），试图将它们扫入肤浅乐观主义的地毯下来做到这一点。这种习惯性的、不快乐的思想会带来同样类型的身体经验，但你必须审视的是你自己的信念体系。

quotes.translation: （“与某种深层事物的连接。地下的洞穴，如矿井或坟墓。”赛斯在课程早期曾讨论过与信封数据相关的联想，我认为这个数据是很好的例子。我的想法是，这个数据指的是我的工作地点——我从那里获取了该物品——通过提及1964年一位朋友埃兹拉·哈文斯的去世。埃兹拉曾在我工作的地方——艺术卡片公司——工作多年。）

quotes.text: There were countless other events that happened to you on different afternoons at the same hour, both before and after that one. The cells within your hand contain within themselves memories your conscious mind would be dazzled to behold. Yet remember that the cells in your twenty-seven-year-old hand are in no physical way the same cells that experienced any of those events. In some underground of sensation, however, the buried evidences of stimuli and reaction experienced during those numberless "past" afternoons still exist. Some of those memories will certainly be played back, to affect what you think of as your current experience at twenty-seven. Your conscious thoughts and habits regulate which of them will intermix into the maelstrom of the present.
quotes.translation: 在那之前和之后，无数其他事件发生在你不同下午的同一时刻。你手中的细胞，其内部包含的记忆足以让你有意识的心智眼花缭乱。然而，请记住，你二十七岁手中的细胞，在物理上并非体验过那些事件的同一批细胞。但在某种感觉的地下深处，那些无数“过去”下午所经历的刺激与反应的埋藏证据依然存在。其中一些记忆肯定会被回放，影响你视为当前二十七岁体验的内容。你有意识的思维和习惯会调节哪些记忆会混入当下的漩涡。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You must look within yourself for evidences of what you want in terms of positive experience. Examine your past with that in mind. Imagine your future from the power point of the present. In such a way at least you are not using the past to reinforce your limitations, or projecting them into the future. It is only natural to contrast what you want with what you have, and it is very easy to become discouraged in so doing, but looking for errors in the past will not help you. A correctly utilized five-minute period of time can be of great benefit, however. In this period concentrate upon the fact that the point of power is now. Feel and dwell upon the certainty that your emotional, spiritual and psychic abilities are focused through the flesh, and for five minutes only direct all of your attention toward what you want. Use visualization or verbal thought -- whatever comes most naturally to you; but for that period do not concentrate upon any lacks, just upon your desire.
quotes.translation: 你必须在自身之内寻找积极体验方面你所渴望的证据。带着这个想法审视你的过去。从当下的力量点想象你的未来。这样，你至少不会用过去来强化你的局限，或将它们投射到未来。将你想要的与你所拥有的进行对比是很自然的，这样做时很容易感到气馁，但寻找过去的错误对你没有帮助。然而，正确利用五分钟的时间段却能带来巨大益处。在这五分钟里，专注于“力量点就在当下”这一事实。感受并沉浸于这样的确定性：你的情感、灵性与心灵能力都通过肉体聚焦；在这五分钟内，只将全部注意力导向你所渴望的事物。使用视觉化或言语思维——任何对你最自然的方式；但在这段时间内，不要专注于任何匮乏，只专注于你的渴望。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

related_concepts.name_en: The point of power is in the present

quotes.text: (Slowly:) I told you once that there were pulses of activity in which you blinked off and on -- this applying even to atomic and subatomic particles.1 "You" assign as real -- present here and now -- only that activity that is your signal. "You" are not aware of the others. When people think in terms of one self, they of course identify with one body. You know that the cellular structure of it changes constantly. The body is at any given moment, however, a mass conglomeration of energy formed from that rich bank of probable activity. The body is not stable in the terms usually thought of. On deeper biological levels the cells straddle probabilities, and trigger responses. Consciousness rides upon and within the pulses mentioned earlier, and forms its own organizations of identity. Each probability -- probable only in relation to and from the standpoint of another probability -- is inviolate, however, in that it is not destroyed. Once formed, the pattern will follow its own nature.
quotes.translation: （缓慢地：）我曾告诉过你，存在活动的脉冲，你在此中闪烁开启与关闭——这甚至适用于原子和亚原子粒子。¹“你”只将作为你信号的活动指派为真实——当下此地。“你”对其他活动毫无察觉。当人们以单一自我的角度思考时，他们当然会认同于一个身体。你知道身体的细胞结构不断变化。然而，在任何给定时刻，身体都是从那个丰富的可能活动库中形成的能量大杂烩。身体并非通常所认为的那样稳定。在更深的生物层面上，细胞跨越可能性，并触发反应。意识骑乘于并存在于前述的脉冲之内，形成其自身的身份组织。然而，每个可能性——仅相对于另一个可能性的立场而言是可能的——都是不可侵犯的，因为它不会被摧毁。一旦形成，模式将遵循其自身本性。

quotes.text: Since you value sexual performance in the most limited of terms, and use that largely as a focus of identity, then both your old and young suffer consequences that are not so much the result of age as of sexual prejudice. It is interesting to note that both the old and the young also find themselves outside of your organizational frameworks. The young are more freewheeling in their thoughts before they accept sexual roles, and the old are more freewheeling in theirs because they have discarded their sexual roles. I did not say that old or young had no sexual expression -- but that both groups did not identify their identities with their sexual roles. There are of course exceptions. If the man or the woman is taught that identity is a matter of sexual performance, however, and that that performance must cease at a certain age, then the sense of identity can also begin to disintegrate. If children feel that identity is dependent upon such performance, then they will begin to perform as quickly as possible. They will squeeze their identity into sexual clothes, and the society will suffer because the great creative thrusts of growing intellect and intuitions will be divided at puberty, precisely when they are needed.
quotes.translation: 既然你们以最有限的方式珍视性表现，并将其主要用作身份的焦点，那么你们的老年人和年轻人都承受着后果——这些后果与其说是年龄的结果，不如说是性偏见的产物。有趣的是，老年人和年轻人都发现自己处于你们的组织框架之外。年轻人在接受性角色之前，其思想更为自由奔放；而老年人则因抛弃了性角色，其思想也更为自由奔放。我并非说老年人或年轻人没有性表达——而是说这两个群体都不将他们的身份认同于性角色。当然也有例外。如果男人或女人被教导身份是性表现的问题，且这种表现必须在某个年龄停止，那么身份感也可能开始瓦解。如果孩子们觉得身份依赖于这种表现，那么他们就会尽快开始表现。他们会将身份挤入性的外衣中，社会将因此受苦，因为成长中的智力与直觉的伟大创造性推力会在青春期被分裂——恰恰是在最需要它们的时候。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This in no way denies the validity of events as you think of them, for all of your physical activity immediately alters all other relationships at all levels of being. Most of you are familiar with inspiration in one form or another. People who are not writers or artists, or poets or musicians, often suddenly find themselves almost transformed for a brief period of time -- suddenly struck by a poem or a song or a snatch of music, or by a sketch -- that seems to come from nowhere, that seems to emerge outside of the context of usual thought patterns, and that brings with it an understanding, a joy, a compassion, or an artistic bent that seemingly did not exist a moment earlier. Where did the song or poem or music come from? Such individuals feel that they suddenly "know" in a direct manner. They experience knowledge that comes from within rather than information that comes from without.
quotes.translation: 这绝不否认事件如你所认为的有效性，因为你所有的物理活动会立即改变所有存在层面的所有其他关系。你们大多数人熟悉某种形式的灵感。那些不是作家、艺术家、诗人或音乐家的人，常常会突然发现自己在一段短暂时间内几乎被转化——突然被一首诗、一首歌、一段音乐或一幅素描所击中——这似乎凭空而来，似乎出现在通常思维模式的语境之外，并带来一种理解、喜悦、慈悲或艺术倾向，仿佛片刻之前并不存在。那首歌、诗或音乐从何而来？这样的人感到他们突然以直接的方式“知道”。他们体验来自内在的知识，而非来自外在的信息。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In titling this chapter I used the word "mechanics," because mechanisms suggest smooth technological workings. While the world is not a machine -- its inner workings are such that no technology could ever copy them -- this involves a natural mechanics in which the inner dimensions of consciousness everywhere emerge to form a materialized, cohesive, physical existence. Again, your interpretations of identity teach you to focus awareness in such a way that you cannot follow the strands of consciousness that connect you with all portions of nature. In a way, the world is like a multidimensional, exotic plant growing in space and time, each thought, dream, imaginative encounter, hope or fear, growing naturally into its own bloom -- a plant of incredible variety, never for a moment the same, in which each smallest root, leaf, stem, or flower has a part to play and is connected with the whole.
quotes.translation: 为本章命名时，我用了"机制"这个词，因为机制暗示着顺畅的技术运作。虽然世界并非机器——其内在运作方式没有任何技术能够复制——但这涉及一种自然机制，其中意识的内在维度处处涌现，形成物质化、凝聚性的物理存在。再说一次，你对身份认同的诠释教导你以某种方式聚焦意识，以至于你无法追踪那些将你与自然所有部分相连的意识之线。在某种程度上，世界就像一株生长在时空中的多维异域植物，每个念头、梦境、想象际遇、希望或恐惧，都自然绽放成自己的花朵——一株不可思议的多样植物，每一刻都不相同，其中每根最小的根须、叶片、茎干或花朵都扮演着角色，并与整体相连。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Five weeks ago [in the notes leading off Session 821], I wrote that Jane could resume work on The Further Education of Oversoul Seven -- or Seven Two -- at any time. She finished her first novel about Seven in July 1972, and within a month, long before it was published, she wrote the first five chapters for Seven Two. Then we became involved in so many other projects that she laid it aside until August 1976, when she wrote two more chapters. The book has hovered in the back of her consciousness ever since, waiting until she focused her attention upon it once again. "Seven's got all the time in the world," she laughed more than once. But now that her editor, Tam Mossman, has scheduled a visit to us at the hill house in a couple of weeks [on April 10, to be exact], Jane feels that she wants to study what she's done on Seven Two, go over it with Tam, and perhaps take up work on it again.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: You cannot begin to have a true psychology, again, unless you see the living self in a greater context, with greater motives, purposes and meanings than you now assign to it, or for that matter than you assign to nature and its creatures. You have denied many impulses, or programmed others so that they are allowed expression in only certain forms of action. If any of you do (underlined) still believe in the Freudian or Darwinian selves, then you will be leery about impulses to examine your own consciousness, afraid of what murderous debris might be uncovered. I am not speaking merely in hypothetical terms. For example, a well-intentioned woman was here recently. She worried about her overweight condition, and [was] depressed at what she thought of as her lack of discipline in following diets. In her dismay, she visited a psychologist, who told her that her marriage might somehow be part of the problem. The woman said she never went back. She was afraid that she might discover within herself the buried impulse to kill her husband, or to break up the marriage, but she was sure that her overweight condition hid some unfortunate impulse.
quotes.translation: 除非你将活生生的自己置于更广阔的脉络中，赋予它比你现在所赋予的——或就此事而言，比你所赋予自然及其造物的——更伟大的动机、目的和意义，否则你无法开始拥有真正的心理学。你否定了许多冲动，或对其他冲动进行编程，使它们只能以某些特定行动形式表达。如果你们中任何人（确实）仍然相信弗洛伊德或达尔文式的自我，那么你们会对检视自身意识的冲动保持警惕，害怕可能发现什么凶残的残骸。我并非仅以假设性说法举例。例如，最近有位善意的女士来访。她为自己的超重状况担忧，并因认为自己缺乏节食自律而沮丧。在沮丧中，她咨询了一位心理学家，后者告诉她她的婚姻可能也是问题的一部分。这位女士说她再也没回去过。她害怕自己内心可能发现埋藏的杀死丈夫或破坏婚姻的冲动，但她确信自己的超重状况隐藏着某种不幸的冲动。

quotes.text: Some people are only aware of -- or largely aware of -- impulses toward anger, because they have inhibited those natural impulses toward love that would otherwise temper what seemed to be aggressive desires. When you begin trusting yourselves, you start by taking it for granted that to some extent at least you have not trusted yourself or your impulses in the past: You have thought that impulses were dangerous, disruptive, or even evil. So as you begin to learn self-trust, you acknowledge your impulses. You try them on for size. You see where they lead you by allowing them some freedom. You do not follow urges through that would hurt others physically, or that seem in direct contradiction to your present beliefs -- but you do acknowledge them. You do try to discover their source. Behind them you will almost always find an inhibited impulse -- or many of them -- that motivated you to move in some ideal direction, to seek a love or understanding so idealized in your mind that it seemed impossible to achieve. You are left with the impulse to strike out.
quotes.translation: 有些人只意识到——或主要意识到——愤怒的冲动，因为他们抑制了那些本可调和看似攻击性欲望的自然之爱冲动。当你开始信任自己时，你首先要理所当然地认为，至少在一定程度上你过去并不信任自己或自己的冲动：你认为冲动是危险的、破坏性的，甚至邪恶的。因此，当你开始学习自我信任时，你承认自己的冲动。你试穿它们看看合不合身。你通过给予它们一些自由来观察它们会引领你走向何方。你不会遵循那些会伤害他人身体、或与你当前信念直接矛盾的冲动——但你的确承认它们。你确实试图发现它们的源头。在它们背后，你几乎总会发现一个被抑制的冲动——或许多个——这些冲动曾激励你朝着某个理想方向前进，去寻求一种在你心中如此理想化以至于似乎不可能实现的爱或理解。结果你只剩下出击的冲动。
