quotes.text: Once said, the explanation will certainly seem obvious. (Pause.) Prentice-Hall, in capsule form, so to speak, is a representative of the most diverse kinds of thought currently held in your country -- that is, under it's overall auspices you have the most conventional establishment-oriented textbooks, devoted to continuing traditional ideas. You have, there, a concentration upon education as it is understood at that level.
quotes.translation: 一旦说破，解释显然会变得显而易见。（停顿。）普伦蒂斯·霍尔出版社，可以说，以浓缩的形式代表了贵国当前持有的最多元化的思想——也就是说，在其总体支持下，既有最传统的、以建制为导向的教科书，致力于延续传统观念；也有对那个层面所理解的教育的高度关注。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: "Instruments calculated to measure the vibrations with which scientists are familiar will be designed and redesigned. All sorts finally of seemingly impossible phenomena will be discovered with these instruments, until the scientists realize that something is desperately wrong. The instruments will be planned to catch certain camouflages, and since they will be expertly thought out they will perform their function. I do not want to get too involved. However, by certain means the instruments themselves will transform data from terms that you cannot understand into terms that you can understand. Scientists do this all the time."
quotes.translation: "旨在测量科学家熟悉的振动频率的仪器将被设计再设计。最终，这些仪器会发现各种看似不可能的现象，直到科学家意识到出了大问题。仪器将被设计用来捕捉特定的伪装，由于它们经过精心构思，将能发挥其功能。我不想深入探讨。然而，通过某些方式，仪器本身会将数据从你无法理解的术语转化为你能理解的术语。科学家们一直在这样做。"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 1. In Volume 1 of "Unknown" Reality, I wrote in Note 7 for Session 681 that atoms are "processes" rather than things. The classical conception of the typical atom as being composed of a neat nucleus of indivisible protons and neutrons circled by electrons is largely passé, although for convenience's sake we may still describe the atom that way. (In those terms, the one exception is the hydrogen atom, which evidently consists of but one proton and one electron cloud, or "smear.") For the simple purposes of this note, then, I'm leaving out considerations involving quantum mechanics, which concept repudiates the idea of "particles" to begin with. (And surely that notion involves more than a little of the psychic, or "irrational." What a heretical thought from the scientific viewpoint!) But each atom of whatever element is an amazingly complicated, finely balanced assemblage of forces and particles woven together in exquisite detail--one of the more basic examples of the unending and stupendous creativity, order, and design of nature, or consciousness, or All That Is.

quotes.text: It may indeed sound like an oversimplification. However, you are exactly what you think you are, and every thought is mirrored in the physical matter of the human organism. This does not happen by some sort of occult magic. It is not the result of some mumbo jumbo. Since each individual creates subconsciously the physical matter of his own image, then it follows that the condition of this image is his own responsibility.
quotes.translation: 这听起来确实像是过度简化。然而，你正是你所认为的自己，每一个思想都映照在人类有机体的物质中。这并非通过某种神秘魔法发生，也不是某种胡言乱语的结果。既然每个个体都在潜意识中创造自身形象的物质，那么该形象的状态便是其自身的责任。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Your very physical life, then, implies a "source," a life out of which the physical one emerges, dash -- the implied, unspoken, unmaterialized, unsounded vitality that supplied the ingredients for the physical, bodily, molecular "alphabet." Your physical life therefore implies a nonphysical one. You take your particular "language" so for granted, and use it so effortlessly, that you give no thought at all to the fact that it implies other languages also, or that it gains its meaning because of inner assumptions that are never spoken, or by the use of pauses in which no sounds are made. You live your lives in the same fashion.
quotes.translation: 因此，你的物质生命本身暗示着一个"源头"，一个物质生命从中涌现的生命——破折号——那个隐含的、未言明的、未物质化的、无声的生命力，为物质、身体、分子的"字母表"提供了成分。因此，你的物质生命暗示着一个非物质生命。你如此理所当然地接受你特定的"语言"，如此毫不费力地使用它，以至于你完全忽略了它暗示着其他语言的事实，也忽略了它因从未说出的内在假设或无声停顿的运用而获得意义。你以同样的方式生活。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: All manner of insects, birds and beasts cooperate in this venture, producing the natural environment. This is as normal and inevitable as the fact that your breath causes a mist to form on glass if you breathe upon it. All consciousness creates the world, rising out of feeling-tone. It is a natural product of what your consciousness is. Feelings and emotions emerge into reality in certain specific ways. Thoughts appear, growing on the bed already laid. The seasons spring up, formed by ancient feeling-tones, having deep and abiding rhythms. They are the result, again, of innate creative aspects that are a portion of all life.
quotes.translation: 各种昆虫、鸟类和野兽都参与这一冒险，共同创造自然环境。这就像你对着玻璃呼气会形成雾气一样正常和必然。所有意识都创造世界，从感觉基调中升起。这是你意识本质的自然产物。感觉和情绪以特定方式显现为实相。思想出现，在已铺就的床上生长。季节涌现，由古老的感觉基调形成，具有深沉持久的韵律。它们再次是作为所有生命一部分的内在创造面向的结果。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: When women give birth in a crowded world they also know, and with a portion of their conscious minds, that a violation is involved. When your species sees that it is destroying other species and disrupting the natural balance, then it is consciously aware of its violation. When such natural guilt is not faced there are other mechanisms that must be employed. Again at the risk of repeating myself: Many of your problems result from the fact that you do not accept the responsibility of your own consciousness. It is meant to assess the reality that is unconsciously formed in direct replica of your thoughts and expectations.
quotes.translation: 当女性在拥挤的世界中生育时，她们也以部分意识心智明白其中涉及一种侵犯。当你们的物种看到自己正在毁灭其他物种、破坏自然平衡时，便会有意识地觉察到自身的侵犯。当这种自然的罪恶感未被正视时，就必须采用其他机制。再次冒着重复的风险：你们的许多问题源于你们不接受自身意识的责任。意识本应评估那以你们思想和期望的直接复制品形式无意识形成的实相。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: It is, therefore, as if your thoughts appear within other realities as objects -- alive and vital in themselves, growing into other systems as flowers or trees grow up seemingly from nothing within physical reality. These can then be used as the raw material, so to speak, in certain other systems. They are the given "natural data," the raw material of creativity in the realities that you help seed but do not perceive.
quotes.translation: 因此，仿佛你们的思想在其他实相中显现为物体——本身鲜活而充满生命力，如同花朵或树木在物质实相中看似凭空生长般，进入其他系统生长。这些思想可被用作某些其他系统中的原材料，可以说是"自然数据"，是你们协助播种却未感知的实相中创造力的原始素材。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your generation as a whole had to learn the importance of thought and responsibility. You had to learn that basically to hate is to kill. The lesson was a practical one. The Germans and the Jews made it plain. Had the hatred not existed in the Germans, it could not have been channeled as it was against the Jews. Free will operated here as always.
quotes.translation: 你们这一代人整体必须学习思想与责任的重要性。你们必须明白，从根本上说，恨即是杀。这教训是实际的。德国人与犹太人使这一点昭然若揭。倘若德国人心中本无仇恨，便无法将其如此导向犹太人。自由意志在此一如既往地运作。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: You will find exactly what you wish to find. You will make of your lives exactly what you expect to make of your lives. The beauties that exist in your physical universe are the results of constructive and positive thought. The ugliness is a direct result of negative thought.
quotes.translation: 你们将确切找到你们想找的东西。你们将把生活塑造成你们期望的样子。物质宇宙中存在的美，是建设性与正面思想的结果；而丑陋则是负面思想的直接产物。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Behind these, so to speak, exist what you may term temperature pictures, in which delicate gradations of heat form ever shifting Emotional patterns that do have a semi physical outline. From these, you see, normal images are built up, and from the images thoughts are formed.
quotes.translation: 可以说，在这些背后存在着你们可称之为温度图像的东西，其中热度的微妙渐变形成不断变化的情绪模式，这些模式具有半物质轮廓。由此，你们看，正常意象得以构建，而思想又从意象中形成。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Only when you operate from your own stance can you help others to the best of your ability. To anticipate danger, or to imaginatively take on the troubles of others robs you of the very energy with which you could help them. I am not saying, therefore, to turn your eyes from the unfortunate conditions of the world. Practical help is needed in all areas of the human life. Yet it is far better, and more practical ultimately, to concentrate upon the beneficial elements of civilization -- far better to organize your thoughts in areas of accomplishment than to make mental lists of man's deficiencies and lacks.
quotes.translation: 只有当你们从自身立场出发时，才能竭尽全力帮助他人。预想危险，或想象性地承担他人的烦恼，只会剥夺你们用以帮助他们的能量。因此，我并非要你们对世界的不幸状况视而不见。人类生活的各个领域都需要实际帮助。然而，专注于文明的有利元素终究更好、更实际——将思想组织在成就领域，远比罗列人类的缺陷与匮乏更有益。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I am speaking generally here, for remember that your individual beliefs, thoughts, and emotions cause your reality, so no person dies ahead of his or her time. The individual chooses the time of death. It is true, however, that many cancers and conditions such as AIDS result because the immunity system has been so tampered with that the body has not been allowed to follow through with its own balancing procedures.
quotes.translation: 我在此泛泛而谈，因为请记住，你个人的信念、思想和情感造就了你的实相，所以没有人会提前死亡。个体选择死亡的时间。然而，许多癌症和诸如艾滋病之类的状况确实是因为免疫系统受到严重干扰，以至于身体无法完成自身的平衡程序所致。
quotes.book: The Way Toward Health
quotes.source_file: 10-10-10-10The Way Toward Health - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (I talked about the first session for Jane's symptoms -- the private portion of Session 208, for November 15, 1965 -- yet she said that for her the whole thing really started the day before we went to a party in June 1966 regarding How to Develop Your ESP Power. Our friends wanted to celebrate the publication of her first "psychic" book, her first mention of Seth, but she read poetry at the party and wouldn't talk about the ESP book -- too embarrassed. A strange, protective way to behave.

quotes.text: The very journey involved in self-discovery, the very self-questioning involved, is indeed part of the process of recovery. The energy being used in the maintenance of the ulcer will already begin to take new channels. The thoughts and anticipations of the personality have already begun to stray from the all important ulcer to the causes behind it, for in this case, as in many others, we find a strange phenomenon.
quotes.translation: 自我发现之旅本身，以及其中涉及的自我质疑，确实是康复过程的一部分。用于维持溃疡的能量已经开始转向新的渠道。人格的思想和预期已经开始从至关重要的溃疡转向其背后的原因，因为在这种情况下，与许多其他情况一样，我们发现了一种奇怪的现象。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Ruburt read an article about the development of a strain of mice without thymus [glands]. Since the thymus is very important in the necessary process of maintaining bodily resistance to disease, these particular mice have little resistance. They are bred and sold for experimental purposes. The intent of such procedures is to promote the quality of human life, to study the nature of diseases, and hopefully apply what is learned to some of the lives of human beings. Mice are not considered human. They are not. So like any animal, they are thought of as dispensable, sacrificed to a fine humanitarian end.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏读到一篇文章，关于培育一种没有胸腺的老鼠品系。由于胸腺在维持身体抵抗疾病的重要过程中非常关键，这些特定的老鼠几乎没有抵抗力。它们被培育并出售用于实验目的。这些程序的意图是提升人类生活的质量，研究疾病的性质，并希望将所学应用于一些人类的生命。老鼠不被视为人类。它们不是。所以像任何动物一样，它们被认为是可以牺牲的，为了一个崇高的人道主义目标而被献祭。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: If you listen to an FM radio station, there is a handy lock-in gadget that automatically keeps the station in clear focus; it stops the program from "drifting." In the same way, when you daydream you drift away from your home station, while still relating to it, generally speaking. You also have the mental equivalent, however, of the FM's lock-in mechanism. On your part this is the result of training, so that if your thoughts or experience stray too far this mental gadget brings them back into line. Usually this is automatic -- a learned response that by now appears to be almost instinctive. Period.
quotes.translation: 如果你收听一个FM广播电台，有一个方便的锁定装置，可以自动保持电台的清晰聚焦；它防止节目“漂移”。同样地，当你做白日梦时，你会从你的主台漂移开，同时一般来说仍与之相关。然而，你也有相当于FM锁定机制的心智对应物。对你来说，这是训练的结果，所以如果你的思想或经验偏离太远，这个心智装置会把它们拉回正轨。通常这是自动的——一种习得的反应，现在看起来几乎像是本能的。句号。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Each particular "station" of consciousness perceives in a different kind of reality, and as mentioned earlier (in Session 711, for instance), you usually tune in to your home station most of the time. If you turn your focus only slightly away, the world appears differently; and if that slightly altered focus were the predominant one, then that is how the world would seem to be. Each aspect of the psyche perceives the reality upon which it is focused, and that reality is also the materialization of a particular state of the psyche projected outward. You can learn to encounter other realities by altering your position within your own psyche.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: He is reacting in an exaggerated manner to a truth--and the truth is that thoughts cause reality. He interprets this negatively (pause) because he is so afraid of hurting others. He takes it for granted that natural thought, left alone, will be destructive and hurt others.
quotes.translation: 他对一个真理反应过度——这个真理是思想造就实相。他消极地解释这一点（停顿），因为他如此害怕伤害他人。他理所当然地认为，自然的思想，如果放任不管，会是破坏性的并伤害他人。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: There is no other valid way of changing physical events. It might help if you imagine an inner living dimension within yourself in which you create, in miniature psychic form, all the exterior conditions that you know. Simply put, you do exactly this. Your thoughts, feelings and mental pictures can be called incipient exterior events, for in one way or another each of these is materialized into physical reality.
quotes.translation: 没有其他有效的方法来改变物质事件。如果你想象自己内在有一个内在的活生生的维度，在其中你以微缩的心灵形式创造你所知的所有外在条件，这可能会有所帮助。简单地说，你正是这样做的。你的思想、情感和心灵画面可以被称为初期的外在事件，因为每一种都以某种方式被物质化为物质实相。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Someday we shall explain this in mathematical terms. However for now we shall try plain English. Every thought is composed of its own energy, and it has an effect within energy. We are not speaking here now of your tired old cause and effect theory however. Every action changes every other action. Any probability is a reality whether or not it occurs within your own system, and I shall add to that early mention.
quotes.translation: 总有一天我们会用数学术语来解释这一点。不过现在，我们先用简单的英语来尝试。每个思想都由其自身的能量构成，并在能量中产生效应。然而，我们这里说的并非你们那套陈旧的因果理论。每个行动都会改变其他所有行动。任何可能性都是实相，无论它是否发生在你们自己的系统内，而且我要补充一点之前提到过的内容。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: He did not work on his book, incidentally, because he felt you did not want him to. (Long pause.) You resented it when he began typing some of the sessions. (I thought this was my idea: RFB.) You felt he was trying to deprive you further of your part. He felt particularly poor at your parents because there be felt under combined attack. He does have a natural aggressiveness also however, and a rebellious nature.
quotes.translation: 顺便说一句，他没有继续写书，是因为他觉得你不想让他写。（长时间的停顿。）当你开始打字记录一些课程时，你对此感到不满。（我以为这是我的主意：RFB。）你觉得他试图进一步剥夺你的参与部分。他在你父母面前尤其感到自卑，因为他觉得自己受到了联合攻击。不过，他确实也有一种天生的攻击性和叛逆性格。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: A psychic gestalt is dependent upon matter, not for its identity but merely for its survival in the physical plane. Psychic gestalts or identities or individualities are for all practical purposes immortal. They may join other gestalts but they will never be less than they once were. Identity then is never broken down. Any apparent breaking down is never an actual fact, as the personality could be thought of as a breaking down of the entity; but this is not so. The personality did not exist as such before its creation by the entity, and once it becomes an identity, it retains that individuality.
quotes.translation: 心灵完形依赖于物质，不是为了其身份，而仅仅是为了在物质层面的存续。心灵完形、身份或个体性，从所有实际目的来看，都是不朽的。它们可以与其他完形结合，但永远不会变得比它们曾经的样子更少。因此，身份永远不会被分解。任何看似分解的情况都不是实际事实，就像人格可以被视为存有的分解一样；但事实并非如此。人格在被存有创造之前并不存在，而一旦它成为一个身份，它就保留了那种个体性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: In a manner of speaking, your universe and all others spring from a dimension that is the creative source for all realities--a basic dream universe, so to speak, a divine psychological bed where subjective being is sparked, illuminated, stimulated, pierced, by its own infinite desire for creativity. The source of its power is so great that its imaginings become worlds, but it is endowed with a creativity of such splendor that it seeks the finest fulfillment, for even the smallest of its thoughts and all of its potentials are directed with a good intent that is literally beyond all imagining.
quotes.translation: 可以说，你们的宇宙和所有其他宇宙都源于一个维度，这个维度是所有实相的创造之源——可以说是一个基本的梦境宇宙，一个神圣的心理温床，在那里，主观存在被其自身对创造力的无限渴望所激发、照亮、刺激和穿透。其力量的源头如此巨大，以至于它的想象变成了世界，但它被赋予了如此辉煌的创造力，以至于它寻求最完美的实现，因为它最微小的思想和所有潜能都带着一种善意被引导，这种善意简直超乎一切想象。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Seth began dictating The "Unknown" Reality: A Seth Book, in the 679th session for February 4, 1974, and finished it with the 744th session for April 23, 1975. In the beginning we anticipated another intriguing Seth book, the successor to Seth Speaks and The Nature of Personal Reality. We thought the new work would probably be a long one, but we hardly expected that it would require publication in two volumes.
quotes.translation: 赛斯在1974年2月4日的第679次课程中开始口述《“未知”实相：赛斯书》，并在1975年4月23日的第744次课程中完成。起初，我们期待另一本引人入胜的赛斯书，作为《赛斯说》和《个人实相的本质》的续作。我们认为这部新作可能会很长，但几乎没想到它需要分两卷出版。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Session 704:* More on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Shadows can be pleasant and luxurious, and on a hot sunny day you are certainly aware of their beneficial nature. So some dream hallucinations are beautiful, comforting, refreshing. They can bring great peace and be sought after for themselves. You may believe that God exists as a kindly father, or you might personify him as Christ or Buddha. In your dreams you might then encounter such personages. They are quite valid, but they are also hallucinations cast by your own thoughts and feelings. Dreams of Heaven and Hell alike fall into the same category, in those terms, as hallucinations.
quotes.translation: 阴影可以是宜人而奢华的，在炎热的晴天，你当然会意识到它们有益的特性。同样，有些梦境幻觉是美丽、舒适、令人神清气爽的。它们能带来极大的平静，并且本身值得追求。你可能相信上帝以慈父的形象存在，或者你可能将他具象化为基督或佛陀。在梦中，你可能会遇到这样的人物。它们相当真实，但它们也是由你自己的思想和情感投射出的幻觉。天堂和地狱的梦境，用这些术语来说，都属于同一类幻觉。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 720
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: For now think of it as you usually do, in a time context. It has been fashionable in the past to believe that each species was oriented selfishly toward its own survival. Period. Each was seen in competition with all other species. In that framework cooperation was simply a by-product of a primary drive toward survival. One species might use another, for instance. Species were thought to change, and "mutants" form, because of a previous alteration in the environment, to which any given species had to adjust or disappear. The motivating power was always projected outside* (underlined).
quotes.translation: 暂且按你们惯常的时间观念来理解。过去曾流行一种观点，认为每个物种都自私地以自身生存为导向。句号。每个物种都被视为与其他所有物种竞争。在这种框架下，合作不过是生存本能驱动下的副产品。例如，一个物种可能利用另一个物种。物种被认为会因环境先前的改变而产生变化，形成"突变体"，任何特定物种要么适应环境，要么消失。驱动力始终被投射到外部*（下划线标注）。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: You are intuitively aware that you form your image, and that you are independent of it. You do not realize that you create your larger environment and the physical world as you know it by propelling your thoughts and emotions into matter -- a breakthrough into three-dimensional life. The inner self, therefore, individually and en masse, sends its psychic energy out, forming tentacles that coalesce into form.
quotes.translation: 你直觉地意识到自己塑造了形象，并且独立于它。你尚未认识到，你通过将思想和情感推进物质——突破进入三维生命——创造了你所知的更大环境和物质世界。因此，内在自我无论个体还是集体，都向外发送其心灵能量，形成触须，这些触须凝聚成形态。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This is why I have mentioned earlier that the individual was supremely important. Yet the self limits itself through boundaries that are arbitrary and erected through fear, and also through habit that originally had its source in the necessity for physical survival. Physical survival does not demand these habits any longer, and they remain as shackles. You should know by now that individual thought does not remain within the boundary of the physical individual. This indeed has actually been proven, in so far as telepathy is recognized as a fact, at least by some, and soon by all.
quotes.translation: 这就是为何我先前提到个体具有至高无上的重要性。然而，自我通过任意设立且因恐惧而竖立的边界限制自己，也通过最初源于肉体生存必要性的习惯限制自己。肉体生存已不再需要这些习惯，它们却仍如枷锁般存在。你现在应该明白，个体思想并不局限于肉体个体的边界之内。这实际上已被证实，就心灵感应被承认为事实而言——至少部分人承认，很快所有人都会承认。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: When you leave the physical system after reincarnations, you have learned the lessons, and you are literally no longer a member of the human race in those terms, for you elect to leave it. Only the conscious self dwells within it in any case, and it is other portions of your personality who simultaneously dwell within the other training systems. In other more advanced systems, thoughts and emotions are automatically and immediately translated into action, into camouflage, into whatever approximation of matter there exists. Therefore the lessons must be taught and learned well.
quotes.translation: 当你在轮回后离开物质系统时，你已学会功课，从那些角度而言你已不再是人类种族的一员，因为你选择离开它。无论如何，只有意识自我栖居其中，而你人格的其他部分同时栖居于其他训练系统。在更先进的系统中，思想和情绪会自动且立即转化为行动、幻相、以及任何近似物质的存在。因此，功课必须被教导并学好。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: "It all gives me this feeling of great hilarity that I often have about these ideas. And the thought of families of consciousness merging for different reasons -- even while I accept that all of this is put in very limited terms -- seems to have such perfect inner logic and delightful playfulness about it that I launch into the mergings notion with all kinds of questions, and impressions exploding outward.
quotes.translation: "这一切让我产生那种常有的极度欢愉感。想到意识家族因不同原因融合——即便我明白这一切都是用非常有限的术语表述的——似乎具有如此完美的内在逻辑和愉悦的嬉戏性，以至于我带着各种问题投入融合概念，印象向外迸发。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The language is an excellent example of the coded messages I mentioned earlier (as I'd thought). It is supposed to remain secret, you see, yet becomes the symbol of the all-powerful knowledge of the exaggerated superior self, while making the knowledge impossible to act upon. To translate the information would mean a more serious commitment to physical communication than that young man was willing to make.
quotes.translation: 这种语言是我先前提到的编码信息的绝佳范例（如我所想）。它本应保持秘密，你瞧，却成为夸大优越自我全知全能的象征，同时使知识无法付诸实践。翻译这些信息意味着比那位年轻人愿意做出的更严肃的物理沟通承诺。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: At the beginning of the notes for this 920th session I wrote that the session isn't dictation for Dreams. Yet it is, of course, since in it Seth illuminates from still another perspective his concept of value fulfillment.8 The session grew out of the encounter we had yesterday afternoon with an unexpected visitor from out of state. Seth hardly mentions the individual involved, however, but instead goes into the subject of mental illness in more inclusive terms. I see many correlations between this evening's material and that in Session 917 for Chapter 8; there, among other things, Seth had discussed the reasoning mind, the imaginative mind, and schizophrenia. That session had been triggered, at least in part, by a letter Jane had found to be quite upsetting.

quotes.text: If there are strongly negative characteristics present in your most intimate thoughts, if these actually form bars between you and a more full life, still you often look through the bars, not seeing them. Until they are recognized they are impediments. Even obstacles have a reason for being. If they are your own, then it is up to you to recognize them and discover the circumstances behind their existence.
quotes.translation: 若你最私密的思绪中带有强烈的负面特质，若这些特质确实在你与更丰盛生命之间筑起栅栏，你仍常透过栅栏向外看，却看不见它们。除非被认出，否则它们始终是障碍。即使障碍也有其存在的理由。若它们属于你，便该由你认出它们，并发现其存在背后的情境。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: What they do is take quick glimpses of your plane--and hold in mind that the saucer or cigar shape seen on your planet is a bastard form having little relation to the structure as it is at home base. At a later date I may go into the inhabitants of that plane more thoroughly, but as it is I am not acquainted with them very much myself. There are so many things that you do not understand that I hope to explain to you. There are other things that you do not understand that I cannot explain to you, simply because they would be too alien now for your regular mode of thought.
quotes.translation: 它们所做的只是快速瞥见你们的层面——请记住，在你们星球上看到的飞碟或雪茄形状，是一种畸变形态，与它们在本部基地的结构几乎无关。日后我或许会更深入探讨那个层面的居民，但目前我自己对他们也了解不多。你们有太多不理解之事，我希望能向你们解释。还有一些你们不理解之事，我无法向你们解释，只因它们对你们当前的思维模式而言过于陌生。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: （Speaking of the fact that civilized man should not kill, Seth said the whole idea of killing is fallacious to begin with: an enemy who is "dead" is far more harmful than one who is still alive. Here he was dealing with the basic unity of all consciousness again. Killing is not thought of as an end in itself on other planes, he repeated. But it is wrong to kill on our plane when we do consider it an end.
quotes.translation: （谈到文明人不该杀戮的事实，赛斯说杀戮的整个概念根本就是谬误：一个"已死"的敌人比活着的敌人危害更大。此处他再次触及所有意识的基本统一性。他重申，在其他层面，杀戮本身不被视为目的。但在我们这一层面，当我们确实视杀戮为目的时，杀戮便是错误的。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: （None of us asked questions that might lead to totally new, unfamiliar material that we might wish later was recorded. Bill wanted to ask Seth to comment on the God concept, but thought this was a complicated subject. He settled instead for a question pertaining to a large wooden Tibetan statue he and Peg had purchased in Ithaca, NY. To our surprise this subject developed a few complications of its own.
quotes.translation: （我们无人提出可能导向全新、陌生素材的问题——那些我们事后或许希望被记录下来的内容。比尔本想请赛斯评论上帝概念，但认为这是个复杂主题。他转而问了一个关于他和佩格在纽约州伊萨卡购买的巨大木制西藏雕像的问题。出乎意料的是，这个主题本身也衍生出一些复杂情况。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Often spontaneously we had class sessions, and as you noticed he would miss our next session. He thought that two sessions were quite enough. Our regular sessions will always be the primary source of the material, and the stress will always be laid upon them. Nevertheless, the class sessions serve an important purpose. They are a subsidiary or offshoot, but they are not meant to supersede our own work.
quotes.translation: 我们常有自发进行的课堂聚会，而如你所注意到的，他会错过我们下一次的正式课。他认为两次课已经足够。我们的常规课始终是素材的主要来源，重点也始终放在它们之上。尽管如此，课堂聚会也起着重要作用。它们是辅助或分支，但并非要取代我们自己的工作。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: But thoughts have shapes, as do dreams. I use the word shape for simplicity's sake, but the electrical universe is composed of dimensions which are perceived by the inner self, for the inner self also has existence within the electrical universe. If all of this sounds farfetched, then remember that the shapes that you perceive meaningful, many other species within your own field cannot perceive at all.
quotes.translation: 但思想有形状，正如梦境也有形状。我使用"形状"一词仅为简便起见，但电性宇宙由内在自我所感知的维度构成，因为内在自我也存在于电性宇宙之中。若这一切听起来牵强，请记住：你们认为有意义的那些形状，在你们自身领域内的许多其他物种根本感知不到。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The most private agonies of the soul were assigned a more or less common source in man's primitive "unconscious" drives. The private unquelled thrusts toward creativity were seen as the unbalanced conglomeration of chemicals within a person's most private being -- a twist of perversity. Genius was seen as a mistake of chromosomes, or the fortunate result of a man's hatred for his father. The meaning of life was reduced to the accidental nature of genes. Science thought in terms of averages and statistics, and each person was supposed to fit within those realms.
quotes.translation: 灵魂最私密的痛苦被归因于人类原始"无意识"驱力的某种共同源头。那些未被驯服的、朝向创造力的私人冲动，被视为个人最私密存在中化学物质的失衡堆叠——一种乖僻的扭曲。天才被视作染色体的错误，或男人对父亲憎恨的幸运结果。生命的意义被简化为基因的偶然性。科学以平均值和统计数字思考，每个人都应被框定在这些范畴内。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (In Note 2 for Session 801, I commented on the sociological implications represented by her correspondence.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: This operates in individual and mass terms. Suppose that today your home was robbed. Yesterday, the theft was one of innumerable probable events. I chose such an example because more than one person would have to be involved -- the victim and the robber. (Pause.) Why was your home ransacked, and not your neighbor's? In one way or another, through your conscious thought you attracted such an event, and drew it from probability into actuality. The occurrence would be an accumulation of energy -- turned into action -- and be brought about by corollary beliefs.
quotes.translation: 这在个体与集体层面同时运作。假设今日你家遭窃，昨日这桩盗窃还只是无数可能事件之一。我选择这个例子是因为涉及不止一人——受害者和窃贼。（停顿）为何是你家被洗劫而非邻居？通过某种方式，你以有意识的思想吸引了这样的事件，将其从可能性拉入现实。这个事件会是能量的累积——转化为行动——并由衍生信念促成。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The same beliefs often spread to economic areas in which (long pause) people who met pleasure in God's eyes were therefore gifted with wealth and prosperity, as well as good health. Therefore God was seen to be on the side of those who competed most strenuously, so that to be poor or sick was almost seen as a sign of God's disfavor. All such concepts appear in one form or another at most official levels of thought and education. The whole idea of the esthetics of nature is forgotten -- a subject that we will touch upon further as we continue our discussion.
quotes.translation: 同样的信念常蔓延至经济领域，（长停顿）那些在上帝眼中获得喜悦之人，因此被赐予财富、繁荣与健康。于是上帝被视为站在最激烈竞争者的那一边，贫穷或疾病几乎成了上帝不悦的征兆。所有这些概念以各种形式出现在大多数官方思想与教育层面。自然美学的整体概念被遗忘——这个主题我们将在后续讨论中进一步触及。
quotes.book: The Way Toward Health
quotes.source_file: 10-10-10-10The Way Toward Health - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Jane did very well reading aloud the first two notes I'd written for Session 907 of Dreams, as well as yesterday's session. In fact, her reading of the session was as fast, or faster, than any I've heard her do. Peggy Gallagher visited briefly in the middle of Jane's effort.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 4: "The Broken-Hearted, the Heartless, and Medical Technology."

quotes.text: （See the tracing of the object on page 91, and the notes on page 92. At first the data meant little, but Seth's answer to the second question furnished the key that made it intelligible to us. It would have been quite opaque to an outsider. This is a case where Seth used the object as a springboard to delve into data that is connected to it through location mainly. My thought was that the bulk of the material he gives had more appeal emotionally for him than the object itself, and he confirmed this after break. Still, the turn the data took was unexpected.
quotes.translation: （参见第91页的物体追踪记录及第92页的注释。起初这些数据意义甚微，但赛斯对第二个问题的回答提供了钥匙，使其对我们而言变得可理解。对外人来说这完全晦涩难懂。在此案例中，赛斯以该物体为跳板，主要透过位置关联来探究相关数据。我的想法是，他提供的材料主体在情感上比物体本身更吸引他，休息后他证实了这点。不过，数据转向仍出人意料。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: They will be and they are prisoners of their own tools. More galaxies will seemingly be discovered, more mysterious radio stars will be perceived, until the scientists realize that something is desperately wrong. Instruments designed to measure the vibrations with which scientists are familiar will be designed and redesigned. All sorts, finally, of seemingly impossible phenomena will be discovered with these instruments. The instruments will be designed to catch certain camouflages and since they are expertly thought out they will perform their function.
quotes.translation: 他们将成为且已是自身工具的囚徒。更多星系看似会被发现，更多神秘射电星会被观测，直到科学家意识到某些根本性错误。为测量科学家熟悉的振动而设计的仪器将被反复改良。最终，这些仪器将发现各种看似不可能的现象。这些仪器被设计来捕捉特定伪装，由于经过精密构思，它们将完美执行功能。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Physical reality is therefore glimpsed by other kinds of personalities in other systems, from their own unique viewpoint. Peering at it from this angle, so to speak, you would not recognize it as your own home system. From some of these viewpoints, your physical matter has little or no permanency, while to others your own thoughts have a shape and form, perceived by observers but not by yourselves.
quotes.translation: 因此，其他系统中的其他类型人格，从各自独特视角瞥见物质实相。若从这个角度窥视，你甚至认不出这是你自己的本家系统。从某些视角看，你们的物质几乎没有持久性；而对另一些观察者而言，你们的思想具有形状与形态，能被观察者感知，你们自己却浑然不觉。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The distinctions formed by the outer senses therefore actually limit perception as a whole, while intensifying it into a small but vivid, seemingly enclosed radius of reality. When conceptual thought develops far enough, then it is imagined that all energy originates from what seems to be a closed system; and this misconception then colors all deductions made concerning the nature of energy itself.
quotes.translation: 外在感官所形成的区分因此实际上限制了整体的感知，同时将其强化为一个微小但生动、看似封闭的现实范围。当概念性思维发展到足够程度时，人们便会想象所有能量都源自一个看似封闭的系统；这种误解随后会染指所有关于能量本身性质的推论。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: （The following material is included here became it is dealt with in the next session, the 14th. I wrote these notes on the morning of January 8; they came to mind while I was working on an ink drawing of a complicated tree bearing within it two birds nests. I began the drawing in reference to an image Seth had conjured up in an earlier session on the fifth dimension. I believe these thoughts came to me so easily because I had been making some conscious efforts to think in new ways, and they were triggered by my working on this particular drawing.
quotes.translation: （以下材料之所以收录于此，是因为它将在下一节（第14节）中讨论。我在1月8日早晨写下这些笔记；它们在我创作一幅包含两个鸟巢的复杂树木水墨画时浮现脑海。这幅画的灵感源于赛斯在早期关于第五维度的课程中唤起的一个意象。我相信这些想法之所以如此轻易地涌现，是因为我一直在有意识地尝试以新的方式思考，而创作这幅画则触发了它们。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: All of your physical experience must, of course, be pivoted in the corporeal reality of the body. The energy that moves your image comes from the soul. Through your own thoughts you direct the body's expression, and it can be of health or of illness. Out of a knowledge of the contents of your own conscious mind you can definitely heal most maladies of the body, within conditions to be given later.
quotes.translation: 当然，你们所有的物质体验都必须以身体的物质实相为轴心。驱动你们形象的能量来自灵魂。通过你们自己的思想，你们引导身体的表达，它可以是健康或疾病。凭借对自身意识心内容的了解，在稍后给出的条件下，你们确实可以治愈身体的大多数疾病。

quotes.text: (Very long pause.) He should also realize that pleasure is indeed a virtue. By all means express your emotions to each other as they naturally occur. Ruburt was not taught to love himself as a child, and thought of his talents as a way of justifying his existence -- an existence of somewhat suspicious nature, he felt, since his mother told him often that he was responsible for her own poor health.
quotes.translation: （长时间的停顿。）他还应该认识到，快乐确实是一种美德。务必让你们的情绪自然流露，彼此表达。鲁柏在童年时未被教导要爱自己，他认为自己的才华是证明自身存在合理性的方式——他感觉这种存在带有某种可疑的性质，因为他的母亲经常告诉他，他对自己糟糕的健康状况负有责任。

quotes.text: Some feelings and thoughts are translated into structures that you call objects; these exist, in your terms, in a medium you call space. Others are translated instead into psychological structures called events, that seem to exist in a medium you call time.

quotes.text: My form may be changed, but so do you change the form of your own thoughts. When you dream of a particular location, that location does then exist in fact. It has a definite reality, although it may not have a physical reality. Because you experience it, and you are partially physical, it does have some basis of reality in physical terms, even though it may not exist full-blown in physical terms.
quotes.translation: 我的形态可能会改变，但你们也会改变自己思想的形态。当你梦见某个特定地点时，那个地点实际上确实存在。它拥有明确的实相，尽管可能没有物质实相。因为你体验到了它，而你部分上是物质的，所以它在物质层面上确实具有某种实相基础，即使它可能尚未在物质层面上完全展现。

Marilyn said the G did not refer to any person with that initial that she knew of, in connection with the object. However, she thought it might refer to the fact that the ceramic cat has a certain type of high-gloss glaze fired on; this glaze being made of glass.
quotes.translation: “M和G”再次提出了如何解读缩写的问题。M可能指的是制作陶瓷猫的玛丽莲。玛丽莲说，就这个物件而言，G并非指她认识的任何以该字母开头的人。不过，她认为这可能指的是陶瓷猫表面烧制的一种特殊高光釉料，这种釉料由玻璃制成。

quotes.text: Now: We do not need to name the two portions separately. I had thought of specifically having the first part deal with inner and outer immediate environments, and then to lead to the larger reality of the soul and its perception in the second part. This is what I did, but the material is so interwoven that I felt the given separation enough, and did not want to reinforce the idea of a division.
quotes.translation: 现在：我们无需将这两部分分开命名。我原本打算让第一部分专门探讨内在与外在的直接环境，然后引导至第二部分中灵魂及其感知的更广阔实相。我确实这样做了，但材料交织得如此紧密，以至于我觉得现有的区分已经足够，并不想强化分裂的概念。

Or, the voices that you hear within your mind or heart? Are they yours? To what counterparts do they belong? And yet each of you, in your own identity, has the right to do precisely as you wish, and to form your own reality....
quotes.translation: 现在，仅就你们的说法而言，这些其他对应体就像你们心智中的潜在模式。回响。你们中有多少人真正思考过无意识可能是什么？或者，你们在内心或心灵中听到的声音？它们是你们的吗？它们属于哪些对应体？然而，你们每个人，在你们自己的身份中，都有权完全按照自己的意愿行事，并形成自己的实相……

I'd just read aloud portions of the 721st session when one longtime student, whom I'll call Florence, commented that there "has to be a balance between each of us and our counterparts." Speaking strongly and humorously, Seth immediately took over the discussion.

quotes.text: In such circumstances these perceptions may find their way to the ego, appearing as inspirations or intuitive thought. It is obvious that many such intuitions appear when the personality is in a dreaming state or in states of dissociation. The intuitions themselves, while seemingly mental, are action, and as such they produce changes in the physical organism, both chemical and electromagnetic.
quotes.translation: 在这种情况下，这些感知可能会找到通往自我的途径，表现为灵感或直觉思维。显然，许多这样的直觉出现在人格处于梦境状态或分离状态时。这些直觉本身，虽然看似是心智的，却是行动，因此它们会在物理有机体中产生化学和电磁方面的变化。

quotes.text: Concerning the overpopulation problem, remember that more than physical mechanisms are involved. The problem will be solved by mankind indeed; but mankind is composed of re-incarnational entities. It is quite within the probabilities that the statistics will reverse themselves and that for no reason that scientists can discover, children are born stillborn, as in a mass epidemic, and that entities simply refuse to inhabit the bodies made for them. The bodies then remaining empty to decay unused. (What a grim situation, I thought.)
quotes.translation: 关于人口过剩问题，请记住，这涉及的不只是物理机制。这个问题确实将由人类解决；但人类是由转世实体组成的。完全有可能的是，统计数据会自行逆转，并且出于科学家无法发现的原因，婴儿会像大规模流行病一样死产，而实体们只是拒绝居住在为它们制造的身体里。这些身体于是空置着，未经使用便腐烂。（多么严峻的情况，我想。）

Fox, and enclosed with Roy's Christmas card of last Christmas. It is a black and white print on very thin, almost transparent paper. Once again the test results were interesting, and somewhat different in nature. （We were not sure that Seth would have any data on Peggy Gallagher during the session, since we thought it possible she had left Washington, DC by now.

quotes.text: Until you are honest with yourself and become consciously aware of yourself, you cannot honestly relate with others; you will project upon them your own fears and prejudices. You cannot afford to help them because you have too many insecurities within yourself. Now, you form the physical reality that you know, individually and en masse. To change your world you must change your thoughts. You must become consciously aware of what you tell yourself is true every moment of the day, for that is the reality that you project outward.
quotes.translation: 除非你对自己诚实，并有意识地觉察自己，否则你无法真诚地与他人相处；你会将自己的恐惧与偏见投射到他们身上。你无法真正帮助他们，因为你内心有太多不安全感。现在，你个人与集体共同构建了你所认知的物质实相。要改变你的世界，你必须改变你的思想。你必须时刻有意识地觉察自己告诉自己为真的一切，因为那正是你向外投射的实相。

quotes.text: The thoughts would not be perceived if they were present, you see. If a certain intensity is reached here however, a peak of intensity, then you could perceive the spacious present as it exists within your native system. You could, from this peak, theoretically look into the other system, but you would not understand what you perceived. You would not have the proper root assumptions, you see.
quotes.translation: 你看，如果思想存在，它们本应被感知。然而，若在此处达到某种强度峰值，你便能感知到存在于你原生系统中的"广阔当下"。从这一峰值出发，理论上你可以窥见其他系统，但无法理解所感知之物——因为你缺乏相应的根本假设，你明白吗？

quotes.text: You live in a physical community, but you live first of all in a community of thoughts and feelings. These trigger your physical actions. They directly affect the behavior of your body. The experience of the animals is different, yet in their own ways animals have both individual intent and purpose. Their feelings are certainly as pertinent as yours. They dream, and in their way they reason.
quotes.translation: 你生活在物质社群中，但首先你活在思想与情感的社群中。这些思想情感触发你的物质行动，直接影响你身体的行为。动物的体验虽不同，但它们以各自的方式同样拥有个体意图与目的。它们的情感与你同样真切。它们会做梦，并以自己的方式推理。

quotes.text: You operate quite nicely politically, living in villages, townships, countries, states, and so forth, each with certain customs and local ordinances. These in no way affect the land itself. They are designations for practical purposes, and they imply organization of intent or affiliation at one level. They are political patterns, invisible but highly effective. There are, however, far more vigorous invisible mental patterns, into which the thoughts and feelings of mankind are organized -- or, naturally, organize themselves.
quotes.translation: 你在政治层面运作良好——生活在村庄、乡镇、国家、州郡等区域，各有特定习俗与地方法规。这些规定丝毫不影响土地本身，只是实用层面的划分，暗示着某一层面的意图归属或组织形态。它们是无形却高效的政治模式。然而，还存在更活跃的无形心智模式，人类的思想与情感在其中被组织——或者说，自然地进行自我组织。

quotes.text: Ready answers for he or his son would only betray them. They would use them as excuses. Now give me, a moment... I will tell him that many of his worries concerning the boy are projections of his own fears, and will not materialize. The boy is reacting telepathically for one thing to the father's worries. The best thing the father can do for now is to be simply kind to the boy, but relieve the child of his heavy-handed thoughts mentally. Leave the boy alone. He is picking up the father's negative attitudes telepathically.
quotes.translation: 为他或他儿子准备现成答案只会害了他们。他们会把这些当作借口。且容我稍作停顿……我要告诉他，他对那男孩的诸多担忧实则是自身恐惧的投射，不会成真。男孩正以心电感应方式回应父亲的忧虑。父亲当下能做的，就是单纯善待男孩，同时在心智上解除对孩子的沉重思想。让那孩子独处吧。他正以心电感应接收着父亲的负面态度。

quotes.text: The unharmonious sounds have become a part of that portion of the body as a result of the inner sound of your own thought-beliefs. That is why it is vital that you not reinforce these inner sounds through repeating the same negative suggestions to yourself. Verbal suggestions are translated into inner sound. This passes through your body in somewhat the same way that some kinds of light do.
quotes.translation: 不和谐的声音已成为身体某部分的一部分，这是你自身思想信念的内在声音所致。因此，切勿通过对自己重复相同的负面暗示来强化这些内在声音，这一点至关重要。言语暗示会被转化为内在声音，并以类似某些光线穿透身体的方式，流经你的全身。

quotes.text: Your ideas themselves follow certain laws of creativity. They have their own rhythms. The associative processes of your mind, working through the brain, have great connection with the minute behavior of your cells. As you learn to use your thoughts, or even as they naturally change, resulting alterations take place within the cells. There is an orderly progression, an intimate relationship.
quotes.translation: 你的思想本身遵循特定的创造力法则，拥有自己的节奏。你的心智通过大脑运作的联想过程，与细胞的细微行为有着密切关联。当你学会运用思想，甚至当它们自然变化时，细胞内部也会发生相应的改变。这其中存在着有序的进程与亲密的关系。

quotes.text: This would imply short-term memory you see, but this is not the case, for it is intensity and not duration that makes the difference. Once the symptoms become physical however, then they follow physical patterns, and it takes the physical system some time to heal usually, (underlined). In terms of intensity alone, both the foot and the hip symptoms were highly charged, representing of course degrees of immobility and withdrawal--learned, you see, from the mother: a memory reaction adopted without conscious thought.
quotes.translation: 你看，这似乎暗示着短期记忆，但事实并非如此，因为起决定作用的是强度而非持续时间。然而，一旦症状变得身体化，它们就会遵循身体模式，身体系统通常需要一段时间才能痊愈（下划线强调）。仅就强度而言，脚部和髋部的症状都带有高度电荷，当然代表着不同程度的行动不便和退缩——这是从母亲那里学来的，你看：一种未经有意识思考而采纳的记忆反应。

You can correct a physical condition for example, in the manner just given. quotes.translation: 一片黑暗且大多阴郁的内在风景应引起你的警觉，以便你立即开始改变它。这些成就没有一项是我的读者无法企及的，尽管任何人可能会发现某一特定壮举比另一项更难。你还必须意识到，我是在用实际的术语说话。例如，你可以用刚才给出的方式纠正一种身体状况。然而，如果这样做，通过审视思想的内心风景，你会发现最初导致身体疾病的根源就在这里。（停顿。）

quotes.text: Now. This will be a relatively brief session and we shall end shortly. I will here repeat my earlier suggestion that our friend read my earlier material.
